title: Registration

extendedProps.summary: Skip the crowds and queues – register early, grab your welcome bag, and start exploring the city!
extendedProps.description: Kick off FAB25 the smart way with early registration! Just show the QR code from your purchased ticket, and you’ll receive your badge and welcome bag in no time. It’s a big help for the organising team and saves you from the hustle later. Once you’re all set, take the chance to explore the city centre – it’s just around the corner and full of places to eat and drink.

title: Welcome to FAB25 party!

speakers.title: Noisy Pots
speakers.description: Noisy Pots mix buckets, pots, and DIY percussion with synths, electronics, and vocals to create a sound all their own. They've played major Czech festivals like Colours of Ostrava and Rock for People, and performed abroad at Reeperbahn (DE), Trans Musicales (FR), Seoul Music Week (KOR), and more. Influenced by acts like Moderat and Mura Masa, as well as jazz and classical music, the duo has released multiple singles since 2020, with radio play on Expres FM, Radio 1, and others. Collaborations include tracks with singer and rapper Tanita Yankova.

speakers.title: Tanita Yankova
speakers.description: Tanita Yankova is a Bulgarian-born musician, vocalist, and songwriter known for her dynamic contributions to the Czech music scene. She was an original member of the all-female band Vesna, which blends Slavic folk influences with contemporary pop and alternative sounds. Yankova played a pivotal role in Vesna's formation in 2016, contributing vocals, flute, and piano. She was part of the band until 2018 and rejoined in 2023 for their Eurovision entry, "My Sister's Crown," where she co-wrote the song that secured a top-ten finish for the Czech Republic.

extendedProps.summary: Get into the FAB25 spirit with a vibrant evening in an unforgettable theatre setting!
extendedProps.description: Join us for the official welcome party of FAB25 – an unforgettable evening set in a stunning theatre foyer and its lively outdoor piazzetta. Expect great music, refreshing drinks, and amazing company, all wrapped in the perfect atmosphere to launch a week full of digital fabrication, creativity and collaboration. This is where the FAB25 energy begins!

title: FAB25 Grand Opening

speakers.title: Scotty Allen
speakers.bio: YouTube Creator and Hacker - Strange Parts
speakers.description: Scotty is a hacker turned storyteller who runs the YouTube channels Strange Parts and Electric Ranch. He makes videos that merge hacking, technology, and adventure, from building and modifying iPhones in the markets of Shenzhen, China, to visiting factories all over the world diving into the details of how stuff is actually made. He previously was a software engineer at Google, Ooyala, and Shopkick, before starting his own startup, AppMonsta, which he sold in 2020. Scotty was a member of the Noisebridge hackerspace in San Francisco for a number of years.  He’s spent extensive time living and traveling throughout Asia, and now lives on a 100+ acre ranch in the mountains outside Denver with his dog Ambrose and his partner Katie.

speakers.title: Neil Gershenfeld
speakers.bio: Director of MIT's Center for Bits and Atoms
speakers.description:
Prof. Neil Gershenfeld is the Director of MIT's Center for Bits and Atoms, where his unique laboratory is breaking down boundaries between the digital and physical worlds, from pioneering quantum computing to digital fabrication to the Internet of Things. Technology from his lab has been seen and used in settings including New York's Museum of Modern Art and rural Indian villages, the White House and the World Economic Forum, inner-city community centers and automobile safety systems, Las Vegas shows and Sami herds.

He is the author of numerous technical publications, patents, and books including Designing Reality, Fab, When Things Start To Think, The Nature of Mathematical Modeling, and The Physics of Information Technology, and has been featured in media such as The New York Times, The Economist, NPR, CNN, and PBS. He is a Fellow of the American Physical Society, has been named one of Scientific American's 50 leaders in science and technology, as one of 40 Modern-Day Leonardos by the Museum of Science and Industry, one of Popular Mechanic's 25 Makers, has been selected as a CNN/Time/Fortune Principal Voice, and by Prospect/Foreign Policy as one of the top 100 public intellectuals.

He's been called the intellectual father of the maker movement, founding a growing global network of over one thousand fab labs that provide widespread access to prototype tools for personal fabrication, directing the Fab Academy for distributed research and education in the principles and practices of digital fabrication, and chairing the Fab Foundation. Dr. Gershenfeld has a BA in Physics with High Honors from Swarthmore College, a Ph.D. in Applied Physics from Cornell University, honorary doctorates from Swarthmore College, Strathclyde University and the University of Antwerp, was a Junior Fellow of the Harvard University Society of Fellows, and a member of the research staff at Bell Labs.

speakers.title: Sherry Lassiter
speakers.description:
Sherry Lassiter is one of the architects of the MIT global initiative for field on-site technology development, the Fab Lab program. A Fab Lab, or as users like to call it, fabulous laboratory, is a rapid prototyping platform for technical education, innovation, and personal expression.

The Fab Lab network includes over 2000 digital fabrication facilities in 126 countries. Lassiter is Director of the Fab Foundation, a non-profit organization committed to building technical capacity in a locality, improving individuals’ abilities to develop themselves and their communities and bringing access to tools and knowledge that cultivate and support innovating practices. After a two-decade career in science journalism as a producer, writer, and director for television series such as Scientific American Frontiers, Discover the World of Science, and The Science Times, she became a protagonist in science and technology, becoming part of the story, rather than just telling the story.

As Program Manager for the NSF-funded Center for Bits & Atoms at MIT, she has seen and enabled the personal fabrication movement as it has grown and evolved. Today she serves as Director of the global Fab Lab Program at MIT as well as leading The Fab Foundation, the non-profit spinoff from MIT. Lassiter is currently engaged in the deployment and growth of Fab Labs around the world, enabling grassroots technology development by, for and of the community.

speakers.title: Ondřej Kašpárek
speakers.description:
Ondřej Kašpárek is the Head of PrusaLab, one of the most advanced makerspaces in Europe, where creativity, technology, and community-driven innovation come together. In his role, he oversees the development and operations of a workshop that serves as a hub for experimentation and digital fabrication for makers, students, startups, and established companies alike.

Ondřej's work focuses on fostering innovation through shared production spaces where technologies like 3D printing, CNC milling, and laser cutting empower groundbreaking projects. His expertise spans design, prototyping, and implementing cutting-edge solutions that connect digital fabrication with practical applications.

His mission is to build bridges between technology, education, and industry while strengthening a maker community passionate about sustainable and open innovation. Under his leadership, PrusaLab has inspired countless projects, embodying the spirit of creativity and collaboration.

extendedProps.summary: The official kickoff of FAB25 – with a spectacular welcome show, inauguration and inspiring keynotes!
extendedProps.description: The FAB25 Grand Opening features a spectacular, tailor-made 15-minute welcome show to ignite your imagination and set the tone for the week. It’s followed by the official inauguration with event hosts, and powerful keynote sessions from renowned speakers sharing their experience, know-how and visions. This is the moment we’ve all been waiting for all year – don’t miss your seat when it all begins!

title: Welcome from the Organizers!

speakers.title: Tomáš Mejzlík
speakers.bio: Managing director FabLab Brno and VIDA!
speakers.description:
Tomáš is a leader in digital fabrication, STEM education, and innovation. In 2016, he began his career at JIC in Brno, where he led the setup of the first FabLab in Czechia. Under his management, FabLab Brno launched as a 24/7 makerspace accessible to the public, businesses, and students. He spearheaded successful projects like FabLab University, a mobile lab serving thousands of students annually, and managed international collaborations, including the H2020 FabLabia. He was also a key initiator in bringing FAB25 to Czechia.

In 2025, he also became the director of VIDA! science center, with a vision to create a versatile STEM education hub that integrates making and technology popularization within its 6,000+ m² space. By 2026, VIDA! is expected to merge with FabLab Brno, combining their strengths to deliver more FUN, EDU and MAKE pleasures to hunderds of thousands people annualy.

speakers.title: Josef Průša
speakers.bio: Founder and CEO of Prusa Research
speakers.description:
Josef Průša is the Founder and CEO of Prusa Research, a company that has become synonymous with cutting-edge 3D printing technology. His company produces the iconic Prusa i3 printers, celebrated for their quality, reliability, and accessibility. Průša is a key figure in the world of 3D printing and a pivotal advocate for bringing digital manufacturing tools to individuals and communities globally.

Technology from Prusa Research is found everywhere—from home workshops to educational institutions, research laboratories, and commercial manufacturing facilities. Josef Průša champions the power of open-source technologies and knowledge sharing, highlighting his commitment to innovation and his dedication to the global maker community.

His work has earned numerous prestigious accolades, including being featured in Forbes 30 Under 30. His printers have received countless awards in the tech industry, and 3D Printing Industry magazine has recognized him as one of the most influential figures in 3D printing.

In addition to leading Prusa Research, Josef actively contributes to the development of open-source software and hardware, organizes workshops, and promotes education in digital manufacturing. His vision bridges technology with sustainability, and his innovations continue to shape and inspire the global maker movement.

extendedProps.summary: Come in time – you don’t want to miss this experience. One time only – no repeats, no reruns!

title: Build my childhood with STEAM: My dreaming Amusement park

speakers.title: Otávio Rodrigues
speakers.description:
Brazilian, technology enthusiast, naturally curious, with a collection of awards including the Make to Move Communities competition (2021), "Hackatagro Desafio Banrisul 2022", the 15th edition of Empreenda Senac São Paulo, and the 5th Senac Robotics tournament, both in 2022.
With training in Computer Science, Systems Analysis and Development, and Computer Engineering, he spends his free time seeking to learn new things and developing Maker projects, as a collaborator of Clube da Mecatrônica Jovem.
He works as a educator at FabLab Livre SP, promoting technology, DIY culture, and the Maker world

extendedProps.description: Have you ever wondered about how a roller coaster, Pirate Ship, or Mad Tea Party works? It seems a kind of complicated or hard, but it's just as simple as turning on an LED and you can build one with your own hands. In this hands-on workshop, you'll be able to create your dream amusement park attraction while you have fun and learn about the concepts of STEAM.

title: Bioplastic and beyond

speakers.title: Marcix Basso
speakers.description: Marcix Basso is an architect, artist, and maker educator, with a focus on innovation and sustainability. Since 2018, she has been part of the technical team at the FAB LAB Livre SP network, where she has been dedicated to researching and developing sustainable technological solutions. Her work includes in-depth studies on polymers and biomaterials, aiming to integrate creativity, technology, and environmental awareness into her projects.

extendedProps.description: During this interactive workshop on bioplastics, participants will be guided through an engaging presentation that explores the fundamentals of bioplastics, including their raw material sources and sustainable life cycle, and ways to make some materials

title: Lunch

extendedProps.summary: Pick-up, refuel, refresh – it’s lunch time at FAB25!
extendedProps.description: Time to recharge! Head to the lunch pick-up points and grab your meal box based on your selected preference. Whether you’re in the mood for something fresh or hearty, we’ve got you covered. Don’t forget to top it off with a coffee, a free water refill, or a chilled drink to beat the summer heat. Grab some energy and get ready for the rest of your FAB25 day!

title: Fabricademy Working group

speakers.title: Fabricademy Textile and Technology Academy
speakers.description: Our mission is to (re)shape and (re)define the implications and applications of technology in the textile and clothing industry, from the fashion sector to the upcoming wearable market. The program offers a broad overview of the state of the art of the current industry and builds on “learning by doing” methodologies, tackling themes like personal fabrication, distributed manufacturing, industry 4.0, wearable technology, biofabrication, assistive technologies and sustainability.

extendedProps.description:
Do you have Interest in developing expertise around textile technologies
& sustainability and expand your lab with textiles, materials and bio labs?
Join the fabricademy working group to learn more about the second most active educational program of the Academy that contributes with innovative skills to the Fab Lab Network. Learn more about the 8 year trajectory and the future vision.

title: Ingenious Minds: Empowering young engineers on a shoestring budget

speakers.title: Deborah Ivie
speakers.description: Deb is the Utah STEM Lead for 4-H, the largest youth development organisation in the USA. She has a strong passion for making STEM interesting and accessible for all youth. Her approach involves meeting youth & adults wherever they are on their STEM journey. Often this involves helping people find the STEM connection in everyday activities. She has created innovative curriculum such as the Physics of Music, Kitchen Science, & Quilting Geometry.  Her programmes have been used & adapted across the US. She is excited to collaborate with makers around the world to empower young people.

extendedProps.description: Make way for a new generation of innovators! Often the best ideas start with simplicity. We will look at ways to adapt making for young hands and minds by building on their interests and natural curiosity. Join frontline STEM educators as we explore four different maker challenges that teach kid-friendly engineering skills through creativity and imagination. Come away with a collaboratively-designed game to share with the ingenious minds in your life, and a game plan for engaging young makers.

title: Laser Acrobatics

speakers.title: Eva Korae
speakers.description: Eva has been teaching in tertiary education since 2003 and in 2022 she was elected Assistant Professor at Cyprus University of Technology. She is a hands on maker who designs and exhibits contemporary products internationally, for which she has won awards. She is a graduate of Fab Academy (Fab Lab Amsterdam 2016) and an active supporter of the maker movement. She writes, publishes and presents studies in international academic conferences with an emphasis on her perspective as a maker. She has her most fun times in the workshop, surrounded by her tools.

extendedProps.description: This workshop is based on “Laser Acrobatics, a Picture Book of Cutting, Engraving and Making” soon available digitally and as a physical book. Participants will be required to experiment on a variety of materials exploring the possibilities which arise from popular laser processes as well as the “what nots” of laser cutters. The main focus of this workshop is to create (laser) bridges between makers, designers, artists or educators, always in compliance with laser H&S protocols.

title: Co-creating value for entrepreneurial engagement in Ukraine agricultural sector

speakers.title: Henrik Barth
speakers.description: Henrik Barth is an Associate Professor in Innovation Sciences with a focus on Innovation Management, and he is currently leading several research projects addressing sustainable business model innovation, developed in close collaboration with client companies, students and researchers.

extendedProps.description: Innovating for Sustainable Ukrainian Agriculture. Addressing critical AgriTech challenges, we focus on developing innovative solutions to promote sustainable agricultural practices.

title: Smart Citizen Meets Meshtastic (A Hackathon) - DAY 1

speakers.title: Óscar González
speakers.description: Óscar González is an Industrial Engineer with expertise in data analysis, testing, and calibration through his experience in automotive and sensor development. He is the Sense Making Lead at Fab Lab Barcelona doing research and development on the Smart Citizen project. Óscar has worked as a researcher on various projects, spanning the development and implementation of open source technologies and low-cost sensors, including their application in citizen science initiatives, as tools for environmental and health assessment, and the interoperability of data across research infrastructures.

extendedProps.description: We propose a hackathon focused on air quality monitoring, using decentralised IoT solutions, potentially creating the first building block of a new Smart Citizen Kit. We want to move beyond traditional frameworks towards emancipatory technologies like Meshtastic, helping Fab Labs to build resilient, community-driven sensing networks. Building on over a decade of work, Smart Citizen is now a global network of technologists, scientists, and community leaders.

title: Fab Academy Working Group

speakers.title: Luciana Asinari
speakers.description: Fab Academy Global Coordinator & Dean of Students; Fab Lab Barcelona Education Lead

extendedProps.description: Every year, Neil and the Fab Academy team meet up during the Fab Conference to discuss the past Fab Academy Cycle and plan the coming course. It is aimed at Fab Academy Instructors but anyone interested in Fab Academy is welcomed!

title: Fabricating Humanity: Reimagine Čapek’s Robots with Collage

speakers.title: Mark Blanc
speakers.description: Mark is a graphic artist specializing in design and digital fabrication. With a unique background in communication studies and culinary arts, he seamlessly blends his diverse skills to explore the convergence of the digital and tangible worlds in his projects. Passionate about maker education, Mark actively seeks collaborations with teachers, dedicated to inspiring and empowering the next generation of creators through his practice.

extendedProps.description: Step into a creative exploration of humanity, technology, and ethics inspired by Karel Čapek’s influential play R.U.R. In this hands-on workshop, participants will create expressive, thought-provoking collages combining traditional materials and laser-cut elements. Attendees will reflect deeply on identity, automation, and our evolving relationship with technology, gaining new insights and practical experience in artistic collage and digital fabrication techniques.

title: Unlocking Access: Open-Source Lockers & AI Assistants for the Maker Community

speakers.title: Andreas Kopp
speakers.description: Andreas Kopp is a social entrepreneur and founder of the non-profit Munich Tool Library . The project provides low-cost access to tools and everyday items, empowering people to repair, build, and create — with sustainability, creativity, and community at its core.

extendedProps.description: In this workshop, we’ll explore how open-source locker systems and AI-powered assistants can bridge tool accessibility and knowledge sharing gaps in the maker community. Learn how AI helps users identify the right tools for their projects and access tutorials, safety guidelines, and troubleshooting advice. Participants will test a beta version of the AI assistant, offer feedback, and brainstorm ideas for integrating self-service lockers and AI into diverse maker spaces

title: Print outside the box

speakers.title: Alan Han
speakers.description: Alan Han is a graduate student at the MIT Center for Bits and Atoms, where he’s rethinking how we build electronics - focusing on interconnects, discrete assembly, and making hardware more accessible to everyone. Before MIT, he spent over five years in the connector industry and earned his bachelor’s in Electrical Engineering from Purdue University. Alan got his first formal introduction to the FabLab network as a part of the Fab Academy class of 2022 and is an annual attendee at the Hackaday Superconference.

extendedProps.description: Join this session to participate in a 3D printer hackathon that involves designing 3D printed spring and motion mechanisms, as well as other interesting printing tricks that will (literally) push the build envelopes of 3D printers. Share your hack at the end of the conference!

title: Mobile new technology labs key to digital inclusion

speakers.title: Maciej Naskręt
speakers.description: Expert of STREAM and PBL methodologies. To date, he has led the incubation of more than 100 individual and group projects taking care of the ideation, design, needs research, prototyping, manufacturing and testing phases. He has collaborated with entities such as: Copernicus Science Center, Sector 3.0, Maritime School, SWPS, City of Gdynia, FabLab Trójmiasto, FabLab Małopolska, FabLab Łódź, Fablab powered by Orange, PAKT Olsztyn, PAKT Wrocław, FRSI, PAH, City of Warsaw, Museum of Józef Piłsudski. Trained as an architect and interior designer. Instructor in 3D modeling, 3D printing, CNC machine

extendedProps.description: How can small towns, far from large agglomerations, become centers of innovation and development? Imagine a mobile workshop that travels to even the remotest corners, bringing with it the latest technologies and the desire to share knowledge. A studio that democratizes knowledge and skills, opening the door to digital inclusion. How will the future change when those participating receive hands-on experience with 3D printing, programming, robotics, vector graphics and other modern tools? Could mo

title: Define Your Erasmus+ Strategy

speakers.title: Alexandre Rousselet
speakers.description: Makerspaces explorer since 2015. I co-created with some Erasmus student friends, VULCA Network and together we pushed the door of more than 1000 FabLabs, Makerspaces and Hackerspaces in Europe. Now I work full time for RFFLabs (The French FabLabs Network) and I'm a volunteer supporting the development of my local makerspace "Mountain Makers" located in my village in middle France.

extendedProps.description: Erasmus+ offers many opportunities for FabLabs, but where to start or how to scale up in the framework of Erasmus+? This working group helps participants understand the basics of Erasmus+, identify relevant opportunities, and develop a personalized strategy. By the end of the session, attendees will have a clear roadmap of Erasmus+ calls to align with their FabLab’s goals.

title: Digital Collaborative: How Connected Maker Communities Are Amplifying Their Impact

speakers.title: Abhijeet Khandagale
speakers.description:
I'm Abheejit, a social entrepreneur from Nagpur, India, with extensive experience across the tech, non-profit, and policy sectors. I've been recognized as a Top Innovator by the World Economic Forum for my solution, ekatra. As the founder of Vruksh Ecosystems, a non-profit focused on grassroots entrepreneurship education.

As a community enabler, I lead the NagpurStartups community and have built Misfits, a vibrant network of 500+ early-stage social entrepreneurs across South Asia. I'm dedicated to helping scale startups in their Zero to One journey

extendedProps.description: This talk introduces the Catalyst Now Digital Collaborative that will support High Impact Ecosystems worldwide. As Director of Digital Solutions at Catalyst Now, I'll share insights on how digital tools for collaboration, impact measurement, and knowledge exchange can help makers tackle systemic challenges collectively. Discover how maker spaces can join the Catalyst Now global movement to amplify their impact.

title: How Repair Cafés can save the world

speakers.title: Rafael Calado
speakers.description: Co-founder of Repair Café Lisboa (2016) Biolab Lisboa 2022 and former FabLab Lisboa Coordinator, Architec

speakers.title: Andreas Kopp
speakers.description: Andreas Kopp is a social entrepreneur and founder of the non-profit Munich Tool Library . The project provides low-cost access to tools and everyday items, empowering people to repair, build, and create — with sustainability, creativity, and community at its core.

extendedProps.description: We will engage with like-minded people and makers, to facilitate the creation and viability of Repair Cafés within maker spaces and beyond

title: Shared Tools, Shared Power – How Tool Libraries & Libraries of Things Can Change Cities

speakers.title: Andreas Kopp
speakers.description: Andreas Kopp is a social entrepreneur and founder of the non-profit Munich Tool Library . The project provides low-cost access to tools and everyday items, empowering people to repair, build, and create — with sustainability, creativity, and community at its core.

extendedProps.description: Tool and Thing Libraries across Europe, the UK, Canada, Australia and the US empower communities by providing shared access to tools and everyday items. This talk explores how they promote sustainability, repair culture, and community connection. Participants will gain insights into successful models and be invited to join efforts to create a European network for knowledge exchange, shared infrastructure, and greater impact.

title: Libre Space Foundation, ten years building satellites in a hackerspace

speakers.title: Alfredos Damkalis
speakers.description:
Alfredos (fredy) Damkalis (LSF) as an Open Source enthusiast he has participated and contributed in several free and open source projects and communities. Among others he is a member of Athens (Greece) hackerspace (hackerspace.gr), core contributor in Libre Space Foundation and radio amateur.

The last decade, he has been devoted to make Space accessible for everyone through his contributions in development of SatNOGS project and other LSF projects. Additionally he is operator of the SatNOGS Network of ground stations, supporting satellite teams and missions.

extendedProps.description: For a decade, the Libre Space Foundation has pioneered open-source satellite development, evolving from a grassroots hackerspace project to a sustainable organization. This presentation explores the journey, sharing invaluable lessons learned, the tools and practices refined over time, and the transformative changes that enabled the transition from building the first open-source satellite to establishing a thriving ecosystem supporting open technologies in space.

title: Building a ground station for SatNOGS

speakers.title: George Pliakis
speakers.description: George Pliakis is a new contributor to the Libre Space Foundation, where he plays a key role in project coordination and the development of new proejct initiatives.

extendedProps.description: This hands-on workshop introduces the fablab community to building SatNOGS stations. Participants will learn about the network’s philosophy, origins, operation, and future. Working in small teams, they will follow the assembly process and connect stations to the network. Equipment will be provided for teams to build antennas under coordinator guidance, exploring various SatNOGS configurations.

title: The AI Workshop: How to Add AI to Almost Everything

speakers.title: Mathew Yu
speakers.description: Matthew is a multidisciplinary professional with a ​​Bachelor’s in Electrical and Electronic Engineering (UK)​​ and a ​​2024 Digital Fabrication Certificate​​ from Fab Academy. Currently an ​​AI Developer​​ and ​​Project Manager​​ for Seeed Studio’s Contributor Program, blending expertise in ​​AI Research (NLP)​​, ​​Digital Fabrication​​, ​​Maker Education​​, and ​​Hardware Innovation​​. Notable contributions include collaborating with Glasgow Health to enhance depression screening methods and delivering a keynote on sustainability at a ​​UNU Macao SDG event​​.

extendedProps.description: Presented by Seeed Studio, this hands-on experience is crafted for innovators working with smart hardware, AIoT, and edge computing. We will simplify AI integration into your projects with remarkable ease—truly a game-changer for cross-border innovators seeking to enhance their creations with artificial intelligence. Participants should bring a project they’re actively developing, ready to take their ideas to the next level.

title: Decentralizing Innovation: Georgia’s Maker Journey with Regional FabLabs and Tech Parks

speakers.title: Gigi Kezherashvili
speakers.description: Gigi Kezherashvili is the Head of Tech Parks Department at Georgia’s Innovation and Technology Agency (GITA). Since 2020, he has led the development of FabLabs and innovation spaces across Georgia, with a focus on regional and rural access to technology and entrepreneurship. He actively promotes maker culture, digital skills, and startup ecosystems through public programs and international collaboration.

extendedProps.description: The session explores how GITA initiated the country’s first FabLabs and Tech Parks in 2014-2016 and strategically expanded them into different regions and rural areas. Participants will learn about effective public-led innovation models, the importance of decentralizing tech education, and lessons from building a grassroots innovation ecosystem.

title: Conquer the castle party!

speakers.title: Cimbálová muzika Jožky Imricha
speakers.description: Cimbálová muzika Jožky Imricha is a Brno-based ensemble of five professional musicians from across Moravia, led by violinist Josef Imrich. While deeply rooted in Moravian folk traditions, their repertoire extends to jazz, musical theatre, and film scores, often featuring original arrangements by cimbalom player Jiří Gužík. This blend of traditional and contemporary styles allows them to perform at a wide range of events, from formal occasions to informal gatherings. Their performances showcase the rich musical heritage of Moravia, characterized by the distinctive sounds of the cimbalom and string instruments. This fusion of Eastern and Western European musical elements has influenced Czech classical composers and continues to inspire modern interpretations.

speakers.title: Malalata
speakers.description: Malalata is a Slovak DJ duo renowned for their electrifying fusion of Balkan beats, gypsy music, cumbia, and reggae. Comprising DJs Roman Lalík and Jakub Kmošena, the duo has been igniting dance floors for over 17 years with their high-energy performances and genre-blending sets. Their dynamic shows, often branded as "Disko Balkan Bashavel," have resonated with audiences across Europe and beyond, including performances in cities like Berlin, Vienna, Antwerp, Vilnius, Valletta, as well as in Japan and Mexico. Malalata has graced the stages of prominent festivals such as Pohoda, United Islands, and Grape, sharing lineups with artists like Parov Stelar Band, Goran Bregović, Mahala Rai Banda, Fanfare Ciocărlia, and N.O.H.A. Their unique blend of traditional and contemporary sounds creates an infectious atmosphere, making them a standout act in the world of global music fusion.

extendedProps.summary: Experience Czech culture, cuisine, music and unbeatable views – all from Brno’s iconic castle!
extendedProps.description:
Get ready for a night to remember at one of Brno’s coolest spots – the legendary Brno Castle! The Conquer the Castle Party is your chance to dive into Czech culture and culinary delights, all while soaking in a breathtaking panoramic view of the city.

Live bands will set the vibe, while delicious food, famous Czech beer and other local drinks will keep you fuelled. The courtyard will be buzzing with energy – the perfect setting to reconnect with old friends or make new ones from across the global maker community.

P.S. What’s with the conquering? The castle (full name "Spilberk Castle") sits on a hill, so yes… you might break a little sweat getting up there – but trust us, it’s worth every step.

title: From bits to atoms: Using AI to make physical art

speakers.title: Tex Kang
speakers.description:
Current manager of University of Pennsylvania's library and makerspace 
Former manager of Fab Lab Seoul and Bio Lab Seoul 
Organizer of the Philadelphia Maker Faire 
Burning Man designer and fire performer 
Has two dogs named 'light' and 'speed' in Tolkien elvish

extendedProps.description: Attendees will learn about different AI generators, methodology and ethics, prompt engineering, and even how to create their own database and offline versions. Then we will utilize said AI to produce graphics that can be heat pressed on to canvas bags in minutes for participants to use during the event. This program aims to teach how to use AI effectively with hands on experience to create their own one-of-a-kind design for a reusable tote bag that can be replicated on their own.

title: Fabship: Rural Off-grid fab lab

speakers.title: Anastasia Pistofidou
speakers.description:
Anastasia Pistofidou is a creative technologist and educator who pioneered Textile and Material Labs in the Fab Lab Network. She co-founded the FabTextiles research lab in 2013 and Fabricademy in 2016, which is a global educational program merging textiles, digital fabrication, and biology. From 2010 to 2023, she was part of IAAC Fab Lab Barcelona,
leading European projects and the Textiles and Materials Labs.
She is currently a PhD Candidate at Elisava School of Design Engineering, focusing on the impact of Fabricademy.

extendedProps.description:
Anastasia Pistofidou, Gregoire Durrens
Fabship merges Earthship architecture with Fab Lab principles for tool-enabled local manufacturing. Planned for construction in 2027, it will be the first off-grid microfactory demonstrator on a 4-hectare site that will gather visionaries in an ecovillage of12 self-sufficient homes in Southern France, Lozere

title: First Stitches: Basics of Sewing 101

extendedProps.summary: Learn how to thread a machine, sew a straight line, and make your very first textile creation.
extendedProps.description:
Curious about sewing but never touched a machine before? This is your moment. Join us for a relaxed, hands-on intro where you'll learn the basics – from threading the sewing machine to making your first real stitches.

– Learn how to handle a sewing machine with confidence
– Practice straight stitches, curves, and corners
– Make a small, useful item you can take home

No experience needed, just bring your curiosity. Our instructors will help you stitch with confidence – whether you want to mend your gear or start your next maker project in textiles. This is where it all begins.

title: Lathe Your Wooden Pen

extendedProps.summary: Turn raw wood into your own custom pen – smooth, personal, and made by you.
extendedProps.description:
Step up to the lathe and shape your own custom wooden pen. In this hands-on workshop, you’ll turn a block of wood into a finished writing tool – unique, personal, and satisfying to make.

– Learn the basics of woodturning on a lathe
– Shape and smooth your wooden pen body
– Assemble it with a pre-made pen mechanism
– Optionally add a custom engraving with a laser cutter

No experience needed – just bring your curiosity and a steady hand. Our instructors will guide you through every step, and in just 20 minutes, you’ll walk away with a pen that’s truly yours.

title: Reuse Academy: how to reuse (almost) anything

speakers.title: Felipe Schmidt Fonseca
speakers.description: I am Dr. Felipe Schmidt Fonseca, an experienced Berlin-based Brazilian advocate for social-environmental innovation and free/open-source technologies turned researcher. I am the founder of Reuse City studio and a co-creator of semente, a toolkit for community projects; as well as the co-founder and lead articulator of the Tropixel network; and an active member of organisations such as GIG and Circular Berlin.

extendedProps.description: This talk unveils Reuse Academy: a project developed from my "Generous Cities" research, investigating how we can transition from a linear "take-make-dispose" model to circular, community-based systems. We'll delve into the potential of repair, upcycling, and material re-circulation to create more just and sustainable urban environments, with a focus on practical strategies and collaborative action.

title: The Makers You’re Not Seeing (But Should Be): Innovation Where It’s Needed Most

speakers.title: John Rexford Nzira
speakers.description: John Rexford Nzira is the Executive Director of Twende Social Innovation Center in Tanzania, where he leads efforts to support youth and communities in designing and building technologies that solve real-world challenges. A 2022 African Visionary Fellow, he guides programs that blend indigenous knowledge, digital fabrication, and design thinking—championing a future where innovation is inclusive, locally driven, and shaped by those often left out of global conversations.

extendedProps.description: Innovation is failing those who need it most. In Tanzania, young people and communities are using local tools, design thinking, and digital fabrication to solve real-world problems. This talk reveals the power of grassroots making—and why global innovation must shift from spotlight to margins.

title: Building a community of internal and professional FabLabs Is France a pioneer? A decade of evolution

speakers.title: Aline Becq
speakers.description:
Reoriented my career thanks to FabAcademy in 2017
Took part in Fab13 & Fab14 (deeply happy to come back)
8 years experience in operating academia FabLabs in engineering schools
3 different Labs managed
Tried to launch FabAcademy in 2020 and 2024
VP of the Fab&co association for 4 years now
Actively engaged in promoting diversity in tech
Took part in the FabAllIn 2024 cohort (thrilled to receive my diploma in Europe)
Research focus: How to extend to the FabLab toolbox
Member of the Institute for Future technologies @DVHE
Currently running LabCyber, a FabLab focusing on hardware cybersecurity

extendedProps.description:
FabLabs encompassed in organizations face some specific challenges such as alignement with the global strategy, identification of sponsors or acknowledgment of the expertises of its labmanagers.
Exactly 10 years ago in France 14 such labs launched the Fab&Co association uniting internal FabLabs across different sectors (manufacturing, defense, space, academia…).
Its purpose: Favouring the labs sustainability as well as its labmanagers careers
This talk will present the take aways of a decade.

title: Center Rog: A New Model for Shared Creative Infrastructure

speakers.title: Tauan Bernardo
speakers.description: Tauan Bernardo is the head of the FabLab at Center Rog, a designer, creator, and educator. He has led numerous FabLabs and is a passionate advocate for technology, innovation, digital manufacturing, and the maker movement.

extendedProps.description:
What happens when a city reclaims its industrial past to shape the creative future? Center Rog, Ljubljana’s newest public institution, stands as a bold experiment in cultural regeneration, built on the legacy of the former Rog bicycle factory.
This talk will share the lessons learned, challenges faced, and strategies used in building a participatory, city-supported FabLab ecosystem within a major urban redevelopment.

title: Accessibility to Assistive Technology: Lessons from JNWSFL’s Journey

speakers.title: Tshering Wangzom
speakers.description: Currently managing the Jigme Namgyel Wangchuck Super FabLab, where I oversee operations, conduct R&D, collaborate with external stakeholders, and design and lead training programs and workshops. With a background in Electronics and Communication and an MSc in Telecommunication and Networking, I am passionate about electronics and committed to fostering an inclusive society for all.

extendedProps.description: This talk explores how FabLabs can drive Assistive Technology (AT) innovation, using JNWSFL’s work in Bhutan as a case study. We’ll highlight local AT development, R&D efforts, and collaborations with PWDs, educators, and organizations. Attendees will learn how FabLabs can foster inclusivity through community-driven solutions and capacity-building initiatives.

title: From Textile Waste to Innovation: A Call for Circular Action in FabRegion City Triangle.

speakers.title: Adriana Cabrera
speakers.description: Adriana Cabrera is a designer and media artist working at the intersection of innovation, digital fabrication, and sustainable material ecologies. She co-leads Fabcare.network and lectures at Fabricademy in the field of soft robotics, promoting inclusive health and care through open innovation. At CSCP, she supports Fab City strategies for circular and co-creative regional development in Germany’s Fab Region Bergische Triangle.

extendedProps.description:
In Wuppertal, over 1,000 tons of used clothing are collected each year. A local non-profit sorts these textiles, but most remain unused.
Fab Region Bergisches City Triangle activates its network of FabLabs to turn this challenge into opportunity. We invite creatives, recyclers, and makers to co-create local solutions and give new life to textile waste.

title: Co-creating value for agri-entrepreneurial engagement in Ukraine

speakers.title: Henrik Barth
speakers.description: Henrik Barth is an Associate Professor in Innovation Sciences with a focus on Innovation Management, and he is currently leading several research projects addressing sustainable business model innovation, developed in close collaboration with client companies, students and researchers.

extendedProps.description: The project CRAFT, financed by the Swedish Institute, aims to strengthen the resilient agri-businesses in Ukraine, focusing on means of innovative problem-solving approaches. The design thinking methodology serves as a model to overcome identified barriers, and FabLab Halmstad, Sweden, is an innovation arena to explore and develop resilient solutions, but also as a hub for exchanging experience and knowledge between students, entrepreneurs and partners -share globally and act locally.

title: Public Outer Space

speakers.title: Miha Tursic
speakers.description:
Miha Turšič (SI/NL) is enagaged in space related art, culture and humanities for more than 20 years. He is developing cultural and research programs, founding organisations, supporting communities and making art.

Curently he works at Waag Futurelab as a Space Lab lead, coordinator of More-than-Planet initiative, and developer and researcher of art-science-technology collaborations. He is also a co-founding member of the Postgravityart group, Cultural Centre of European Space Technologies (KSEVT) and creative studio Asobi.

extendedProps.description: Outer space is renowned for scientific milestones, from Moon landings to deep-space research. Yet, national and commercial interests dominate Earth-Space environment, claiming to serve humanity while sidelining civil and public perspectives. This session aims to explore how to make outer space more public. The maker community cn play a key role by linking local knowledge, diverse skills, while shaping its own societal and cultural agency.

title: FabLab & Agriculture

speakers.title: Luc Hanneuse
speakers.description: Luc Hanneuse is Agrilab UniLaSalle manager in Beauvais, France. Starting in the FabLab ecosystem in 2010 in Brussels FabLab.iMAL. Since 2018, he manages AgriLab, an open Innovation center in Agriculture and a FabLab in a Farm related to the polytechnical campus with large agricultural facilities & agricultural experimental fields. It wants to be the bridge between Agriculture, FabLab and the open source world. Instructor in FabAcademy, part of several working group, he develops a potential Agricademy distributed training program to empower agroecology & new technology practises.

extendedProps.description:
10 minutes wide audience on FabLab & Agriculture.
Quick show of the most interesting projects and advance.
The idea is to emaluate peopel to participate in the other workshop & working group proposed by AgriLab.

title: Accessibility Keyboards For People Who Can’t Type

speakers.title: Vik Olliver
speakers.description: Vik is best known as the person who built the first replicating 3D printer, the RepRap, the ancestor of most desktop 3D printers. He also invented PLA filament. These days he works at the local Fab Lab in New Zealand which has a high proportion of special needs and elderly members of the community, as well as significant involvement with local cultural arts. He is equally at home behind a keyboard, soldering iron, screwdriver, or something entirely more energetic and dangerous.

extendedProps.description:
Discover and make a unique type of USB keyboard, used with one hand - and without looking. Developed originally for the blind and those who cannot move their arm or wrist, with tremors, brittle bones, or fragile finger joints, “Quirkey” touch-types at writing speed - and is small enough to carry to use with your phone.
The 3D-printed shell contains an RP2040 or AT32U4-based micro and 6 switches, so only 12 wires to solder. It’s a mature, documented, Open Source aid you can make at your Fab Lab.

title: From Labs to Legacy: Learning from the Fab Foundation

speakers.title: Norella Coronell
speakers.description: With expertise spanning the tech and nonprofit sectors. Norella is a seasoned event producer, as well as marketing, networking and currently the Communications Director of the Fab Foundation. Holding a Fab Academy Diploma in Digital Fabrication, a master’s degree in Project Management, and a Bachelor’s degree in Product Design. Norella brings a multidisciplinary perspective to every endeavor, combining creativity with strategic execution.

speakers.title: Rodney Williams
speakers.description: As Program Manager for the Fab Foundation, Dr. Rodney Williams oversees educational outreach projects with public, corporate, and private partners across the U.S. and select international locations. He forges strategic partnerships that enhance the impact of these initiatives. Working closely with educators, lab managers, and administrators, he ensures successful project implementation. Driven by a passion for STEAM education, Dr. Williams is dedicated to expanding access to digital fabrication and hands-on learning worldwide.

extendedProps.description: Curious about how the Fab Foundation helps power a global network of makers, educators, and changemakers? This session offers a deep dive into the organization’s mission, core values, and global initiatives

title: Research Paper Presentations - DAY 1

speakers.title: Peter Troxler
speakers.bio: Research Professor at Rotterdam University of Applied Sciences

title: Humanitarian Working Group

speakers.title: Andrew Lamb
speakers.description: Andrew Lamb has been participating in or leading the Humanitarian FabLabs working group since the first sessions at Fab11 in Boston. He is the former Innovation Lead of the humanitarian relief organisation Field Ready and now chairs the Internet of Production Alliance which supports a number of makerspace initiatives around the world. Andrew is also the chair of FabLab Winam in Kenya.

extendedProps.description: The Humanitarian Working Group reconvenes at Fab25! This group brings together all those FabLabs and makers who have taken part in disaster relief and humanitarian work over the last year, and those who are interested to learn about getting involved in humanitarian relief in future.

title: The Quest for the Data Workbench; starring GIS, Jupyter, Pandas, Python and QGIS

speakers.title: Pieter van der Hijden
speakers.description:
Pieter van der Hijden (Netherlands) studied Applied Physics, worked in R&D in gaming/simulation and then became a management consultant. He did projects on gaming, making and learning; also abroad and especially in Suriname where his wife Bidia comes from. 
In Suriname Pieter implemented the first Caribbean fablab. Now, he is a volunteer of ZapLab AT Villa Zapakara there, currently in STEAM curriculum development for outbound activities in the interior. Within the fablab network Pieter coordinates the Global Working Group "Fablabs and the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)".

extendedProps.description:
Fablabs use data to control their machines, to embed in their products, to run their organization, to understand and change their environment. Doesn't every fablab need a data workbench?
In this hands-on workshop we will learn some favorite components for a data workbench: Python, Pandas and Jupyter, we will combine data from fablabs.io with other sources, Geospatial Information Systems and especially QGIS, and finally we will get a picture of our own Data Workbench and what is still missing.
More info: https://bit.ly/fab25-sdgs

title: Open Footwear - Shoes made by you

speakers.title: Juraj Šuška
speakers.description: I’ve been designing footwear for over 25 years. Open Footwear combines my love for running, digital tools, and sustainable fashion.

extendedProps.description: Take part in our 4 hours workshop where you will learn how to make your own shoes. Using open source templates and digital tools. Making is sustainable!

title: Heart in hand

speakers.title: Emily Eickhoff
speakers.description: The daughter of an analytical-minded father and a resourceful & creative mother, Emily Eickhoff is a biomedical engineer by training. She first discovered the world of digital and personal fabrication in 2018, volunteering at a fab lab in downtown Boston as a creative outlet after work. Over the years she could be found in different cities and different maker spaces, but most recently she completed Fab Academy in Fab Lab BCN, graduating in 2024.

extendedProps.description: I would like to share my experience of using a personal project (3D printing my own heart) to pivot my career. As a direct result of that project, I am now part of one of the leading cardiac 3D modeling programs in the world. The drive behind medical modeling programs is to provide a workflow to optimize patient-specific presurgical planning. It also allows patients to interact with their anatomy directly, to gain a deeper understanding of their unique anatomy and make informed medical decisons.

title: The Factory I Want to Make is Local Accessible Urban Digital Sustainable!

speakers.title: serena cangiano
speakers.description: Serena Cangiano is Senior Researcher at the Design Institute of SUPSI University, founder and Head of FabLab and faculty at MA in Interaction Design, an international program on prototyping and fabrication of future experiences. Her work centers on multimodal experimental practices to drive transformative processes in education, research, and technology innovation. Digital literacy and co-creation crossing complex social issues are key topics of her research as exemplified by the project LAUDS Factories (lauds.eu). She is board member of Swiss Design Network and Swiss Design Association.

extendedProps.description: FabLabs across Europe are proving that sustainable urban production can thrive through innovation rooted in knowledge sharing, community involvement, and care for the environment. In this talk, I will share my personal blueprint of a dream factory in Europe, shaped by the inspiring ideas and resources from the LAUDS Factories project (lauds.eu).

title: Keynotes, panels, talks (FabLabs 25 Years & Beyond)

speakers.title: Massimo Banzi
speakers.description:
Massimo Banzi is the co-founder of Arduino – an Interaction Designer, Educator, and Technologist who has helped shape the maker movement. He has worked on innovation projects for brands like Prada, Artemide, Persol, Whirlpool, and Adidas. Massimo spent four years as Associate Professor at the Interaction Design Institute Ivrea (IDII), and has led workshops and spoken at institutions around the world. Before that, he was CTO at Seat Ventures and worked as a software architect in Milan and London.
He’s the author of Getting Started with Arduino (O’Reilly), and has written for Wired Italia and other publications. Massimo launched Italy’s first FabLab, leading to FabLab Torino, and was the first curator of Maker Faire Rome. Today, he teaches Interaction Design at SUPSI in Lugano and is a visiting professor at CIID in Copenhagen. In 2024, he founded SuperModerno – a platform sharing the history of technology and the people behind it.

speakers.title: Marc Lambaerts
speakers.description: Marc is one of the most experienced FabLab managers in the world since he founded FabLab Leuven in 2009. From in the beginning of the Fab-movement, he saw the opportunities that came with FabLabs and started one of the biggest labs in the university of KULeuven. He specialized in the economics side of the FabLabs and is an advocate for being realistic about what a FabLab can and can't do. The economics side also took him to the entrepreneurial possibilities of the lab and since then, FabLab Leuven was the start for 30+ small companies. As a side note, he has a PhD in molecular biology and started recently a BioLab.

speakers.title: Sherry Lassiter
speakers.description:
Sherry Lassiter is one of the architects of the MIT global initiative for field on-site technology development, the Fab Lab program. A Fab Lab, or as users like to call it, fabulous laboratory, is a rapid prototyping platform for technical education, innovation, and personal expression.

The Fab Lab network includes over 2000 digital fabrication facilities in 126 countries. Lassiter is Director of the Fab Foundation, a non-profit organization committed to building technical capacity in a locality, improving individuals’ abilities to develop themselves and their communities and bringing access to tools and knowledge that cultivate and support innovating practices. After a two-decade career in science journalism as a producer, writer, and director for television series such as Scientific American Frontiers, Discover the World of Science, and The Science Times, she became a protagonist in science and technology, becoming part of the story, rather than just telling the story.

As Program Manager for the NSF-funded Center for Bits & Atoms at MIT, she has seen and enabled the personal fabrication movement as it has grown and evolved. Today she serves as Director of the global Fab Lab Program at MIT as well as leading The Fab Foundation, the non-profit spinoff from MIT. Lassiter is currently engaged in the deployment and growth of Fab Labs around the world, enabling grassroots technology development by, for and of the community.

speakers.title: Marleen Stikker
speakers.description: Marleen Stikker is founder and executive director of Waag Futurelab in Amsterdam. Waag Futurelab reinforces critical reflection on technology, develops technological and social design skills, and encourages social innovation. Marleen leads the trans-disciplinary team of designers, artists and scientists, utilising Public Research and Key Enabling Methodologies to empower people to participate in the collective design of open, fair and inclusive futures. Marleen founded 'De Digitale Stad' (The Digital City) in 1993, the first virtual community introducing free public access to the Internet in Amsterdam. Marleen Stikker strongly adheres to the Maker's Bill of Rights motto: 'If You Can't Open It, You Don't Own It'.

speakers.title: Rob Postema
speakers.bio: Project Manager for Metal 3D Printer at ESA
speakers.description:
Within the Directorate of Human and Robotics Exploration of the European Space Agency, Rob is coordinating and supporting the implementation and operation of instruments in the ESA Columbus Module of the International Space Station. As part of this role he has managed the development and operations of the Metal 3D Printer, a Technology Demonstrator that has printed the first true 3D metal sample in sustained orbit.
He has a background in aerospace engineering and management, working in different areas including development of instrumentation for microgravity research on sounding rockets and ISS, as well as the development of applications and services utilising satellite data for terrestrial use.

extendedProps.summary: Take a comfy seat and dive into a day of reflection, insight and bold visions for the next 25 years of FabLabs.
extendedProps.description:
On this Fab Conference day, we will dive into the topic how FabLabs have grown over the past 25 years – and where we’re headed next. From their early days in Boston to a global network driving innovation, education and community empowerment, FabLabs have reshaped how people create and collaborate.

Get a comfy seat and connect with voices from across the FabLab world – pioneers, newcomers, educators and makers. Together, we’ll reflect on what’s been built, share best practices, and explore how we can bridge the gap between past achievements and future opportunities.

title: Featured talk

speakers.title: Sherry Lassiter
speakers.description:
Sherry Lassiter is one of the architects of the MIT global initiative for field on-site technology development, the Fab Lab program. A Fab Lab, or as users like to call it, fabulous laboratory, is a rapid prototyping platform for technical education, innovation, and personal expression.
