As Program Manager for the NSF-funded Center for Bits & Atoms at MIT, she has seen and enabled the personal fabrication movement as it has grown and evolved. Today she serves as Director of the global Fab Lab Program at MIT as well as leading The Fab Foundation, the non-profit spinoff from MIT. Lassiter is currently engaged in the deployment and growth of Fab Labs around the world, enabling grassroots technology development by, for and of the community.

title: Rethinking the FLA Curriculum: Innovations for K-12 Maker Education

speakers.title: Santi Fuentemilla
speakers.description: Santiago Fuentemilla Garriga holds a Master’s in Architecture and a postgraduate specialization in digital fabrication from Fab Academy, boasting over 15 years of international experience at studios like OPR, FHAUS, and OPERA. Since 2013, he has led the Future Learning Unit at IAAC – Fab Lab BCN, pioneering innovative educational models. He directs the Fab Academy and Fabricademy in Barcelona, executive board of the Fab Learning Academy board, and teaches in advanced design master’s programs MDEF and MDDI, shaping future digital innovators.

speakers.title: Liz Whitewolf
speakers.description: Liz Whitewolf is the founder of eduFAB, a global organization advancing equity in STEM through digital fabrication. She leads the annual International Fab Educators Summit and supports global STEM educators. A former classroom teacher, she co-founded the Fab Learning Academy and serves as a Global Instructor and executive board member. Liz earned her Ed.D. in STEM Education from the University of Pittsburgh, focusing on K–12 makerspace integration, and is a 2016 FAB Academy graduate.

speakers.title: Iván Sánchez Milara
speakers.description: I am university teacher at University of Oulu (Finland) and instructor at Fab Lab Oulu. I graduated from Fab Academy in 2017. In the last years I have developed different education programs, courses, workshops and other educational activities aiming to develop professional competence of teachers in STEAM and digital fabrication. I am currently part of the executive committee of the Fab Learning Academy.

extendedProps.description: This workshop engages educators and facilitators in reviewing the Fab Learning Academy (FLA) curriculum, assessing its four modules, and identifying areas for improvement. Participants will discuss how to connect FLA with other Academany programs and innovative teaching methods, creating a more inclusive and dynamic maker education model. The session aims to develop strategies for advancing digital learning and fabrication in K-12 education.

title: Czechia’s Biggest Makerspace Coming Soon!

speakers.title: Walbert Schmirler
speakers.description: Walbert Schmirler began his professional career while studying at the Faculty of Architecture at the Czech Technical University, from which he graduated with honours. He worked in several architectural studios and designed buildings for housing, urban amenities and public spaces. Since 2022 he has been working as a city architect in Terezín, which also led him to the Innovation Centre of the Ústí nad Labem Region, where he participated in the conception of the Transformation Centre of the Ústí nad Labem Region. In recent years, he has also been involved in designing and building the largest makerspace in Czechia.

title: Doors Opened
Venue Shift

extendedProps.summary: A short 15-min walk workshop venue (1200 m), plus time to relax!
extendedProps.description:
After lunch, it’s time to shift gears and spaces. Let's move from the main venue to the workshop venue at University, just a short and easy 15-minute walk away. Once you arrive, feel free to grab a coffee, take a short break, and get ready for the afternoon full of hands-on or theoretical individualized workshop sessions.

Need help finding the way? Check out the route here.

title: Hands-On STEM Education for Youth: Innovation & Inclusion at Ghana Fablab

speakers.title: Matthew Koomson
speakers.description: Matthew Koomson is a Workshop Coordinator and Project Manager at Ghana FabLab, specializing in digital fabrication, STEM education, and sustainable innovation. He has expertise in electronics, mechatronics, robotics, IoT, CNC, and 3D printing. Passionate about hands-on learning, he empowers youth and makers through technology, creativity, and problem-solving, driving innovation and community development.

extendedProps.description: At Ghana Fablab, we empower both K-12 students and adults through hands-on STEM and digital fabrication education. Our programs blend robotics, IoT, 3D printing, and sustainability to solve real-world challenges. This session will showcase our inclusive, project-based approach, engaging youth and adults alike with practical skills in coding, automation, and renewable energy solutions. Attendees will learn effective STEM teaching strategies, maker skills, and ways to foster innovation in diverse

title: Spin & Cut: CNC Essentials

extendedProps.summary: Get hands-on with CNC basics and contribute to a one-of-a-kind maker art piece.
extendedProps.description:
Learn how to prepare your design for cutting, tweak the key parameters, and transfer your file to the Mekanika CNC machine – all in a quick, beginner-friendly session.

No prior CNC experience? No problem. Our instructors from Mekanika and the FAB25 team will walk you through every step:
– How to set up your cutting data in minutes
– What parameters really matter (and why)
– How to safely and easily run your file on the machine

Once your custom piece is milled, you’ll add it to a growing collaborative artwork – a living, evolving piece made by the FAB25 community.

Fast, friendly, and hands-on – this is the perfect way to get started with CNC and leave your personal mark on the event.

title: The Economics of Making (+Q&A)

speakers.title: Marc Lambaerts
speakers.description: Marc is one of the most experienced FabLab managers in the world since he founded FabLab Leuven in 2009. From in the beginning of the Fab-movement, he saw the opportunities that came with FabLabs and started one of the biggest labs in the university of KULeuven. He specialized in the economics side of the FabLabs and is an advocate for being realistic about what a FabLab can and can't do. The economics side also took him to the entrepreneurial possibilities of the lab and since then, FabLab Leuven was the start for 30+ small companies. As a side note, he has a PhD in molecular biology and started recently a BioLab.

title: Maker culture is the basis for strategic autonomy

speakers.title: Marleen Stikker
speakers.description: Marleen Stikker is founder and executive director of Waag Futurelab in Amsterdam. Waag Futurelab reinforces critical reflection on technology, develops technological and social design skills, and encourages social innovation. Marleen leads the trans-disciplinary team of designers, artists and scientists, utilising Public Research and Key Enabling Methodologies to empower people to participate in the collective design of open, fair and inclusive futures. Marleen founded 'De Digitale Stad' (The Digital City) in 1993, the first virtual community introducing free public access to the Internet in Amsterdam. Marleen Stikker strongly adheres to the Maker's Bill of Rights motto: 'If You Can't Open It, You Don't Own It'.

title: Printing Metal in Space

speakers.title: Rob Postema
speakers.bio: Project Manager for Metal 3D Printer at ESA
speakers.description:
Within the Directorate of Human and Robotics Exploration of the European Space Agency, Rob is coordinating and supporting the implementation and operation of instruments in the ESA Columbus Module of the International Space Station. As part of this role he has managed the development and operations of the Metal 3D Printer, a Technology Demonstrator that has printed the first true 3D metal sample in sustained orbit.
He has a background in aerospace engineering and management, working in different areas including development of instrumentation for microgravity research on sounding rockets and ISS, as well as the development of applications and services utilising satellite data for terrestrial use.

extendedProps.summary: In Orbit Demonstration of 3D Metal Additive Manufacturing

title: Featured talk
Day Start + Fab Announcements

extendedProps.summary: Let's start the day together with a short briefing and news from the FabLab network!

title: Keynote (+Q&A)

speakers.title: Massimo Banzi
speakers.description:
Massimo Banzi is the co-founder of Arduino – an Interaction Designer, Educator, and Technologist who has helped shape the maker movement. He has worked on innovation projects for brands like Prada, Artemide, Persol, Whirlpool, and Adidas. Massimo spent four years as Associate Professor at the Interaction Design Institute Ivrea (IDII), and has led workshops and spoken at institutions around the world. Before that, he was CTO at Seat Ventures and worked as a software architect in Milan and London.
He’s the author of Getting Started with Arduino (O’Reilly), and has written for Wired Italia and other publications. Massimo launched Italy’s first FabLab, leading to FabLab Torino, and was the first curator of Maker Faire Rome. Today, he teaches Interaction Design at SUPSI in Lugano and is a visiting professor at CIID in Copenhagen. In 2024, he founded SuperModerno – a platform sharing the history of technology and the people behind it.

title: Bio Break
FAB25 Maker Coin: Solder It. Blink It. Wear It.

extendedProps.summary: Come and finish your FAB25 Maker Coin by soldering everything together.
extendedProps.description:
Grab a soldering iron, the PCB from your welcome pack, and components from the table – and let's bring your FAB25 Maker Coin to life in just about 20 minutes!

  • By choosing the resistor values, you get to control how fast your badge blinks. It’s not just about soldering – it’s about making it your own.
  • Never soldered before? No worries. Our friendly instructors are here to guide you step by step. Whether you're a first-timer or a seasoned maker, this is a fun and hands-on way to kick off your FAB25 experience.
  • Wear your coin proudly all week – especially during networking sessions. Attach it to your plastic pouch or use the included magnet for a clean, stylish look.

title: Lunch

extendedProps.summary: Pick-up, refuel, refresh – it’s lunch time at FAB25!
extendedProps.description: Time to recharge! Head to the lunch pick-up points and grab your meal box based on your selected preference. Whether you’re in the mood for something fresh or hearty, we’ve got you covered. Don’t forget to top it off with a coffee, a free water refill, or a chilled drink to beat the summer heat. Grab some energy and get ready for the rest of your FAB25 day!

title: Explore Brno!

extendedProps.summary: An open evening to explore Brno’s local gems on your own – or simply relax and recharge your way.
extendedProps.description:
This evening is all yours – it’s time to Explore Brno! Head out on your own or with fellow Fabbers and discover the city through our hand-picked local recommendations. Whether you're into culture, craft beer, street vibes or just a good sunset stroll, Brno has something for everyone.

Practice your Czech skills, try local food, or dive into Brno’s famous bar scene. Want to meet locals? This is the perfect chance. Or if you prefer, take it slow: head back to your hotel, rest up, and get ready for the next day. We've even booked a huge local brewery just for FAB25 attendees – but heads up, capacity is limited, so plan ahead!

The FAB25 Map with best picks by the locals is available here (more places are being added).

Your Brno evening, your way.

title: Build your own machine

speakers.title: Quentin Bolsée
speakers.description: Quentin Bolsée is a postdoctoral researcher at the MIT Center for Bits and Atoms. His work focuses on open source machine building, computer vision applications and making digital fabrication more accessible.

extendedProps.description: Join us to learn about open source machines, and assemble your own. We'll provide supplies to build a simple one-axis motion system, as well as the electronics needed to control the motion directly from your computer.

title: Biofabricating Materials Working Group

speakers.title: Fabricademy Textile and Technology Academy
speakers.description: Our mission is to (re)shape and (re)define the implications and applications of technology in the textile and clothing industry, from the fashion sector to the upcoming wearable market. The program offers a broad overview of the state of the art of the current industry and builds on “learning by doing” methodologies, tackling themes like personal fabrication, distributed manufacturing, industry 4.0, wearable technology, biofabrication, assistive technologies and sustainability.

extendedProps.description: nterested in implementing biodesign and sustainable materials in your Fab Lab and projects? In this working group we will share our experiences, opportunities and knowledge around material, machines and biolabs! If you are developing or you are interested in biomaterials, mycelium composites, bacterial cellulose, 3d paste printers among others, join the biofabrication working group!

title: Exploring Sports and Making: Bringing Athletes, Fans, and Sports Enthusiasts into the Fab Community

speakers.title: Marcelo Worsley
speakers.description: Marcelo Worsley is the Karr Family Associate Professor of Computer Science and Learning Sciences at Northwestern University. He has written multiple articles on making and directs the technological innovations for inclusive learning and teaching (tiilt) lab. tiilt works to design interdisciplinary learning experiences and tools that allow users to tap into their agency as designer, inventors, and innovators.

extendedProps.description: Whether it is 3D printing sneakers, making sensor-embedded sports equipment, or a fitness wearable, there are countless opportunities to bridge sports and digital fabrication. In this workshop, participants will test sports technologies that we have created with youth and adults with different levels of athletic and technical proficiency. Participants will prototype their own sports (or physical movement) technologies using microcontrollers, low-cost materials, and digital fabrication tools.

title: From Prototype to Product: A Hands-on Workshop for Makers Entering the Market

speakers.title: Abhijeet Khandagale
speakers.description:
I'm Abheejit, a social entrepreneur from Nagpur, India, with extensive experience across the tech, non-profit, and policy sectors. I've been recognized as a Top Innovator by the World Economic Forum for my solution, ekatra. As the founder of Vruksh Ecosystems, a non-profit focused on grassroots entrepreneurship education.

As a community enabler, I lead the NagpurStartups community and have built Misfits, a vibrant network of 500+ early-stage social entrepreneurs across South Asia. I'm dedicated to helping scale startups in their Zero to One journey

extendedProps.description: This workshop guides participants through the critical steps of transforming a prototype into a market-ready product. Through interactive exercises, participants will develop their own maker-to-market roadmap, learn practical validation techniques, and discover resource optimization strategies. This hands-on session provides makers with actionable tools to navigate commercialization while staying true to their innovative vision.

title: Lens production using gel polish

speakers.title: Tal Mor
speakers.description: Industrial designer, FabLab manger, lecturer at HIT for 3d printing.

extendedProps.description: Lens production using gel polish and UV light machine, exploring optics and materials.

title: Research Paper Presentations - DAY 2

speakers.title: Peter Troxler
speakers.bio: Research Professor at Rotterdam University of Applied Sciences

title: FabLab & Agriculture working group

speakers.title: Luc Hanneuse
speakers.description: Luc Hanneuse is Agrilab UniLaSalle manager in Beauvais, France. Starting in the FabLab ecosystem in 2010 in Brussels FabLab.iMAL. Since 2018, he manages AgriLab, an open Innovation center in Agriculture and a FabLab in a Farm related to the polytechnical campus with large agricultural facilities & agricultural experimental fields. It wants to be the bridge between Agriculture, FabLab and the open source world. Instructor in FabAcademy, part of several working group, he develops a potential Agricademy distributed training program to empower agroecology & new technology practises.

extendedProps.description:
Discussion & presentation on FabLab & Agriculture.
I will explain some of AgriLab projects and progress, both rural & urban agriculture.
And the work toward a potential distributed Agricademy program.
Everybody involved in that subject is welcome to participate.

title: Tool Library & Library of things Working Group with Andreas & Charles

speakers.title: Andreas Kopp
speakers.description: Andreas Kopp is a social entrepreneur and founder of the non-profit Munich Tool Library . The project provides low-cost access to tools and everyday items, empowering people to repair, build, and create — with sustainability, creativity, and community at its core.

extendedProps.description: Discover how tool libraries & Library of things across Europe, the UK, and the US are rethinking ownership, repair, and sustainability. In this hands-on session, you’ll learn how to start or scale a tool library, explore real-world models, and connect with others building community through shared tools. Let’s fix things together — one borrowed tool at a time.

title: Fabman - Easy and Safe Fab Lab Management

speakers.title: Karim Jafarmadar
speakers.description: Karim Jafarmadar is a Co-Founder of Happylab in Vienna and Fabman, a workshop management system startup. Since co-founding Happylab in 2006 alongside Roland Stelzer, he has helped establish it as a key hub in Vienna’s maker scene. His expertise lies in conceptualizing national and international projects, significantly expanding access to technical education and creative technology training.

extendedProps.description: Learn how to manage your Fab Lab efficiently with Fabman. This workshop covers setup, access control, usage tracking, and member management. Discover best practices, explore new features, and gain practical skills to optimize your lab’s operations.

title: Distributed Disaster Response: Open source, offline, off-grid solutions for resilient manufacturing.

speakers.title: Eric Nitschke
speakers.description: Eric Nitschke is a Czech-based social entrepreneur building open source hardware and software for a more resilient and inclusive Internet. With his team at Wakoma Nitschke is working on distributed and localized production of connectivity infrastructure for disaster preparedness and response. Previously, Nitschke won the 2021 IEEE Connecting the Unconnected Challenge for his work on the 'nimble', and in 2018 co-founded what has become the largest community network in South Africa.

extendedProps.description: With at least 2.6 billion people unconnected the digital divide(s) remain among the most challenging global gaps to bridge. In places already connected we are seeing disasters and conflict increasingly disrupting communications and energy infrastructure and supply chains of all scales. This session will investigate open, resilient solutions that empower distributed makers with the tools, resources, and skills required to localize and produce essential items without reliable internet access.

title: From FabLabs to Urban Factories: scaling up sustainable productions

speakers.title: serena cangiano
speakers.description: Serena Cangiano is Senior Researcher at the Design Institute of SUPSI University, founder and Head of FabLab and faculty at MA in Interaction Design, an international program on prototyping and fabrication of future experiences. Her work centers on multimodal experimental practices to drive transformative processes in education, research, and technology innovation. Digital literacy and co-creation crossing complex social issues are key topics of her research as exemplified by the project LAUDS Factories (lauds.eu). She is board member of Swiss Design Network and Swiss Design Association.

extendedProps.description: FabLabs across Europe show that sustainable urban production is possible through innovation based on knowledge sharing, community engagement, and environmental care. This working group will explore key lessons from emerging practices and how to scale them from maker to market through local, small-scale production. It will also share insights on the open calls promoted by the EU-funded LAUDS Factories project.

title: Designing the Reuse Economy: Material Valuer Skills

speakers.title: Felipe Schmidt Fonseca
speakers.description: I am Dr. Felipe Schmidt Fonseca, an experienced Berlin-based Brazilian advocate for social-environmental innovation and free/open-source technologies turned researcher. I am the founder of Reuse City studio and a co-creator of semente, a toolkit for community projects; as well as the co-founder and lead articulator of the Tropixel network; and an active member of organisations such as GIG and Circular Berlin.

extendedProps.description: This workshop explores how makers can drive a circular economy through reuse. We'll define the "Circular Material Valuer" – a key role in assessing & revaluing discarded goods. Participants will identify skills needed for this work and ideate on fab/maker oriented initiatives on the topic.

title: Imagining a Global High School on the FabLab Network

speakers.title: Lit Liao
speakers.description: Lit is an experienced curriculum developer with over 12 years of experience at the intersection of engineering, management, design, and education. Her team has played a pivotal role in projects across 20+ public and international schools. Their commitment to excellence is reflected in multiple awards received at city, province, and national levels in curriculum design competitions as well as academic publications. Her research interests include project-based learning and interdisciplinary curriculum design.

extendedProps.description: High school is crucial for young learners to explore their interests and shape their futures. They deserve a say in their education. For the past four years, I’ve practiced cross-disciplinary, FabLab-based learning to help students engage with the real world and reflect critically. Now, I envision a distributed high school—co-created by the FabLab community—where students have more autonomy. Join this workshop to imagine a FabLab-powered global high school!

title: Fab Caps: how to create engagement in the Fab Academy alumni network.

speakers.title: Pablo Nuñez
speakers.description: I have been Fab Academy graduate since 2013 and i am collaborator and Fab Academy Instructor in Fab Lab León since then. I have been involved in the Fab Academy network actively, being part of Global Open Time, Assessment Team and Meme creator.

extendedProps.description:
Fab Caps its a new decentralized token, only valid in the Fab Academy ecosystem, to trade and earn knowledge and friendship. We want to gamify the Fab Academy course and network, provinding a way to recognice the effort and knowledge of those who make this Fab Academy network something unique in the whole world.
¿Why dont we think in a way to recognice this incredible effort they are making?
¿Why dont we create a new way to trade time and knowledge?
Come and join to co-create it.

title: Workshop: Intellectual Property Law and Open Source Hardware in Practice

speakers.title: Greta Sparzynski
speakers.description: Greta Sparzynski is a research assistant at the Bucerius Law School IP Centre in Hamburg and a newly qualified lawyer with expertise in the fields of intellectual property law, copyright and licensing. Greta currently works on IP, licensing and liability issues associated with the Hamburg Fab City project.

extendedProps.description: This workshop is designed to provide a practical understanding of the intersection between IP Law and OSH. We will start with an overview of key IP concepts relevant to OSH projects, focusing on the legal considerations in design, development, and sharing of hardware. By the end of the workshop, participants will be equipped with actionable knowledge and strategies to manage IP rights, mitigate legal risks, and foster effective collaboration in OSH projects.

title: Objects to Think With: A CAD Library for Teachers

speakers.title: Liz Whitewolf
speakers.description: Liz Whitewolf is the founder of eduFAB, a global organization advancing equity in STEM through digital fabrication. She leads the annual International Fab Educators Summit and supports global STEM educators. A former classroom teacher, she co-founded the Fab Learning Academy and serves as a Global Instructor and executive board member. Liz earned her Ed.D. in STEM Education from the University of Pittsburgh, focusing on K–12 makerspace integration, and is a 2016 FAB Academy graduate.

extendedProps.description: Discover the story behind Objects to Think With, the first peer-reviewed publication of its kind dedicated to CAD models designed for K–12 education. This session traces the development of CADlibrary.org—from initial concept to a fully launched, open-access platform that supports teachers with ready-to-use, classroom-tested, fabricated manipulatives. Learn how this project is expanding the role of digital fabrication in education by bridging open design, pedagogy, and peer-reviewed practice.

title: FabLabs & Erasmus+ Finding Partners & Structuring Your Project

speakers.title: Alexandre Rousselet
speakers.description: Makerspaces explorer since 2015. I co-created with some Erasmus student friends, VULCA Network and together we pushed the door of more than 1000 FabLabs, Makerspaces and Hackerspaces in Europe. Now I work full time for RFFLabs (The French FabLabs Network) and I'm a volunteer supporting the development of my local makerspace "Mountain Makers" located in my village in middle France.

extendedProps.description: A successful Erasmus+ application requires strong partnerships and a well-defined project. In this session, we go beyond theory to help FabLabs identify potential partners and structure their project. Participants will leave with a preliminary project outline and first (or new) contacts for their consortium.

title: Ready, Aim, Launch! Build Your Own Trebuchet

speakers.title: noy gabai
speakers.description: I'm a graphic designer and FabLab operator at Gelfand FabLab, Schwartz/Reisman Science Education Center, Weizmann Institute of Science, for two years. My work involves teaching physics to high school students through hands-on activities and interactive workshops, creating educational demonstrations, and mentoring student's and teacher's projects, including the International Safe-Cracking Competition and other physics projects.

extendedProps.description: In this hands-on workshop, participants will build a trebuchet—a medieval weapon that uses a heavy counterweight to launch projectiles. Using laser-cut parts and simple materials, they'll experiment and compete to achieve maximum range. No physics experience required—just creativity, teamwork, and fun!

title: Web3 for Makers: Regenerative Governance, Collective Power and Impact Funding

speakers.title: Luisa Fosco
speakers.description: Luisa Fosco is a designer, artist and maker from Rio de Janeiro, currently building bridges between Web3 Regen movement and maker communities through creative governance, collective care, and projects like RegenRio. Part of the advisory board of GreenPill Brazil and Cofounder of Fabulosa Lab.

extendedProps.description:
This workshop is designed to connect two powerful movements: the Maker Movement and Web3. It’s a hands-on, beginner-friendly session where we’ll explore how decentralized tools can support the way we organize, govern, and fund our maker communities and Fab Labs in a way that’s aligned with our values of openness, autonomy, and collaboration. As makers, we already build physical infrastructure, prototype ideas, and experiment with open tools. Web3 complements this by giving us digital infrastructure: things like DAOs (decentralized autonomous organizations), community tokens, and shared governance platforms that can help us share power, coordinate more fairly, and sustain our spaces. We’ll go fully hands-on to onboard and empower makers, navigating the web3 ecosystem to catalyze innovation and sustainability within the maker movement.

Program:
Each participant will:

  • Create a Web3 wallet
  • Learn decentralized governance tools
  • Create physical token to store their wallet key securely using digital fabrication

title: Introduction to satellite operations by using SatNOGS and other open-source tools

speakers.title: Alfredos Damkalis
speakers.description:
Alfredos (fredy) Damkalis (LSF) as an Open Source enthusiast he has participated and contributed in several free and open source projects and communities. Among others he is a member of Athens (Greece) hackerspace (hackerspace.gr), core contributor in Libre Space Foundation and radio amateur.

The last decade, he has been devoted to make Space accessible for everyone through his contributions in development of SatNOGS project and other LSF projects. Additionally he is operator of the SatNOGS Network of ground stations, supporting satellite teams and missions.

extendedProps.description:
SatNOGS project, the open-source ground stations network, is a platform for receiving, observing and tracking satellites, aiming to make Space exploration a little more accessible for everyone. It supports satellite missions, offers education opportunities and contributes to Space Situational Awareness (SSA).

In this workshop participants will get a basic knowledge on satellites and satellites operations and learn how to use SatNOGS and other open-source tools to support satellite missions.

title: Lathe Your Wooden Pen

extendedProps.summary: Turn raw wood into your own custom pen – smooth, personal, and made by you.
extendedProps.description:
Step up to the lathe and shape your own custom wooden pen. In this hands-on workshop, you’ll turn a block of wood into a finished writing tool – unique, personal, and satisfying to make.

– Learn the basics of woodturning on a lathe
– Shape and smooth your wooden pen body
– Assemble it with a pre-made pen mechanism
– Optionally add a custom engraving with a laser cutter

No experience needed – just bring your curiosity and a steady hand. Our instructors will guide you through every step, and in just 20 minutes, you’ll walk away with a pen that’s truly yours.

title: First Stitches: Basics of Sewing 101

extendedProps.summary: Learn how to thread a machine, sew a straight line, and make your very first textile creation.
extendedProps.description:
Curious about sewing but never touched a machine before? This is your moment. Join us for a relaxed, hands-on intro where you'll learn the basics – from threading the sewing machine to making your first real stitches.

– Learn how to handle a sewing machine with confidence
– Practice straight stitches, curves, and corners
– Make a small, useful item you can take home

No experience needed, just bring your curiosity. Our instructors will help you stitch with confidence – whether you want to mend your gear or start your next maker project in textiles. This is where it all begins.

title: From Heritage to Hardware: Bridging Craft & Tech

speakers.title: Shefali Desai
speakers.description: As a designer, maker, and educator, I’m deeply inspired by the intersection of traditional craftsmanship and digital techniques. My practice focuses on creating meaningful, intentional, and aesthetically rich designs that blend heritage with innovation. With a strong eye for detail and a strategic approach, I strive to craft work that resonates both visually and conceptually.

extendedProps.description: This talk explores the intersection of traditional cultural symbols and contemporary digital making. Centered on the Shri Yantra Mandala Jewelry Collection, the project examines how ancient visual languages can be reinterpreted through modern tools like laser cutting, 3D printing, and DIY material experimentation. It highlights the potential of digital fabrication to not only preserve but reimagine heritage craft, offering new ways to connect past wisdom with future-forward design.

title: Project Mjolnir, Open Source Adaptative Mountainbike

speakers.title: Jorge Montalvo
speakers.description: Mechatronic Designer, and Digital Fabrication Specialist based in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates. Holds a Master's degree in Engineering and an a wide array of certifications including Project Management Professional PMP, Solidworks Expert CSWE, and Autodesk Certified Professional. With vast experience in Digital Manufacturing, 3d printing, CAD/CAM/CAE, and product design

extendedProps.description:
Project Mjolnir is an Open-Source Adaptive Mountain
Bike Initiative conceived to enable more people with
disabilities, in particular wheelchair users to build a
bike and participate in the sport of mountain biking.
The objective is to create a modular system that has a
low barrier of entry for anyone interested in building
their own adaptive mtb or anyone who wants to help
someone build an adaptive MTB.

title: Sketch-to-business: from concept, to clients, to selling everywhere

speakers.title: Alexander Iulius Vasilescu
speakers.description:
25 year old nerd, with 10 years of printing, 4 of which professionally. Proud babysitter of 3 FDM and 2 resin printers. =)
- Robotics BEng from Plymouth UK
- nearly 2 years of e-Nable volunteering as designer for hand prosthetics. (enablingthefuture.org, hub.e-nable.com)
- founder of 3Dapi, a 3d printing startup that connects designers with print farms

extendedProps.description: Designers and print farms are two essential sides of the 3D printing ecosystem. We’ll explore the current market: where and how different consumer niches operate today, where inefficiencies exist, and how 3DAPI bridges the gap between designers with local production.

title: How to I+D for local entrepreneurship

speakers.title: Pablo Nunez
speakers.description: Im a enthusiast of new technologies applied to manufacturing and collaborator of Fab Lab León from 2012. Fab Academy graduate in 2013, i have been involved in the Fab Lab Network since then. Undertaking a new business model with the web fabricotusideas.com i help entrepreneurship in my area to innovate and improve with their bussiness.

extendedProps.description: Listen how I have helped the local entrepreneurship in my town, León in Spain, to co-create and develop new products and resources to create innovative ideas. From more than 10 years ago i have applied the digital fabrication to local products and bussiness issues, helping to transform ideas in reality. I will make a showcase of the best ideas i have fabricated, to inspire you to do the same.

title: Fabricating Social Impact

speakers.title: Rico Kanthatham
speakers.description: Rico is the Founder of Skylabworkshop in Kamakura, Japan, an independently run community fablab that provides STEAM learning workshops for children and young adults. Rico is also a Fabacademy and Fabricademy instructor, a co-creator of the Fab All-in program, and a coordinating member of the Global Kids team.

extendedProps.description: In this talk I will share my learnings and observations from the various social impact initiatives that I have engaged in as a member of the Fablab community.

title: Smart Citizen Meets Meshtastic (A Hackathon) - DAY 2

speakers.title: Óscar González
speakers.description: Óscar González is an Industrial Engineer with expertise in data analysis, testing, and calibration through his experience in automotive and sensor development. He is the Sense Making Lead at Fab Lab Barcelona doing research and development on the Smart Citizen project. Óscar has worked as a researcher on various projects, spanning the development and implementation of open source technologies and low-cost sensors, including their application in citizen science initiatives, as tools for environmental and health assessment, and the interoperability of data across research infrastructures.

extendedProps.description: We propose a hackathon focused on air quality monitoring, using decentralised IoT solutions, potentially creating the first building block of a new Smart Citizen Kit. We want to move beyond traditional frameworks towards emancipatory technologies like Meshtastic, helping Fab Labs to build resilient, community-driven sensing networks. Building on over a decade of work, Smart Citizen is now a global network of technologists, scientists, and community leaders.

title: Conservation of Andalusian Flamenco Heritage: Digital fabrication of castanets

speakers.title: Ana Pilar Valerga
speakers.description:
Ana Pilar Valerga is currently working in the Department of Mechanical Engineering and Industrial Design at the University of Cadiz. She is a lecturer and researcher in Digital Design, Manufacturing Engineering, and Materials Engineering, and more particularly in Additive Manufacturing processes.
She is the coordinator of FabLab Cadiz since its creation by the Director of the Centre, Luis Lafuente Molinero, where it is located (the School of Engineering of the University of Cadiz).

extendedProps.description: Discover the fusion of digital fabrication and cultural heritage in this hands-on workshop, where participants will craft traditional Andalusian castanets using modern manufacturing techniques. Attendees will learn 3D printing and vacuum casting methods to create these iconic percussion instruments, preserving and evolving the craftsmanship of flamenco music. This session offers a unique opportunity to explore digital craftsmanship while celebrating Andalusian musical traditions.

title: Soft Robotics Prototyping

speakers.title: Fabricademy Textile and Technology Academy
speakers.description: Our mission is to (re)shape and (re)define the implications and applications of technology in the textile and clothing industry, from the fashion sector to the upcoming wearable market. The program offers a broad overview of the state of the art of the current industry and builds on “learning by doing” methodologies, tackling themes like personal fabrication, distributed manufacturing, industry 4.0, wearable technology, biofabrication, assistive technologies and sustainability.

extendedProps.description: Soft material technologies provide lightweight, more-human and bio-mimetic solutions in the field of design for interaction and robotics. In this workshop we will introduce a quick and fun fabrication method for inflatables that is allowing designers to easily prototype soft actuators and create motion and interaction by understanding airflow.

title: TELLURIC: A Journey of Paper from Earth to Garment — Crafting with Machines, Tradition, and Waste

speakers.title: Surzhana Radnaeva
speakers.description: Surzhana Radnaeva is a Buryat Siberian designer and founder of Traditional Futures, a studio dedicated to honoring ancestral knowledge through material innovation. Her work treats garments as evolving systems, using biomaterials such as paper, mycelium, kombucha etc. By blending craft, performance, and fabrication, she collaborates with makers and engineers to preserve and reimagine cultural traditions through textile machines and open-source tools—bridging past and future.

speakers.title: Maxime Gravet
speakers.description: Co-founder and lead product developer at Mekanika, my main background is mechatronics engineering and coding. I also have experience in product design and in the industrialisation of various types of products.

extendedProps.description: Project TELLURIC, from the Latin "comes from Earth," explores life, cycles, matter, and transformation. A collaboration between Traditional Futures and Mekanika, the project centers on a modular, open-source Hollander Beater that transforms textile waste into high-quality paper. This paper is used to craft avant-garde garments, merging fashion, craft, and engineering in a circular system where garments return to their origin.

title: Fablab Fellowship: A Global Platform for Purpose-Driven Technological Ventures from Academia

speakers.title: JAIR JESUS LEON LUCANO
speakers.description: Jair Leon is a researcher and knowledge manager at a FabLab. He leads initiatives focused on investment in project portfolios and international engagement for undergraduate students through the Fellowship program, where students develop projects aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and with potential for market application or academic dissemination through Scopus-indexed research. He is a specialist in STEM projects, a publicist, and a maker.

extendedProps.description: Join us for an inspiring deep-dive into the FabLab UC Fellowship model an end-to-end framework that transforms social-impact ideas into conference-ready projects.

title: Algorithmic modelling with Grasshopper - not just for clay 3D printing

speakers.title: Petr Křivohlavý
speakers.description: I am a PhD student at the Faculty of Mechanical Engineering, Brno University of Technology, focusing on robotic 3D printing. As part of my academic work, I teach courses on algorithmic and parametric modeling as well as 3D printing. Additionally, I am the administrator of additiveLAB at strojLAB, overseeing its operations and activities related to additive manufacturing.

extendedProps.description: This workshop focuses on algorithmic modeling with Grasshopper and is designed for beginners or those new to the software. Participants will learn the fundamentals of Grasshopper and apply them to create a script generating geometry for clay 3D printing. However, these skills are widely applicable beyond this use case. By the end of the session, attendees will have a solid foundation in Grasshopper for various design and fabrication applications.

title: Is Open Source worth It?

speakers.title: Dave Hakkens
speakers.description: Dave Hakkens is a designer from the Netherlands. His work is centred around developing open-source projects that work on global environmental problems. Bringing people together and building communities to collaborate on these issues. With Precious Plastic he built a global community of recyclers, Projects Kamp is Researching how to live sustainable and Fixing Fashion is focussed on reducing textile waste.

extendedProps.summary: Lessons learned from building global Open Source Projects

title: Keynotes, panels, talks (Maker-to-Market)

speakers.title: Bas van Abel
speakers.description:
Bas van Abel, a Dutch visionary designer and entrepreneur, stands at the forefront of sustainable innovation with his open design approach. With grit and determination, he has paved the way for the groundbreaking sustainable smartphone company Fairphone and co-founded the circular food startup De Clique.

As a passionate tinkerer, Bas firmly believes in the Maker movement's mantra: "if you can't open it, you don't own it." This kind of ownership is crucial in the cultural shift needed to address today's pressing environmental and social issues. During his time at Waag Futurelab, Bas established Fablab Amsterdam and co-authored the book "Open Design Now." In 2013, he made the bold move to spin off Fairphone as a social enterprise, aiming to revolutionize the phone industry from the inside out.  

With a decade of experience as the founder and CEO of Fairphone, Bas has successfully navigated the competitive smartphone market, turning the mission-driven company into an international success story. His trailblazing efforts have earned him prestigious awards and media coverage from the likes of CNN, The New York Times, and Der Spiegel.

speakers.title: Roldan Descamps
speakers.description: Roldan is an engineer with an entrepreneurial spirit. After exploring sectors as diverse as scientific research, sustainable finance and start-ups, he partnered up to co-create Mekanika and aim at opening and bringing more transparency to manufacturing. His diverse expertise combined with the deep technical skills of his co-founders helped build a unique and successful open-hardware business, growing in the heart of Europe. He is also a fine cook and a jazz enthusiast, and remains a true maker to his core, building all kind of hardware projects in his free time.

speakers.title: Dave Hakkens
speakers.description: Dave Hakkens is a designer from the Netherlands. His work is centred around developing open-source projects that work on global environmental problems. Bringing people together and building communities to collaborate on these issues. With Precious Plastic he built a global community of recyclers, Projects Kamp is Researching how to live sustainable and Fixing Fashion is focussed on reducing textile waste.

speakers.title: Jeremy Losaw
speakers.bio: FabLab Specialist at UNC Charlotte Super Fab Lab
speakers.description: From RC cars to heart valves, Jeremy is an experienced maker. Jeremy is currently the Super Fab Lab Specialist at the University of North Carolina Charlotte where he helps the university community to create new innovations. For 13 years he served as Director of Engineering at Enventys Partners, a product development and launch agency in Charlotte, NC USA. He is a YLAI (Young Leaders of America) and YTILI (Young Transatlantic Innovation Leaders Initiative) alumni and has delivered innovation and IoT training in the US, Dominican Republic, Thailand, Namibia, Italy, and Grenada. He has more than 10 issued patents and many products that have been launched via crowdfunding and brought to mass production. In addition, Jeremy is a former NASCAR auto racing engineer and experienced prototyper, and has contributed more than 200 articles to Inventors Digest magazine.

speakers.title: Simon Klinga
speakers.description: Simon Klinga is the founder and leader of the student team LASAR, which won over a NASA jury at the prestigious U.S. competition Conrad Challenge. In 2024, his team launched the first Czech student satellite, LASARsat, aboard SpaceX’s Falcon 9. The satellite tests the use of lasers to restart unresponsive satellites and help clean up space debris. Simon started LASAR at 16, and under his leadership the team grew from 5 to 35 members, prototyping the satellite also in FabLab Brno. He’s received the Czech Prime Minister’s Award, a Moonshot Award in New York, and a Future Port Award. In 2025, Forbes named him to its 30 Under 30 list.

speakers.title: Kohshi Katoh
speakers.description:
Kohshi Katoh is an engineer at Toyota Motor Corporation, dedicated to creating transformative mobility systems that awaken and enhance the body’s innate kinetic capabilities, delivering deeply engaging and immersive experiences. Through such innovations, he aims to make a meaningful impact on society.

He is responsible for advanced technology and value development related to user experience. He is passionate about leveraging human kinetic motion to enhance the riding experience for both drivers and passengers. One of his earlier studies was applied to the Toyota FV2, a concept vehicle that is steered by the rider’s body lean and directional pressure applied through the legs. The FV2 was showcased at the 2013 Tokyo Motor Show.

He has also developed the “Kinetic Seat,” featuring a movable hip and backrest designed to utilize the natural function of the human spine for improved body support and head stabilization. This concept was first presented as the “Lexus Kinetic Seat Concept” at the 2016 Paris Motor Show, and more recently exhibited at the Toyota booth at the Japan Mobility Show 2023, as well as at the International Home Care & Rehabilitation Exhibition & Forum in 2022, 2023, and 2024.

speakers.title: Michael Lorentschk
speakers.description:
Michael is the initiator of a new tech education program at UnternehmerTUM, according to Financial Times Europe's biggest entrepreneurship center since 2024, focussing on STEM education. Through industry-relevant making in combination with design teamwork, personal growth and social inclusion, he is constantly piloting established formats like hackathons at the UnternehmerTUM MakerSpace workshop locations for new target groups together with a strong network in Munich. He's also actively committed in driving systematic convergence of schools and fablabs/makerspaces in Bavaria. 

Since joing UnternehmerTUM he has accompanied the founding journies of many successful embedded/hardware-based B2B startups like Angsa, Filics or Olive Robotics – supporting fast development cycles that turn ideas into functioning multi-disciplinary prototypes that excite in demonstrations and pave the way for small series, patents, investments and market entry.

extendedProps.summary: From tinkering to market – explore how FabLab ideas become real-world innovations with impact.
extendedProps.description:
The Maker-to-Market track dives into one of the most exciting – and challenging – transitions in the FabLab journey: taking maker projects from prototypes to products that truly make an impact. While FabLabs have long been a launchpad for creative experimentation, commercialization remains a challenge.

Hear success stories from FabLab-born businesses, learn how to turn projects into products and how incubation can support your next step, and dive into the conversation around open-source vs proprietary approaches. We’ll also examine the evolving role of FabLabs as key players in local innovation ecosystems – no longer just spaces for DIY enthusiasts, but active drivers of education, entrepreneurship and economic development.

title: Hands-On Rapid Prototyping by PrusaLab: Print, Solder, Laser!

speakers.title: Aleksandar Ristić
speakers.description:
PrusaLab is a prototype workshop of Josef Prusa and Prusa Research.
We offer development and prototyping support for individuals, project teams and companies.
With the backing of a large company and a team of experts, we provide consulting, acceleration packages and access to a wide ecosystem of technologies.
We are a place where almost anything can be made, and our goal is to turn ideas into reality.

Aleks is PrusaLab based Electrical engineer resposible for all smart solutions for our instalations and other projects.

extendedProps.description:
Dive into the fundamentals of rapid prototyping! Participants will learn and experience key techniques firsthand: 3D printing, soldering, and the basics of laser cutting*.
What You'll Take Away: A 3D printed creation and a light-up souvenir to remember FAB25.
What you´ll need: A Tinkercad account created beforehand is highly recommended, but it´s possible to create one during the workshop.
*3d printing and soldering during workshop / lasser cutting via presentation

title: Working Group: Fab Learning Academy

speakers.title: Iván Sánchez Milara
speakers.description: I am university teacher at University of Oulu (Finland) and instructor at Fab Lab Oulu. I graduated from Fab Academy in 2017. In the last years I have developed different education programs, courses, workshops and other educational activities aiming to develop professional competence of teachers in STEAM and digital fabrication. I am currently part of the executive committee of the Fab Learning Academy.

speakers.title: Liz Whitewolf
speakers.description: Liz Whitewolf is the founder of eduFAB, a global organization advancing equity in STEM through digital fabrication. She leads the annual International Fab Educators Summit and supports global STEM educators. A former classroom teacher, she co-founded the Fab Learning Academy and serves as a Global Instructor and executive board member. Liz earned her Ed.D. in STEM Education from the University of Pittsburgh, focusing on K–12 makerspace integration, and is a 2016 FAB Academy graduate.

speakers.title: Santi Fuentemilla
speakers.description: Santiago Fuentemilla Garriga holds a Master’s in Architecture and a postgraduate specialization in digital fabrication from Fab Academy, boasting over 15 years of international experience at studios like OPR, FHAUS, and OPERA. Since 2013, he has led the Future Learning Unit at IAAC – Fab Lab BCN, pioneering innovative educational models. He directs the Fab Academy and Fabricademy in Barcelona, executive board of the Fab Learning Academy board, and teaches in advanced design master’s programs MDEF and MDDI, shaping future digital innovators.

extendedProps.description: Fab Learning Academy is part of the Academany family of programs, focused on developing educators' professional skills through STEAM and digital fabrication. The first edition of the program ran during autumn and winter 2024–2025, with over 20 participants from around the world. In this working group session, we'll give a short overview of the program and collaborate on shaping the second edition, which will launch in September 2025.

title: Doors Opened
Venue Shift

extendedProps.summary: A short 15-min walk workshop venue (1200 m), plus time to relax!
extendedProps.description:
After lunch, it’s time to shift gears and spaces. Let's move from the main venue to the workshop venue at University, just a short and easy 15-minute walk away. Once you arrive, feel free to grab a coffee, take a short break, and get ready for the afternoon full of hands-on or theoretical individualized workshop sessions.

Need help finding the way? Check out the route here.

title: The impact of a FAB LAB in a school: state of the art from the first portuguese Fab Lab in a school

speakers.title: António Gonçalves
speakers.description: Degree in physics, Secondary school teacher of physics and chemistry. Coordinator at Lab Aberto Fab Lab. Fab Academy 2019. Interested about FAB LAB in school.

speakers.title: Liz Whitewolf
speakers.description: Liz Whitewolf is the founder of eduFAB, a global organization advancing equity in STEM through digital fabrication. She leads the annual International Fab Educators Summit and supports global STEM educators. A former classroom teacher, she co-founded the Fab Learning Academy and serves as a Global Instructor and executive board member. Liz earned her Ed.D. in STEM Education from the University of Pittsburgh, focusing on K–12 makerspace integration, and is a 2016 FAB Academy graduate.

extendedProps.description:
I'm absolutely thrilled to invite you to a special roundtable discussion! We'll be diving deep into the transformative impact of a fab lab in a school environment, particularly how it's been slowly and steadily reshaping things since the first Portuguese fab lab opened its doors in the Henriques Nogueira Secondary School (https://fablab.aehn.net/), Torres Vedras, portugal.

Our conversation will focus on strategies in the FAB LAB implementation procedure and results in three key areas: how these labs have revolutionized learning, breathed new life into social activities, and significantly boosted motivation among everyone involved.

We're incredibly eager to hear about your own experiences and insights. By sharing our collective knowledge and best practices, we aim to document our discussion, ensuring everyone benefits from the valuable lessons learned in your unique setting."

title: Maker-to-market with Solidworks

speakers.title: Suchit Jain
speakers.bio: Vice President of Strategy & Business Development Dassault Systèmes
speakers.description:
Suchit Jain is a technology and business leader dedicated to driving innovation at the intersection of design, engineering, and manufacturing. As Vice President of Strategy and Business Development at Dassault Systèmes, he plays a pivotal role in shaping the future of SOLIDWORKS, leading its evolution through cloud innovation, AI, and the expansion into the Mainstream Business. He also spearheads User Advocacy and Engagement, championing a thriving global community of millions of SOLIDWORKS users.

Beyond Dassault Systèmes, Suchit is a mentor, advisor, and advocate for startups and entrepreneurs, collaborating with leading incubators and accelerators worldwide. He is involved with MassChallenge, MassRobotics, and serves on the advisory board of Greentown Labs, North America's largest cleantech incubator.

His passion for education and democratizing technology extends to his involvement with the Fab Foundation and MIT’s Center for Bits & Atoms, where he supports initiatives that provide global access to digital fabrication tools and skills. Suchit frequently shares insights on AI, design, manufacturing, and the future of work, inspiring professionals, students, and entrepreneurs to embrace cutting-edge technologies and transformative collaboration models.

title: 11 Deadly Sins in Product Development

speakers.title: Jeremy Losaw
speakers.bio: FabLab Specialist at UNC Charlotte Super Fab Lab
speakers.description: From RC cars to heart valves, Jeremy is an experienced maker. Jeremy is currently the Super Fab Lab Specialist at the University of North Carolina Charlotte where he helps the university community to create new innovations. For 13 years he served as Director of Engineering at Enventys Partners, a product development and launch agency in Charlotte, NC USA. He is a YLAI (Young Leaders of America) and YTILI (Young Transatlantic Innovation Leaders Initiative) alumni and has delivered innovation and IoT training in the US, Dominican Republic, Thailand, Namibia, Italy, and Grenada. He has more than 10 issued patents and many products that have been launched via crowdfunding and brought to mass production. In addition, Jeremy is a former NASCAR auto racing engineer and experienced prototyper, and has contributed more than 200 articles to Inventors Digest magazine.

extendedProps.description: This talk describes 11 common ways that inventors and entrepreneurs fail at bringing products to market. Product development mythology has plenty of success and rags to riches stories about how the garage inventor brought a wildly successful product to market. However, there are so many more lessons to learn from projects that never made it to the store shelves. This program describes the commonalities and how to avoid them.
