title: ERA-5 the Digital Fabricated Robot Kit fills the gap in Iraqi education with Czech Republic support

speakers.title: Nawres Naser
speakers.description: Industrialist, painter, and sculptor who wanted his sculptures to move so he made them robots, Academically he graduated from pharmacy school, but his passion as a maker, where he self-taught 3D design and mastered it, mixing science and art, starting from it to the world of industrial design, digital Fabrication, and 3D printing, where he excels in the Digital Fabrication, which is the science of transforming mental images into tangible products. He founded the Science Camp by sharing the workshop of his house with local makers to be the first Iraqi Makerspace & digital Fab Lab in 2013

extendedProps.description:
Supporting the educational system in Iraq with interactive STEM Education by digitally fabricated educational Robot Kits made by Science Camp Fab Lab supported by the Czech Republic Embassy, a +300 students success story.
Digital Fabrication design, laser cut manufacturing, women in tech mentors, and the friendly makers' culture are the recipe for changing the reality of traditional education to a new era of interactive and closer to a real-life problem-solving approach.

title: The SDG Impact Game; the Czechia case; practice your impact-by-design

speakers.title: Pieter van der Hijden
speakers.description:
Pieter van der Hijden (Netherlands) studied Applied Physics, worked in R&D in gaming/simulation and then became a management consultant. He did projects on gaming, making and learning; also abroad and especially in Suriname where his wife Bidia comes from. 
In Suriname Pieter implemented the first Caribbean fablab. Now, he is a volunteer of ZapLab AT Villa Zapakara there, currently in STEAM curriculum development for outbound activities in the interior. Within the fablab network Pieter coordinates the Global Working Group "Fablabs and the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)".

extendedProps.description:
In this game with the Czech Republic as a case, three ministries identify an SDG target suitable for the business community to take up. Three SDG investment funds formulate rules of the game to keep their portfolio both commercially and sustainably sound. Three fablabs see the SDG targets as the impact to be achieved, reason back from there to the activities to be undertaken and draw up a business plan for this: impact-by-design that can count on the support of the SDG investment fund.
More info: https://bit.ly/fab25-sdgs

title: Spin & Cut: CNC Essentials

extendedProps.summary: Get hands-on with CNC basics and contribute to a one-of-a-kind maker art piece.
extendedProps.description:
Learn how to prepare your design for cutting, tweak the key parameters, and transfer your file to the Mekanika CNC machine – all in a quick, beginner-friendly session.

No prior CNC experience? No problem. Our instructors from Mekanika and the FAB25 team will walk you through every step:
– How to set up your cutting data in minutes
– What parameters really matter (and why)
– How to safely and easily run your file on the machine

Once your custom piece is milled, you’ll add it to a growing collaborative artwork – a living, evolving piece made by the FAB25 community.

Fast, friendly, and hands-on – this is the perfect way to get started with CNC and leave your personal mark on the event.

title: FAB25 Maker Coin: Solder It. Blink It. Wear It.

extendedProps.summary: Come and finish your FAB25 Maker Coin by soldering everything together.
extendedProps.description:
Grab a soldering iron, the PCB from your welcome pack, and components from the table – and let's bring your FAB25 Maker Coin to life in just about 20 minutes!

  • By choosing the resistor values, you get to control how fast your badge blinks. It’s not just about soldering – it’s about making it your own.
  • Never soldered before? No worries. Our friendly instructors are here to guide you step by step. Whether you're a first-timer or a seasoned maker, this is a fun and hands-on way to kick off your FAB25 experience.
  • Wear your coin proudly all week – especially during networking sessions. Attach it to your plastic pouch or use the included magnet for a clean, stylish look.

title: Day Start + Fab Announcements
Transferrable Skills and Formative Assessments: Using the Maker Literacies Rubrics and Lesson Plans

speakers.title: Morgan Chivers
speakers.description: Morgan Chivers is a lifelong learner who graduated San Jose State University with four simultaneously-conferred degrees: BA History, BA Global Studies, BFA Photography, & BFA Spatial Arts. Morgan then earned an MFA in Glass/Intermedia before joining the UTA FabLab to lead the evolution from small engineering makerspace to a huge space with thriving culture representative of UTA’s extraordinarily diverse campus! Morgan is now the Maker Literacies Librarian, focusing on embedded librarianship and research around experiential learning and the transferrable skills gained through iterative design.

extendedProps.description: Participants will engage in an interactive curriculum development exercise that demonstrates how to integrate the Maker Literacies competencies and rubrics into coursework to foster adaptable cross-disciplinary thinking. Through discussion and hands-on planning activities, attendees will leave with concrete strategies for incorporating Maker Literacies into their curricula to cultivate problem-solving, global awareness, and 21st Century skills in their students.

title: Gender inclusive making - how we supported more than 2000 women in FabLabs

speakers.title: Karolina Guzek
speakers.description: Since 2017 Karolina has been involved in the development of FabLabs in Poland, designing and coordinating innovative social projects in the field of STEAM, tailoring them to the needs of various beneficiaries, including marginalized groups. Head of FabLab Warsaw, member of the board of the Robisz.to Association. Equality and women’s rights advocate. She holds a master's degree in law with a specialisation in environmental protection and human rights, and has a background in design. She is included in the list of 100 persons of merit for the development of digital skills in Poland in 2024.

extendedProps.description: During my speech, I'll discuss Association Robisz.to’s women-focused programs at FabLabs— Maker Woman and PAKT for Women — that have reached over 2,500 people in eight years. I will examine the status of women in the ICT sector in Poland and Europe, delving into their needs and motivations. Using case studies and an in-depth report covering over 800 participants and educational outcomes for 300 women, I will highlight how tailored initiatives can enhance career prospects in ICT.

title: MakeHERSpace: Empowering Women in STEM through Makerspace Mentorship and Workshops

speakers.title: Sarah Jane Fabito
speakers.description: Sarah serves as the Executive Director of Women in Tech - Batangas chapter in the Philippines. WiTech Batangas, a youth-led nonprofit organization, is dedicated to bridging the gender gap in STEM fields and empowering the next generation through education and inspiration. She firmly believes in the transformative power of education to uplift lives and society, advocating strongly for STEM education among girls. Currently, she is pursuing her Master's in Education at the University of Sussex in the UK.

extendedProps.description: The goal of my talk is to showcase the impact of maker activities and mentorship in empowering women in STEM. I've seen girls who initially doubted their abilities and become confident individuals ready to take on STEM. Maker education is still new in the Philippines. By sharing our story, I hope to show that organizing makerspace activities in communities or schools can help encourage students to pursue a career in STEM.

title: STEM Meets Art: Empowering Girls Through the TechCreate Program

speakers.title: Claire Chaikin-Bryan
speakers.description: Claire Chaikin-Bryan is the Fab Lab Lead at Lake Mac Libraries Fab Lab, Australia.  She is a qualified Town Planner, Civil and Environmental Engineer and Fab Academy Instructor with a background in smart cities, construction, visual arts, graphics design, digital engineering, coding and electronics.

extendedProps.description: Discover the TechCreate Girls Program; an Australian initiative reimagining STEM education for high school girls by introducing STEM via creative disciplines. During this intensive 10 week program participants dive into digital art, fashion wearables, bioart and interactive media, learning to merge technology with creativity. By combining this with foundational maker skills the program encourages girls to become confident innovators and future leaders in both tech and creative industries.

title: BioZine: DIY, Machines & Smashing Stereotypes – The Maker Woman Experience Through Zine-Making

speakers.title: Malgorzata Zaluska
speakers.description: Motivated by design thinking, technology, and soft skills development, I am an educator and craftswoman. I blend technical expertise with social design, emphasizing community-driven applications. As an instructor at a FabLab powered by Orange, I create hands-on learning experiences for diverse audiences. I foster collaboration across age groups, teaching design thinking and creativity to build confidence and problem-solving skills. Continuously refining program content with fellow educators, I incorporate feedback to enhance engagement and address real-world challenges.

extendedProps.description: Let's make a zine! In this four-hour in-situ workshop, we’ll design and print graphics using the "quick screen printing" technique. Instead of a traditional one, we’ll use a CO₂ laser cutter and steel mesh frames coated with biopolymers. Pre-prepared components and quick-drying ink will let us create a mini-publication on the spot—smashing stereotypes thanks to maker culture!

title: Working Group Care: Co-Creating Health Through Fab Communities

speakers.title: Adriana Cabrera
speakers.description: Adriana Cabrera is a designer and media artist working at the intersection of innovation, digital fabrication, and sustainable material ecologies. She co-leads Fabcare.network and lectures at Fabricademy in the field of soft robotics, promoting inclusive health and care through open innovation. At CSCP, she supports Fab City strategies for circular and co-creative regional development in Germany’s Fab Region Bergische Triangle.

extendedProps.description: This theoretical workshop brings together initiatives like FabCare and Careables to explore how digital fabrication and collaborative design can transform health and well-being. Through case studies, it reflects on how Fab Labs can help co-create inclusive, accessible, and replicable care solutions with patients, caregivers, and professionals, while promoting global networks of open innovation.

title: Breaking Into Lock Picking

speakers.title: Vik Olliver
speakers.description: Vik is best known as the person who built the first replicating 3D printer, the RepRap, the ancestor of most desktop 3D printers. He also invented PLA filament. These days he works at the local Fab Lab in New Zealand which has a high proportion of special needs and elderly members of the community, as well as significant involvement with local cultural arts. He is equally at home behind a keyboard, soldering iron, screwdriver, or something entirely more energetic and dangerous.

extendedProps.description:
Locks are the archetypal “Black Box.” They are supposed to only operate one way, and have secret contents. But inquisitive people want to undo them, explore them, understand the inner workings and limitations of technology. This is the essence of education in the Fab Lab, and why lock picking is such a subversively good fit. Are you one of those people? If you are, this workshop presents a hands-on guide to how locks work – and how to make them work without the key.

You can let yourself out...

title: Make chocolate of your face

speakers.title: Tex Kang
speakers.description:
Current manager of University of Pennsylvania's library and makerspace 
Former manager of Fab Lab Seoul and Bio Lab Seoul 
Organizer of the Philadelphia Maker Faire 
Burning Man designer and fire performer 
Has two dogs named 'light' and 'speed' in Tolkien elvish

extendedProps.description: We will 3D scan your face and make chocolate heads you can give to others and say "eat me". We will be using free to use 3D scanners using your phone (Android or iOS) and learning how to convert the data into printable files that will be printed and then used to make food safe molds to make chocolate or candles (if food is an issue).

title: Rapid prototyping for laser cutting in Onshape with scripts (Laser Joint and Auto Layout)

speakers.title: Luis Daniel Haro Laque
speakers.description:
Mechanical Engineer with additional training in Electronics and Industrial Automation. I have experience in digital manufacturing and rapid prototyping in Fab Lab environments. I consider myself an enthusiast of using free and alternative software tools, such as FreeCAD and Onshape, among others, to develop accessible and innovative solutions.

I have knowledge in laser cutting, 3D printing, CNC milling, and parametric design. I have skills in collaborative work, agile methodologies, and knowledge transfer through technical teaching and practical training. I am passionate about technology, con

extendedProps.description: In the rapid prototyping workshop, participants will learn how to use Onshape software to design parts for laser cutting. Using the Laser Joint and Auto Layout scripts, they will automate the generation of joints and the efficient layout of parts on sheets. Finally, they will export the files in DXF format, ready for manufacturing.

title: Scanning: ScaleTag, 3D (un)printing, and other assorted adventures

speakers.title: Nikhil Lal
speakers.description: Nikhil Lal is a researcher at the MIT Media Lab focusing on tools in genetic engineering and modular machine platforms for biotechnologists. His research leverages applied hardware expertise to create machines (hardware) that make machines (wetware/biology). Prior to MIT he spent years as lead engineer of various startups where he was responsible for the gamut of engineering disciplines and earned his Bachelor's and Master's at Cornell University where he founded the Rapid Prototyping Lab - the university's first open makerspace for engineers. Nikhil has historically bounced in and out of make

extendedProps.description: Bridge the physical-digital divide by learning how to capture objects, not just print them. Using basic fab lab tools like digital cameras, CNCs, printers we’ll teach attendees how to easily scan their ideas, 1 pixel at a time. Participants will use our simple tool suite to create 2D and 3D scans of everyday objects, no advanced hardware required!

title: Essential Multimeter Skills: A Hands-on Workshop

speakers.title: Jeffrey Roe
speakers.description: Jeffrey Roe is a software and hardware engineer from Ireland, designing public transport systems at Telclic. He is an active member of Tog Hackerspace, co-runs Dublin Maker, and lectures in design innovation at Maynooth University. With expertise in IoT, electronics, and sustainable transport, Jeffrey enjoys sharing hands-on skills through workshops on Arduino, repair, and citizen science. He is also an amateur radio enthusiast and part of the global maker movement.

extendedProps.description: Learn how to confidently use a multimeter in this beginner-friendly, hands-on workshop. Whether troubleshooting electronics, repairing household devices, or working on DIY projects, this session covers measuring voltage, testing resistance, and ensuring electrical safety. Participants will gain practical experience and leave with the knowledge to use a multimeter effectively in their projects.

title: Balancing Cognitive Loads for Integrating Fabrication: A Framework for K–12 STEM Lesson Design

speakers.title: Adam devitt
speakers.description: Adam Devitt Ph.D. is an Associate Professor of Science Education at California State University, Stanislaus, and the Director of Maker Pedagogy and K-12 Outreach in the Warrior Fab Lab and Director of the Early Childhood Education Credential Program.

extendedProps.description: The workgroup will introduce a quadrant framework to balance cognitive loads in K–12 STEM lessons involving digital fabrication (DF) tools. The session will highlight how to design lessons that balance technological and content demands, supporting student learning. By using the quadrant tool, educators can create tiered activities, integrate TPACK, and foster differentiated instruction. This framework aims to optimize lesson planning, ensuring both technology and content are aligned effectively.

title: Fab Community & Rural Innovation bridging the digital divide

speakers.title: Santi Fuentemilla
speakers.description: Santiago Fuentemilla Garriga holds a Master’s in Architecture and a postgraduate specialization in digital fabrication from Fab Academy, boasting over 15 years of international experience at studios like OPR, FHAUS, and OPERA. Since 2013, he has led the Future Learning Unit at IAAC – Fab Lab BCN, pioneering innovative educational models. He directs the Fab Academy and Fabricademy in Barcelona, executive board of the Fab Learning Academy board, and teaches in advanced design master’s programs MDEF and MDDI, shaping future digital innovators.

extendedProps.description: This open session brings together Fab Labs, makerspaces, organizations, and stakeholders tackling the digital divide in rural areas through community-driven innovation. Rooted in the RURACTIVE approach, we will explore practical strategies for digital inclusion, local capacity-building, and fostering rural innovation ecosystems. Participants will share experiences, discuss best practices, and collaborate on actionable solutions to empower rural communities through digital tools and maker culture

title: Makerspaces and Third Spaces: Can We Be Open While Taking a Stand?

speakers.title: Daniela Novac
speakers.description: I am a researcher and educator in computer science with a strong interest in makerspaces, third spaces, and community-driven innovation. As an active member of the maker community, I explore the intersections of technology, education, and social impact. My current focus is on fostering inclusive, collaborative environments while navigating the challenges of openness, political positioning, and conflict resolution in shared spaces.

extendedProps.description: Makerspaces prioritize openness and inclusivity, but what happens when these ideals clash with ideology? Can a space stay open while rejecting the far right or embracing feminism? Is producing materials for protests against government institutions political, and how should spaces handle such activism? This discussion examines balancing openness, political positioning, and conflict prevention — creating a welcoming space while upholding values and setting boundaries against harmful ideologies.

title: The power of small actions: how digital fabrication workshops open up career paths

speakers.title: Maciej Naskret
speakers.description: Short CV: Expert of STREAM and PBL methodologies. To date, he has led the incubation of more than 100 individual and group projects taking care of the ideation, design, needs research, prototyping, manufacturing and testing phases. He has collaborated with entities such as: Copernicus Science Center, Sector 3.0, Maritime School, SWPS, City of Gdynia, FabLab Trójmiasto, FabLab Małopolska, FabLab Łódź, Fablab powered by Orange, PAKT Olsztyn, PAKT Wrocław, FRSI, PAH, City of Warsaw, Museum of Józef Piłsudski. Trained as an architect and interior designer. Instructor in 3D modeling, 3D printing, C

extendedProps.description: Can one afternoon change our future? What impact can our chosen hobby afternoon activities have on our career path? Can the marriage of new technologies and crafts be the impetus to change our lives? Listen to the story of how democratic access to new technologies and free professional training can empower not only the digitally and professionally excluded, but each of us, to discover our potential. The change that begins with a workshop of a few hours or a course of a few weeks can be the begin

title: Making Safer Spaces: Co-Creating Codes of Conduct in Makerspaces

speakers.title: Katrin Nora Kober
speakers.description: Katrin Nora Kober (she/her) is a designer, maker, and educator pursuing a PhD in Human-Computer Interaction at the University of Salzburg. Her research explores often-overlooked agencies and activities in technology-driven making. She is part of a six-year collaborative research project focused on how more diverse groups can access and benefit from making.

extendedProps.description: This workshop explores how to co-create codes of conduct through participatory, diversity-centered approaches tailored to the respective requirements of indivudal makerspaces and their members. Together, we’ll examine how to embed such codes into the varied formats, activities, and individual trajectories that define our communities. Participants are invited to share their challenges and strategies, working toward inclusive, actionable frameworks that reflect the dynamic realities of making.

title: FabLabs take on the challenges of the intermediate vehicle sector! #eXtremeDefi

speakers.title: Amelie Tehel
speakers.description: Amélie Tehel, Doctor in information and communication sciences (Sciences Po Rennes). I'm now the CEO of RFFLabs. (French FabLabs Network)

extendedProps.description: Makers and Fablabs participate in the development of new, lighter, simpler and cheaper vehicles ! R&D - Prototyping - Assembling - Repairing - Training ... what could be the place of the Makers Network in this future market.

title: Bridging Islands: An Innovative 3D-Printed Lingerie Collection Rooted in Jamaican Heritage

speakers.title: Dr Kadian Gosler
speakers.description:
Dr. Kadian A. Gosler is redefining lingerie at the crossroads of design, technology, and
wearer experience. A pioneering design researcher, earning her PhD from University
of the Arts London, where she developed an experience-centred design process for Bra
Wearables, a subset of smart bras. She founded KDN Research & Design Atelier,
a future-focused lab advancing bra wearables, 3D-printed lingerie, and smart design.
Passionate about inclusivity and innovation, Dr. Gosler challenges traditional lingerie
discourse, ensuring intimate apparel is shaped by wearer needs and cutting-edge technology.

extendedProps.description:
Discover how Jamaican heritage—lacebark cloth, flowers, Taino symbols—informs 3D-printed lingerie in this vibrant talk. Gain insights into a new design process layering craftsmanship and parametric designs, blending rigid/flexible filaments, and leaning into tacit knowledge.
Through lived-experience and material experimentation, I’ll show how ancestral knowledge bridges gaps between tradition and futurism. Leave empowered to reimagine your heritage as a catalyst for radical, inclusive design.

title: AI, IoT, hands-on learning, and Unihiker K10 hardware

speakers.title: Rockets Xia
speakers.description: rockets, co-founder of Mushroom Cloud Fablab, and a senior engineer at DFRobot, is frequently active in both domestic and international maker communities. rockets actively endeavors to facilitate the implementation and dissemination of cutting-edge technologies such as artificial intelligence and the Internet of Things in the realm of makers and maker education.

extendedProps.description:
Empower educators and makers to integrate AI/IoT into STEM curricula.
Lower barriers for beginners with plug-and-play hardware and visual coding tools.

title: Writing Your First (or Next) Erasmus+ Application

speakers.title: Alexandre Rousselet
speakers.description: Makerspaces explorer since 2015. I co-created with some Erasmus student friends, VULCA Network and together we pushed the door of more than 1000 FabLabs, Makerspaces and Hackerspaces in Europe. Now I work full time for RFFLabs (The French FabLabs Network) and I'm a volunteer supporting the development of my local makerspace "Mountain Makers" located in my village in middle France.

extendedProps.description: It’s time to put theory into practice! This hands-on session helps participants take their project idea and start drafting a real Erasmus+ application. Using an official application template, attendees will work through key sections, ensuring they leave with a solid draft and a follow-up plan for submission.

title: Rural areas are the place of radical change

speakers.title: Leopold Zyka
speakers.description:
DNA: X2b, R1a1a
Born as digital immigrant in Vienna, Austria, passionate Software Architect and Open Source Evangelist dedicated to fostering innovation through technology and sustainability.
🚀 Founder of OpenLandLAB: A makerspace and fab lab in rural Austria. As moderator of FAB Region Südburgenland, I champion the philosophy of "produce locally, network globally."
🌱 Visionary Projects: A multidisciplinary studio surrounded by TinyLabs for mushrooms, compost, algae. KInsecta, EdgeAI, Connecting The Things Network to the WOOD WIDE WEB

extendedProps.description: The keynote for the working groups “Fab Cities & Fab Regions in Dialogue for Sustainable Transitions” and “Design Revolution Now!” will present "Wakeup Fabregion Südburgenland," a project positioning Austria as a leader in regenerative economies and local production. Austria's proximity to Prague, home to open-source pioneer Prusa Research, offers unique opportunities for knowledge exchange and global collaboration. The vision: locally produced, globally networked solutions.

title: Hands-on: Japanese Wood Print × Laser Cutter

speakers.title: Natsuka Kamei
speakers.description:
My name is Natsuka Kamei, a Japanese maker who loves making things in both digital/analog ways. 
I am a graduate of Fab Academy 2021 and an instructor of Fab Academy 2022 at Fablab Kannai Japan. 
I worked as a technical staff of the makerspace in university, helping students to realize their ideas/creativity, and offer the events/workshops.
In 2023, I moved to Germany for family matters and started to explore the local Fab community here. Also I enjoy fabrication at my tiny home lab!
https://fabacademy.org/2021/labs/kannai/students/natsuka-kamei/index.html

extendedProps.description:
In this workshop we'll make Japanese wood prints with a laser cutter.
Ukiyo-e is a Japanese wood printing method which has a history of 400 years. Usually It's made by several craftsmen who are specialized in drawing, engraving, and printing by hand. It takes enormous amounts of time.
In this workshop we'll challenge this traditional method using technologies. Participants can learn the image process with Inkscape, engraving, and printing technique.

title: Ethafa Steammians Kit: Code, Make, Generate

speakers.title: Leyla Tawfik
speakers.description: I'm Leyla Tawfik, a cross-disciplinary problem-solver based in Qatar. In 2017, I’ve co-founded Ethafa, an innovative ed-tech project aimed at transforming education through interactive learning experiences. Currently, I’m a Senior EdTech Designer at Ibtechar and continue to contribute to the growth of Ethafa as a Research Fellow at VCUarts Qatar as part of the research lab Boost Lab.

extendedProps.description: In this 10-minute talk, I’ll share insights into the design process behind the latest iteration of our kit, now featuring AI. I’ll explore how the project has evolved over the years and highlight key learnings and takeaways from the journey.

title: E-textiles and the FabriXIAO

speakers.title: Fabricademy Textile and Technology Academy
speakers.description: Our mission is to (re)shape and (re)define the implications and applications of technology in the textile and clothing industry, from the fashion sector to the upcoming wearable market. The program offers a broad overview of the state of the art of the current industry and builds on “learning by doing” methodologies, tackling themes like personal fabrication, distributed manufacturing, industry 4.0, wearable technology, biofabrication, assistive technologies and sustainability.

extendedProps.description: Intelligence to our everyday clothing is getting closer and closer with the evolution of flexible circuits and conductive textiles. In this workshop we will learn about electronic textiles, soft DIY sensors, and program the FABRIXIAO, a flexible sewable PCB board developed by Adrian Torres using the Seeed Studio microcontrollers for sewable circuits.

title: FAB STORMING: connecting the missing dots between Industries and Fab Labs.

speakers.title: MARIANA TEIXEIRA
speakers.description: Mariana Teixeira is an education leader with 14+ years of experience. At Casa Firjan, she led the Education Lab, driving digital transformation through strategy, prototyping, and tech-integrated learning, bridging education and innovation.

extendedProps.description: FAB STORMING is a strategic workshop designed to connect industries and Fab Labs, driving innovation through digital fabrication. Using a structured brainstorming process and a customized card game, participants identify seven key opportunities to integrate digital fabrication into industrial processes. The workshop is ideal for professionals, educators, and makers seeking collaboration and new pathways for product development.

title: Goodbye Brno Party!

speakers.title: HRTL
speakers.description: HRTL is a force of nature on modular synthesisers, his chosen weapon of over a decade, performing unique and electrifying improvised sets that fuse breakbeat rhythms with liquefying acid and synths sliced from the utmost peaks of euphoria. Performing in cities from Riga to Ankara, and major clubbing capitals such as Berlin, London, Amsterdam and Vienna, the Prague-based musician brings a decade of experience to bear in exhilarating live performances, held in his inimitable style from the very centre of the dancefloor. Through releasing numerous records through European labels like Nona Records, Skylax, Wolfskuil and his own Bükko Tapes imprint, HRTL’s sound has grown beyond the club, setting the scene for live electro-acoustic adventures with highly-regarded Czech jazz and contemporary classical musicians: while his first love is self-evidently the dancefloor, HRTL’s skill and adventurous spirit brings him to ever-expanding musical horizons.

speakers.title: 1flfsoap
speakers.description: Live electronic duo, 100% improvisation, 200% rave! Live drums, guitar & synths.

extendedProps.summary: Halfway through the event, let’s get groovy in Brno’s legendary club!
extendedProps.description:
It’s the midpoint of FAB25 – time to hit the dance floor and celebrate everything we’ve shared so far. The Goodbye Brno Party takes over one of the city’s most iconic clubs for a night of music, movement, networking and maker magic.

Whether you're dancing non-stop, grabbing a drink with new friends, or just soaking in the vibe, this is your chance to unwind and send Brno off in style. It’s not quite goodbye yet – but it’s for sure a night to remember.

Bring your energy. Bring your community spirit.
And bring your groove – FAB25 style.

title: The Bubonic Plague, Falling Roof Beams, Dead Fish and Fablabs; system dynamics to the fablabs impact

speakers.title: Pieter van der Hijden
speakers.description:
Pieter van der Hijden (Netherlands) studied Applied Physics, worked in R&D in gaming/simulation and then became a management consultant. He did projects on gaming, making and learning; also abroad and especially in Suriname where his wife Bidia comes from. 
In Suriname Pieter implemented the first Caribbean fablab. Now, he is a volunteer of ZapLab AT Villa Zapakara there, currently in STEAM curriculum development for outbound activities in the interior. Within the fablab network Pieter coordinates the Global Working Group "Fablabs and the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)".

extendedProps.description:
We walk through the “Borneo Mystery” and see how systems thinking can help analyze complex problems. The diagram language transcends different disciplines, focuses on processes, vicious circles and feedback loops. This systems thinking model can then be quantified in a mathematical model and simulated with a computer (system dynamics). Gaming/simulation adds humans. We show examples for "industrial dynamics" and "urban dynamics" and for the world as a whole. What could Fablabs/SDGs do with this?
More info here: https://bit.ly/fab25-sdgs

title: Designing Hardware at the Speed of Thought

speakers.title: Osama Atwi
speakers.description:
Osama Atwi is the founder of Kyrall, a Munich-based startup that develops intelligent software to automate the design of 3D functional parts. Kyrall is creating a hardware design agent that streamlines and accelerates the design process, enabling engineers and manufacturers to go from concept to manufacturable geometry in seconds.



Before founding Kyrall, Osama worked on building drones; particularly electric Vertical Take-Off and Landing (eVTOL) aircraft, designed to transport small payloads over long distances.

extendedProps.description: In this talk, we’ll share what we’re building to automate the design of 3D functional parts, and why this marks a turning point in how we engineer physical products. The aim is to offer a glimpse into what’s now possible with AI-driven design tools, and what’s coming next as artificial intelligence becomes deeply integrated into engineering workflows.

title: Open-Source Braille Trainer: A replicable tool for inclusive education

speakers.title: Marcela Gonzales Arias
speakers.description: Hello, I’m an industrial designer from Peru with experience in both industry and education. I teach product design and digital fabrication. For my master’s thesis, I focused my research on combining my passion for making with the challenge of improving accessibility, developing tools that support access to education for groups that often lack resources or specialized tools. I created a Braille literacy trainer designed to be produced in Fab Labs, aiming to support public schools and promote more equitable access to Braille literacy.

extendedProps.description: This talk shares the development of a low-cost, open-source Braille trainer designed to be fabricated entirely in Fab Labs. Created as part of my master’s thesis, the device aims to support blind students in public schools through a replicable, affordable solution (each unit can be made for under $100). The session explores the path from prototype to broader adoption and how Fab Labs can serve as production nodes for real-world educational impact.

title: Blow & Glow: Neon Tube Basics

extendedProps.summary: Get hands-on with fire and glass – and discover how glowing neon signs are made.
extendedProps.description:
Ever wondered how neon signs get their glow? Step into the heat and see it up close. In this fiery and fascinating workshop, expert craftsmen will guide you through the basics of working with hot glass tubing – the foundation of classic neon lights.

– Watch live demos of bending neon glass over flame
– Try shaping glass yourself under close supervision
– Learn what gives neon its iconic glow

No experience needed – just bring steady hands and a sense of wonder. Whether you're into signage, lighting, or just love making things glow, this is a rare chance to get hands-on with neon at FAB25.

title: Lathe Your Wooden Pen

extendedProps.summary: Turn raw wood into your own custom pen – smooth, personal, and made by you.
extendedProps.description:
Step up to the lathe and shape your own custom wooden pen. In this hands-on workshop, you’ll turn a block of wood into a finished writing tool – unique, personal, and satisfying to make.

– Learn the basics of woodturning on a lathe
– Shape and smooth your wooden pen body
– Assemble it with a pre-made pen mechanism
– Optionally add a custom engraving with a laser cutter

No experience needed – just bring your curiosity and a steady hand. Our instructors will guide you through every step, and in just 20 minutes, you’ll walk away with a pen that’s truly yours.

title: First Stitches: Basics of Sewing 101

extendedProps.summary: Learn how to thread a machine, sew a straight line, and make your very first textile creation.
extendedProps.description:
Curious about sewing but never touched a machine before? This is your moment. Join us for a relaxed, hands-on intro where you'll learn the basics – from threading the sewing machine to making your first real stitches.

– Learn how to handle a sewing machine with confidence
– Practice straight stitches, curves, and corners
– Make a small, useful item you can take home

No experience needed, just bring your curiosity. Our instructors will help you stitch with confidence – whether you want to mend your gear or start your next maker project in textiles. This is where it all begins.

title: Hackster IoT Workshop: A Hands-On Journey with Nordic Thingy91X DAY 1

speakers.title: Jinger Zeng
speakers.bio: Hackster.io, Head of Applied Innovation Lab
speakers.description: Jinger is a tech entrepreneur, currently leading Applied Innovation Lab initiatives at Hackster.io, an international online community for engineers and innovators. She is a global evangelist for open-source technology and open science, and an alumni of IaaC's Master in Distributed Design program.

speakers.title: Sachin Soni
speakers.description: I'm a passionate Maker and full-time content creator, exploring electronics and IoT to build innovative and practical projects. With over 147K+ subscribers and 550+ videos on my YouTube channel, I've dedicated myself to simplifying technology through engaging tutorials. Our channel proudly won the Best YouTube Channel in Technology & Automation (2023) award. My mission is simple: inspire creativity, build new things, and empower others to learn and innovate in the world of electronics and IoT.

speakers.title: Salman Faris
speakers.bio: Co-founder MakerGram and Customer Success at Screenly
speakers.description:
Salman Faris is a passionate maker, Fab Academy graduate, and dedicated community builder based in Kerala. He leads MakerGram, a vibrant maker community that fosters collaboration, learning, and innovation. Salman has previously served as a Fab Academy instructor at FabLab Kerala and ChaiHio Makerspace. 

He currently works full-time at Screenly as a Customer Success professional and part of the global maker ecosystem as a member of the Hackster Pro, Seeed Studio Ranger team and the Edge Impulse Experts team, with a strong focus on TinyML & IoT.

extendedProps.description: Join Hackster’s IoT workshop to get a hands-on IoT prototyping experience with the Nordic Thingy:91 X. This device supports LTE-M, NB-IoT, GNSS and NR+, and certified for global operation. It is an ideal device for rapidly developing any cellular IoT concept, and is especially suited for asset tracking applications. This board is packed with real-world capabilities like sensors, GNSS, BLE, and ultra-low power performance, making it a perfect playground for both beginners and advanced makers. The workshop is a 2 part series, where we will introduce the hardware in Brno workshop on July 7, then participants will have the full week to play and experiment with it especially during travel, then have a final workshop in Prague on July 11.

title: Breaking Barriers, Creating Possibilities: The Impact of STEM Education in Rural Areas

speakers.title: Jose Alexis Del Aguila Ramos
speakers.description: Mechatronics Engineer specialized in Biomedicine and Robotics, with a solid track record in the development and management of technological projects and applied research. As the founder of my own startup, I have led the design and implementation of innovative solutions that integrate various technological disciplines to solve challenges in the healthcare sector. Passionate about generating innovative solutions through the integration of diverse disciplines, with a focus on improving processes and creating disruptive technologies. Committed to education and knowledge transfer, always oriented t

extendedProps.description:
In the talk, "Breaking Barriers, Creating Possibilities: The Impact of STEM Education in Rural Areas," we will explore how science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) education can transform rural communities, opening new development opportunities and bridging gaps in access to knowledge.
We will explore real-life cases, successful initiatives, and innovative strategies that have enabled young people in rural contexts to develop key skills for the 21st century. Attendees will unders

title: How to get called to HR 8 times and still work in a makerspace

speakers.title: Tex Kang
speakers.description:
Current manager of University of Pennsylvania's library and makerspace 
Former manager of Fab Lab Seoul and Bio Lab Seoul 
Organizer of the Philadelphia Maker Faire 
Burning Man designer and fire performer 
Has two dogs named 'light' and 'speed' in Tolkien elvish

extendedProps.description: Makers are naturally curious and individuals. We are adventurous and prototype things everyday, but sometimes that gets us into trouble. I want to emphasize that makers are like scientists where we need to take responsibility for our projects and always need to be mindful, and to consider the short and long term repercussions of our actions. So using the experience from 8 visits to HR (so far) in the last 2 years, I hope participants learn from my 8 mistakes makers make.

title: DTecla: a Research-Backed Maker Model Tranforming STEAM Education in Primary Schools

speakers.title: Julia Leirado
speakers.description: Julia Leirado is an architect based in Barcelona interested in educational innovation and how new technologies and digital fabrication tools can intersect with traditional education curriculum and their impact on improving the learning experience. She holds a Graduate Diploma in Architecture from the Polytechnic University of Catalonia (ETSAB), a master’s in Project Management (EAE), and a Fab Academy diploma in digital fabrication from the Fab Lab Network.

extendedProps.description: DTecla is a research-based, maker-driven project transforming STEAM education in primary schools in vulnerable environments. Over three years, teachers and students engage in hands-on learning, using a maker approach to integrate STEAM concepts. The project emphasizes teacher training, continuous research, and community-building, ensuring sustainable growth and long-term impact in classrooms beyond the project’s duration.

title: Tradition meets technology: how Fab Labs unite rural knowledge and urban innovation

speakers.title: Martin Gutmann
speakers.description:
Martin Gutmann – Maker, Innovator, Educator 🚀
Architect turned digital fabrication expert, Martin co-founded FarmLab, a rural innovation hub blending tradition with tech. From managing Fab Labs in the Barcelona area to crafting 3D-printed concrete at Baumit in Austria, he’s all about hands-on, high-tech making. A Fab Academy grad and engineering management MSc, he’s worked on EU projects like ROMI and taught digital fabrication. Passionate about sustainable design, robotics, and prototyping, he thrives where creativity meets fabrication.

extendedProps.description: How can makerspaces foster a meaningful exchange between rural and urban contexts? This talk explores how digital fabrication and traditional knowledge can complement each other, offering new perspectives on sustainability. Through FarmLab’s experience and global examples, attendees will discover how an open maker’s approach can bridge gaps, inspire collaboration, and drive innovation across diverse communities.

title: Smart Citizen Meets Meshtastic (A Hackathon) - DAY 3

speakers.title: Óscar González
speakers.description: Óscar González is an Industrial Engineer with expertise in data analysis, testing, and calibration through his experience in automotive and sensor development. He is the Sense Making Lead at Fab Lab Barcelona doing research and development on the Smart Citizen project. Óscar has worked as a researcher on various projects, spanning the development and implementation of open source technologies and low-cost sensors, including their application in citizen science initiatives, as tools for environmental and health assessment, and the interoperability of data across research infrastructures.

extendedProps.description: We propose a hackathon focused on air quality monitoring, using decentralised IoT solutions, potentially creating the first building block of a new Smart Citizen Kit. We want to move beyond traditional frameworks towards emancipatory technologies like Meshtastic, helping Fab Labs to build resilient, community-driven sensing networks. Building on over a decade of work, Smart Citizen is now a global network of technologists, scientists, and community leaders.

title: Pitch Ready: AI-Assisted Development for Media Projects

speakers.title: Monika Jane (MJ) Browatzki (Watz)
speakers.description: MJ Watz is an independent filmmaker, educator, and AI content evaluator passionate about blending storytelling with emerging technologies. They hold a BA in Film Production from UCLA and have written and directed projects across genres, including their feature Leash Leader, music videos, and sketch comedy shorts including for Funny or Die. MJ has taught filmmaking to learners of all ages and worked at Outlier AI, focusing on creative uses of generative tools. Their workshops foster collaboration using open-source AI.

extendedProps.description: In this hands-on workshop, participants will explore using open-source AI tools to build a pitch deck for a media project. Attendees will experiment with AI for collaborative ideation, generating reference images, and creating teaser videos — while striving to maintain their unique voices. Participants will then practice presenting their project pitch and receive group feedback. Ethical considerations will be woven throughout, encouraging a mindful and responsible use of AI. By the end of the session, participants will have gained practical experience in integrating AI into their creative workflow.

title: Boost Your Creativity with Simple Techniques with ROBOTA

speakers.title: Jitka Honsová
speakers.description: manager of ROBOTA public makerspace led by Tomas Bata University in Zlin, non-formal educator, member of the Makerspaces association (CZ), formerly theater manager and manager of UPPER - Center of creative industries and entrepreneurship, maker lover, DIY and upcycling enthusiast

extendedProps.description: Unlock the creative potential of your community! In this hands-on workshop, we’ll explore simple yet effective techniques to spark creativity in both kids and adults. You’ll experience our interactive approach, gain practical insights, and leave with fresh ideas for your FabLab or makerspace. Plus, take home a custom-printed tote bag as a reminder of your creative journey. Join us for inspiration, networking, and effective ways to engage your audience!

title: Sew:bit: Expanding Micro:bit for E-Textiles and Creative Electronics in the Classroom

speakers.title: Julia Leirado
speakers.description: Julia Leirado is an architect based in Barcelona interested in educational innovation and how new technologies and digital fabrication tools can intersect with traditional education curriculum and their impact on improving the learning experience. She holds a Graduate Diploma in Architecture from the Polytechnic University of Catalonia (ETSAB), a master’s in Project Management (EAE), and a Fab Academy diploma in digital fabrication from the Fab Lab Network.

extendedProps.description: This hands-on workshop introduces educators to Sew:bit, an Open Hardware expansion board developed in Fab Lab Barcelona for the micro:bit that enables them to explore e-textiles and wearable electronics in their classrooms. Participants will learn how to move beyond traditional robotics projects to engage students—especially those interested in arts and design—through an accessible, open-source tool. The workshop aims to encourage all students to engage with technology in new and exciting ways.

title: What is the most needed training for makerspaces in Africa? probably not what you expect

speakers.title: Enrico Bassi
speakers.description: Enrico Bassi is a designer, maker, professor, and innovation consultant. He managed Italy's first Fab Lab, Fablab Italia, FabLab Torino, supported by Officine Arduino, and then Opendot. He has been instructor and global evaluator in fab academy. He co-founded Fabcare.network and careables.org, two international projects on co-design open solutions with people with disabilities. He has taught digital fabrication and design at universities in Italy, Germany, Poland, and Switzerland and has participated in projects and events in over 35 countries, from the USA to Bhutan, from Djibouti to Kenya.

extendedProps.description: What is needed to transform FabLabs and Makerspaces in Africa into production centers? What does it take to go from making prototypes to products? The most obvious answer might not be what was really needed. A journey through four different countries and eight makerspaces to share tips and trick on how to design, realize and sell products while improving gender equality and social impact.

title: Fabricating Resilience: Ukraine’s Maker Movement at War

speakers.title: Kostyantyn Leonenko
speakers.description: KL has been building FabLabs in Ukraine since 2012, and most recently led the humanitarian mobile makerspace project TOLOCAR supporting communities affected by the russian invasion.

extendedProps.description: The Ukrainian fab lab network was torn apart by war. Some spaces were occupied and turned into prisons, others forced underground. Some were destroyed; others fell silent when their leaders were drafted. Yet through loss and devastation, labs persist—building bomb shelters, teaching STEM to kids, preserving heritage, and refusing to stop making.

title: Re/upskilling at FabLabs for a Resilient Labour Market

speakers.title: Martina Michlovská
speakers.description: I'm Martina, a passionate designer and maker at FabLab Brno, where I lead student education and help young innovators bring their ideas to life. With my team, I run the FabLab University truck, bringing hands-on tech experiences on the road. I also support high school students through competitions that spark creativity and skill-building. As a former design student and maker at heart, I love experimenting—though I do have a knack for breaking milling cutters along the way! 😄🚀

extendedProps.description: Join this working group to explore how the FabLab network can drive re/upskilling in a fast-changing job market. We’ll present the EU-funded DiSTT project, including pilot results with different target groups and the adaptable Digital Skills Transformation Toolkit – ready for any FabLab or makerspace. Discover its potential and share your feedback! More info about the project here: https://www.interreg-central.eu/projects/distt/?tab=home

title: Advanced Strategies for Clay 3D Printing

speakers.title: Jan Vítek
speakers.description: I am PhD. student at Brno University of Technology focus on both parametric and generative design for technical as well as artistic applications. My current work explores generative strategies in the context of ceramic 3D printing, combining computational design methods with hands-on material experimentation.

extendedProps.description: This hands-on workshop offers a complete introduction to ceramic 3D printing using parametric design. Participants will work with a pre-made Grasshopper script to customize a design and generate printable G-code. Each customized object will then be printed from clay, giving participants the opportunity to experience the full workflow — from digital design to physical fabrication. The process includes an explanation of the technology, printing setup, and advanced clay printing strategies.

title: Bio Break
What does it really mean to learn, in the digital age?

speakers.title: Camila Luna
speakers.description: Camila Luna is a multidisciplinary designer and educator focused on creative learning experiences. She is the co-founder of MoonMakers, an educational non-profit organization that empowers youth through technology and creativity. Camila has led digital fabrication, design, and technology workshops across Mexico, the United States, collaborating with institutions such as Arduino, Make Community, Science Friday, Fab Foundation and  Micro:bti Education Foundation. Her work bridges art, technology, and community, inspiring girls and young people to explore their potential through hands-on projects. She has driving initiatives that combine participatory design, storytelling, and STEAM education to transform learning from the local to the global level.

extendedProps.summary: How Fab Academy made me rethink how we learn and create today

title: Keynotes, panels, talks (Digital Age Competences)

speakers.title: Dale Dougherty
speakers.description: Dale Dougherty believes that all of us are makers, and he is a champion of the people and projects that form the Maker Movement. He founded Make: magazine in 2005 and first used the term "makers" to describe people who enjoyed hands-on work and play. Often called the Father of the Maker Movement, he launched Maker Faire in the San Francisco Bay Area in 2006, an event that has since spread worldwide, regularly showcasing and celebrating maker projects. He is the President of Make Community, LLC, which publishes Make: magazine and licenses Maker Faires globally.

speakers.title: Tomáš Mejzlík
speakers.bio: Managing director FabLab Brno and VIDA!
speakers.description:
Tomáš is a leader in digital fabrication, STEM education, and innovation. In 2016, he began his career at JIC in Brno, where he led the setup of the first FabLab in Czechia. Under his management, FabLab Brno launched as a 24/7 makerspace accessible to the public, businesses, and students. He spearheaded successful projects like FabLab University, a mobile lab serving thousands of students annually, and managed international collaborations, including the H2020 FabLabia. He was also a key initiator in bringing FAB25 to Czechia.

In 2025, he also became the director of VIDA! science center, with a vision to create a versatile STEM education hub that integrates making and technology popularization within its 6,000+ m² space. By 2026, VIDA! is expected to merge with FabLab Brno, combining their strengths to deliver more FUN, EDU and MAKE pleasures to hunderds of thousands people annualy.

speakers.title: Nikodem Bartnik
speakers.description:
Nikodem is a Polish maker and content creator behind two YouTube channels: one in his own name and the other called Proste Części. His work focuses on robotics, DIY projects, electronics, and programming. Notable projects include the IndyMill CNC Machine, Ludwik Drone, ChatGPT Robot, and Dremel CNC.
Most of his creations are shared as open-source through the Indystry.cc platform. His projects have earned awards in national and international contests, including Instructables, and in 2022, together with his friends won the EBEC (European BEST Engineering Competition). Nikodem holds a master’s degree in Data Science from Silesian University of Technology, where his thesis, StarTrckr – 3-axis night sky tracking device, took first place in the TRUMPF national competition for best diploma thesis.

speakers.title: Camila Luna
speakers.description: Camila Luna is a multidisciplinary designer and educator focused on creative learning experiences. She is the co-founder of MoonMakers, an educational non-profit organization that empowers youth through technology and creativity. Camila has led digital fabrication, design, and technology workshops across Mexico, the United States, collaborating with institutions such as Arduino, Make Community, Science Friday, Fab Foundation and  Micro:bti Education Foundation. Her work bridges art, technology, and community, inspiring girls and young people to explore their potential through hands-on projects. She has driving initiatives that combine participatory design, storytelling, and STEAM education to transform learning from the local to the global level.

speakers.title: David Škaroupka
speakers.bio: Assistant professor at Brno University of Technology
speakers.description: David is an assistant professor at Brno University of Technology, CEO of 3Deposition, and co-founder of strojLAB. He’s an innovator, educator, and designer passionate about large-format additive fabrication, machine design, and bringing new tech to life. Dedicated to connecting academia with industry, he turns creative ideas into real-world impact. David has led or contributed to numerous national and international grant projects and realizations, including TOVA by IAAC and the first 3DCP Parkour circle in Prague by 3Deposition. When not in the lab, he’s likely renovating his family house, riding his motorbike, or chasing down music.

speakers.title: Karolina Guzek
speakers.description: Since 2017 Karolina has been involved in the development of FabLabs in Poland, designing and coordinating innovative social projects in the field of STEAM, tailoring them to the needs of various beneficiaries, including marginalized groups. Head of FabLab Warsaw, member of the board of the Robisz.to Association. Equality and women’s rights advocate. She holds a master's degree in law with a specialisation in environmental protection and human rights, and has a background in design. She is included in the list of 100 persons of merit for the development of digital skills in Poland in 2024.

extendedProps.summary: How can FabLabs and STEM education help us stay on track with the everyday changing digital era?
extendedProps.description:
In today’s fast-changing digital world, FabLabs can play a key role in equipping people with the skills and mindsets needed to keep up – and lead the way. This Digital Age Competences track looks at how FabLabs support STEM education, foster creativity, and empower individuals to master digital tools and technologies while building resiliency.

As digital fabrication machines are becoming a standard and schools are integrating 3D printers, laser cutters, and coding into classrooms, the next challenge is clear: how can FabLabs continue to shape the future of learning and innovation?

FabLabs also have the potential to become powerful educational hubs – places where learning happens through experimentation in a safe, open, and failure-friendly environment. Here, curiosity is encouraged, mistakes are part of the process, and learning is hands-on and inclusive. We’ll also hand over the stage to the next generation. It’s time to hear directly from young, talented makers and innovators who are already redefining what’s possible.

Martin currently serves on various academic and automotive groups and boards. He is the holder of several awards.

title: Fab Fun-o-graph (Phonograph)

speakers.title: ARISTARCO CORTES
speakers.description: Aristarco is an industrial engineer with MBA studies and aspiring polymath. He has over 30 years of working experience in different sectors such as industry, government and universities. He is an entrepreneur, in love with fabrication processes, Fab Academy alumni and hardcore Maker. After being director of the biggest makerspace in the world, the Innovation, Design and Technology Institute, IDIT, at the Jesuit Iberoamericana University of Puebla, Mexico, today he develops entrepreneurial tech, social and innovation projects at IDIT.

extendedProps.description: Reproduce Edison's invention with Fab Lab's tools and understand the principles of recording sound on a solid material.

title: Curriculum-Connected Making: Bridging Fab Labs and Classroom Learning

speakers.title: Liz Whitewolf
speakers.description: Liz Whitewolf is the founder of eduFAB, a global organization advancing equity in STEM through digital fabrication. She leads the annual International Fab Educators Summit and supports global STEM educators. A former classroom teacher, she co-founded the Fab Learning Academy and serves as a Global Instructor and executive board member. Liz earned her Ed.D. in STEM Education from the University of Pittsburgh, focusing on K–12 makerspace integration, and is a 2016 FAB Academy graduate.

speakers.title: Iván Sánchez Milara
speakers.description: I am university teacher at University of Oulu (Finland) and instructor at Fab Lab Oulu. I graduated from Fab Academy in 2017. In the last years I have developed different education programs, courses, workshops and other educational activities aiming to develop professional competence of teachers in STEAM and digital fabrication. I am currently part of the executive committee of the Fab Learning Academy.

speakers.title: Santi Fuentemilla
speakers.description: Santiago Fuentemilla Garriga holds a Master’s in Architecture and a postgraduate specialization in digital fabrication from Fab Academy, boasting over 15 years of international experience at studios like OPR, FHAUS, and OPERA. Since 2013, he has led the Future Learning Unit at IAAC – Fab Lab BCN, pioneering innovative educational models. He directs the Fab Academy and Fabricademy in Barcelona, executive board of the Fab Learning Academy board, and teaches in advanced design master’s programs MDEF and MDDI, shaping future digital innovators.

extendedProps.description: Explore how digital fabrication and making can be integrated into school curricula to support student learning. Using examples from the first graduates of Fab Learning Academy, this session will guide participants through a human-centered design process to develop fab projects aligned with classroom learning objectives. Ideal for those working with educators to bring meaningful, hands-on experiences into K–12 learning environments.

We'll highlight main learnings from its first official edition and the collaborative process that shaped its curriculum and structure.

After lunch, it’s time to shift gears and spaces. Let's move from the main venue to the workshop venue at University, just a short and easy 15-minute walk away. Whether you’re in the mood for something fresh or hearty, we’ve got you covered. Don’t forget to top it off with a coffee, a free water refill, or a chilled drink to beat the summer heat. extendedProps.summary: Let’s celebrate the accomplishments of the students in this year’s Academany educational programs.

The Academany Graduation Ceremony is a special moment to honour the dedication and achievements of students from this year’s programs – including Fab Academy, Fabricademy, MDDI and Fab Learning Academy.

Join us to celebrate alongside graduates and their closest supporters as they receive their diplomas and present the final projects they’ve worked so hard to bring to life. These projects represent the future of digital fabrication, biodesign, inclusivity, and innovation within the FabLab network.

Whether you're already part of the community or simply curious, this is a perfect opportunity to get inspired and see what’s possible through Academany’s hands-on, global learning programs. Who knows – maybe next year, it’ll be you on that stage.

title: Spin & Cut: CNC Essentials

extendedProps.summary: Get hands-on with CNC basics and contribute to a one-of-a-kind maker art piece.
extendedProps.description:
Learn how to prepare your design for cutting, tweak the key parameters, and transfer your file to the Mekanika CNC machine – all in a quick, beginner-friendly session.

No prior CNC experience? No problem. Our instructors from Mekanika and the FAB25 team will walk you through every step:
– How to set up your cutting data in minutes
– What parameters really matter (and why)
– How to safely and easily run your file on the machine

Once your custom piece is milled, you’ll add it to a growing collaborative artwork – a living, evolving piece made by the FAB25 community.

Fast, friendly, and hands-on – this is the perfect way to get started with CNC and leave your personal mark on the event.

title: FAB25 Maker Coin: Solder It. Blink It. Wear It.

extendedProps.summary: Come and finish your FAB25 Maker Coin by soldering everything together.
extendedProps.description:
Grab a soldering iron, the PCB from your welcome pack, and components from the table – and let's bring your FAB25 Maker Coin to life in just about 20 minutes!

  • By choosing the resistor values, you get to control how fast your badge blinks. It’s not just about soldering – it’s about making it your own.
  • Never soldered before? No worries. Our friendly instructors are here to guide you step by step. Whether you're a first-timer or a seasoned maker, this is a fun and hands-on way to kick off your FAB25 experience.
  • Wear your coin proudly all week – especially during networking sessions. Attach it to your plastic pouch or use the included magnet for a clean, stylish look.

speakers.description: Cindy is a researcher, teacher and writer in Design-for-Sustainability, focusing on everyday design, maker culture and grassroots urban activism. title: Welcome to Prague party!

speakers.title: Edy Fung
speakers.description:
Edy Fung is an artist whose practice explores the material conditions and technological paradigms of contemporary society, including the philosophy of information, history of science, and machine intelligence. She creates intermedia projects incorporating sound, text, installation, video, digital artefacts, and performance. Since 2023, she has focused on the complexities of quantum technology –⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ a field marked by paradoxes and opacity, especially when linked to computing and algorithms. Seeking alternatives to Big Tech, she collaborates with photonic labs and scientists, while critically reflecting on art-science collaboration. Her work has been presented at Haus der Kulturen der Welt, CTM Berlin, FACT Liverpool, Fylkingen, MUTEK, Sónar+D, Somerset House Studios, Tabakalera, Galway International Arts Festival, Goethe-Institut Irland, and the Irish Museum of Modern Art.

Dear Quantum

Type: Performance
Photo Credits: Edy Fung

A live performance that turns quantum science into sound, visuals and emotion. Using radio waves, real-time data and abstract visuals, Edy Fung explores how we relate to things we can’t fully see or understand – like randomness, probability and the strange logic of quantum mechanics. A poetic, immersive experience at the edge of tech and mystery.

speakers.title: César Chunk
speakers.description: César Chunk is a Peruvian-born DJ and producer based in Prague, known for his immersive, genre-blending sets that fuse tribal rhythms, electronic beats, and global sonic textures. Describing his style as “alternative music from around the world,” he integrates elements like Arabic flutes, African drums, and indigenous chants into his performances, often delivering them while masked to enhance the sensory experience.

extendedProps.summary: Settle into Prague with a relaxed evening above the FAB25 campus – drinks, views, and good company await!
extendedProps.description:
We’re delighted to welcome you to the FAB25 campus at the Prague Exhibition Grounds, including access to the rooftops of the iconic pavilions. On your arrival day in Prague, join us for a relaxed evening with drinks, music and great company.

Enjoy our bar and lounge area, take in the behind-the-scenes vibe of the venue’s final preparations, and soak up the sunset over the lush green spaces surrounding the area. It’s the perfect chance to unwind and get excited for everything that’s coming in the final days of FAB25.

Cheers to Prague – let’s make it unforgettable!

title: Maker Train + Lunch

extendedProps.summary: The most efficient, green and fun way to travel from Brno to Prague!
extendedProps.description:
Hop on board the Maker Train – a private, custom-rented train that takes us from Brno to Prague in the greenest and most efficient way possible. This isn’t just travel – it’s a rolling maker experience!

The ride normally takes around 3 hours, and while you're on board, you’ll enjoy:

  • Simple hands-on surprise workshop right in the train.
  • Lunch packages prepared and waiting for you
  • Plenty of time to network – chat, share ideas, and relax with your fellow Fabbers as the Czech countryside rolls by

🚃 Boarding station: Brno Dolni nadrazi Train Station
Please note: this is NOT the main train station in Brn – make sure you head to the correct one!

🏁 Exit station: Prague-Holesovice Train Station.
This station is just a short walk from the FAB25 campus and the recommended hotels.

Let’s move the maker community – literally!

title: Check-in to Prague

extendedProps.summary: Arrive in Prague, settle in, and get ready for the secong part of FAB25!
extendedProps.description:
Welcome to Prague – the next stop in our FAB25 journey! After the Maker Train ride, it’s time to check in to your accommodation, freshen up, and get ready for the Prague welcome party later in the evening.

Take a moment to relax, explore your surroundings, or grab a bite before diving into the Prague FAB25 campus at the Prague Exhibition Grounds. You’ve made it to one of Europe’s most stunning cities – a 3D architectural textbook and home to both the largest castle complex in the world and a thriving, modern creative scene.

title: Check-out from Brno

extendedProps.summary: Pack up, check out, and get ready for the unforgettable Maker Train ride!
extendedProps.description:
It’s time to say goodbye to Brno – for now! Make sure to pack your things and check out of your accommodation on time. But don’t worry, the adventure continues...

Next stop? The one and only Maker Train – an unforgettable ride filled with makers, conversation, views, and surprises on the rails. So grab your bags, your badge, and your best travel vibes – we’re heading into the next chapter of FAB25 – Prague, the capital of 3D printing.

title: Manufacturing localisation: what and how to produce in Europe. Scaling from one to thousands.

speakers.title: Oleksandr Nesterenko
speakers.description:
Seasoned engineer and entrepreneur with over 20 years of experience in plastic processing technologies, injection molding, 3D printing, hardware, software, design engineering, and product development.
Founded ARTKB, an engineering company recognized among the top 10 companies transforming Ukraine, and co-founded Hushme startup, which made a splash on CES 2017. During this time, Hushme made successful crowdfunding campaigns on Kickstarter (US), Indiegogo (EU), and Campfire (JP) platforms.
Based in the Czech Republic since 2022.

extendedProps.description:
• How to develop a product on your own
  • Own R&D workshop from scratch
  • An iterative approach to development
  • What is Design for Manufacturing
  • Small batch production options
  • 3D printing for serial production
  • Advantages of a local manufacturer
  • Scaling up production options

title: Developing Tools to Assist the Elderly

speakers.title: JAIME SILVA
speakers.description:
Sou um educador apaixonado pela cultura maker e desejo  ajudar a expandir a criatividade, promover o aprendizado e apoiar inovações em diversos campos. Meu papel na comunidade de criadores se reflete em minha capacidade de gerar ideias, conteúdos e soluções, estimulando a criatividade e a resolução de problemas. Além disso, ofereço suporte a educadores, municipes e estudantes para aprimorar o ensino e a aprendizagem, tornando o acesso à informação mais inclusivo e acessível a todos este é o meu foco.
Utilizo linguagens naturais, sou capaz de interagir de maneira intuitiva com diversos setores.

extendedProps.description:
Estou desenvolvendo ferramentas para auxiliar os idosos, focando em melhorar sua qualidade de vida e facilitar a interação com a tecnologia. Meu objetivo é criar soluções que atendam às necessidades específicas dessa faixa etária, promovendo autonomia, segurança e bem-estar no dia a dia.
I am developing tools to assist the elderly, focusing on improving their quality of life and facilitating interaction with technology. My goal is to create solutions that address the specific needs of this age
