Practice your Czech, try local food, and explore the vibrant Prague atmosphere at your own pace. The FAB25 Map with top local picks is available here (more places are being added).

Your Prague evening, your way.

title: Let's create original Sashiko designs! How to creat the coding for embroidery

speakers.title: YUKI TAKEMURA
speakers.description: I have traveled while conducting research on embroidery, textiles, and clothing around the world, starting with Latin America. In particular, I have conducted interviews with embroidery designers. At Fab Lab Hamamatsu (Japan), I have created works using embroidery machines, incorporating embroidery designs from different countries. Additionally, I have held workshops on generating embroidery designs through programming (coding), including sessions at Fab Conferences in 2023 and 2024. In 2024, I also participated in collective activities and exhibited embroidery works at local art festivals.

extendedProps.description: By gaing from taking Fabricademy getting the broader perspective, I will have a workshop to create original sashiko designs while appreciating its cultural heritage, adding color and creativity to the things around ourselves.

title: Applied Biomechanics: Hand Prosthesis Development with STEM

speakers.title: Jose Alexis Del Aguila Ramos
speakers.description: Mechatronics Engineer specialized in Biomedicine and Robotics, with a solid track record in the development and management of technological projects and applied research. As the founder of my own startup, I have led the design and implementation of innovative solutions that integrate various technological disciplines to solve challenges in the healthcare sector. Passionate about generating innovative solutions through the integration of diverse disciplines, with a focus on improving processes and creating disruptive technologies. Committed to education and knowledge transfer, always oriented t

extendedProps.description:
In the Applied Biomechanics: Hand Prosthesis Development with STEM workshop, participants will explore the process of building a functional mechanical prosthesis, applying principles of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM).

Throughout the session, they will learn to assemble and adjust a prosthetic hand using predesigned parts, understanding its working mechanics and the biomechanical principles behind its movement. They will also develop practical skills in using tools, ass

title: Creating souvenirs using unique acrylic materials

speakers.title: KEISUKE INOUE
speakers.description:
One of the co-founders of FabLab Kanda Nishikicho. He has been involved in FabLab activities since 2012. He specializes in creating large-scale objects by combining various machines and tools.
   He is committed to building the FabLab community and nurturing makers. His business involves almost anything related to making, from providing guidance on manufacturing techniques to developing prototypes of imaginative ideas.

extendedProps.description:
This program invites participants to unleash their creativity and craft original souvenirs using unique acrylic materials born in Tokyo. The diverse range of acrylic offers endless possibilities for imaginative creations.
　Participants will also gain insights into the acrylic production process and fabrication techniques, learning the basics of digital fabrication. This deeper understanding of materials will enhance their ability to bring ideas to life.

title: FabRAG: an IA powered assistant for FabLabs, preserving authorship using open source technologies

speakers.title: César García
speakers.description: My name is César García. I have been involved with the maker movement in Spain and Europe for the last decade. I am co-founder of Makespace Madrid and also host of the podcast/Youtube channel La Hora Maker, where I divulge to Spanish speaking audiences the advances in digital fabrication and other open source technologies.

extendedProps.description: In this workshop we will explore FabRAG, a project to build an IA assistant for FabLabs based on the information provided by the expert network of the FabAcademy participants. This project aims to provide every FabLab with a local knowledge based that can be consulted using a chat interface, providing contextualized answers to the question. To do so, it uses an Open source technology stack, that enables multilingual solutions. It provides means to attribute the origin of the sources too!

title: Reimagining Tradition and Craftsmanship: 3D-Printed Lace as Cultural Dialogue

speakers.title: Dr Kadian Gosler
speakers.description:
Dr. Kadian A. Gosler is redefining lingerie at the crossroads of design, technology, and
wearer experience. A pioneering design researcher, earning her PhD from University
of the Arts London, where she developed an experience-centred design process for Bra
Wearables, a subset of smart bras. She founded KDN Research & Design Atelier,
a future-focused lab advancing bra wearables, 3D-printed lingerie, and smart design.
Passionate about inclusivity and innovation, Dr. Gosler challenges traditional lingerie
discourse, ensuring intimate apparel is shaped by wearer needs and cutting-edge technology.

extendedProps.description: Dive into a transformative 4-hour workshop exploring how AI and 3D printing can modernise traditional lace design—reimagining cultural craft. Participants will explore lace traditions, use AI as a co-designer, create hybrid physical-digital 3D-printed lace samples, and learn ethical approaches to preserving heritage through technology. Through cultural and ethical dialogue, attendees gain practical skills bridging tradition, heritage, and innovation.

title: QuantumMiniLabs: Build Your Own Ultrasonic Levitation Device

speakers.title: Michael Schäfer
speakers.description: Michael Schäfer is a Professor of Technical Computer Science at Hochschule Ruhr West (HRW), Germany. After studying physics at TU Dortmund University, he conducted research in distributed multimedia software development and was involved in early internet applications for SMEs in the mid-90s. He later founded a software company focused on microcontroller systems and database-driven applications, which he led for 14 years and contributed to numerous national and European R&D projects. Since 2010, he has been teaching and researching at HRW with a focus on digital systems, embedded development, a

extendedProps.description: In this hands-on workshop, you will build your own ultrasonic levitation device based on a simple one-sided PCB. While not a quantum system itself, the setup illustrates core concepts used in ion-trap-based quantum computers and offers an ideal entry point into electronics and quantum physics education.

title: FreeCAD hands-on session: M.C. Escher's Relativity '53

speakers.title: Pieter Hijma
speakers.description: Pieter Hijma is a self-employed software developer and researcher focusing on open source software.  His background is Computer Science and has a PhD on the topic of High Performance Computing.  He is an open source software and hardware enthusiast with an interest in how to document open source hardware.  After a research project on automatically generating assembly manuals for open source hardware, he deepened his knowledge of documenting open source hardware by completing the Fab Academy program at Waag Futurelab Amsterdam. This inspired him to dedicate his time to develop FreeCAD to 1) make designing physical products highly accessible to the public in general and 2) to ensure that open source hardware can be designed with open tools.

extendedProps.description: Experience FreeCAD 1.0 and exchange experiences with two developers from the FreeCAD team: Pieter Hijma (Fab Academy alumnus) and Chris Hennes (member of the board of the FreeCAD Project Association). The topics are scripted geometry in Python, the new Assembly Workbench, and Addon Development with as running example M.C. Escher's lithograph "Relativity" 1953. We are also very interested in the needs of the FAB Community regarding FreeCAD.

title: Learn to harness microbial fuel cell technology combined with hydroponics

speakers.title: Günter Seyfried
speakers.description: Günter Seyfried is an Austrian artist. He has a background in medicine and psychology, which he studied at the University of Vienna, and has strong links to the fine arts, digital art, and media art, having graduated from the University of Applied Arts Vienna (Department of Digital Art). He is teaching at the New Design University in St. Pölten and works at Biofaction, Vienna, as a researcher, developer. He is involved in diverse projects, including technology assessment studies on hacking communities. As a developer, he is producing workshops and science communication programs.

extendedProps.description: The system will modulate nitrogen forms, plant root microbiomes optimise nitrogen uptake, and mobilise phosphorous, averting the need for chemical fertilisers. This circular, sustainable platform turns carbon into biomass and reclaims nitrogen from wastewater streams.

title: From the lab to the forest and back, a STEM-project on plant science.

speakers.title: Lowie Goossens
speakers.description: We are the founders of STEM-Support, a belgium based start-up in STEM-education support. We provide projects to schools and individuals and organise workshops. Originated from FabLab-Leuven, where we started out as working students. Lowie is an engineer in computer science student (last year) and Femke is working as a highschool teacher. This combination is what drives STEM-support.

extendedProps.description: In cooperation with FabLab-Leuven, we developed a complete workshop around plant science, from plant seeds to monitoring an entire forest. For this project, we made our own growth chamber with which you can control numerous growth conditions. This chamber turned out to be so effective that it is currently even tested in a University research lab. We also designed a sensor-box to monitor life conditions in a forest and deployed that in Meerdael-forest where it has been gathering information.

title: Build a Quakecraft earthquake simulation table for testing model houses and structures

speakers.title: Bridget McKendry
speakers.description: Bridget McKendry is a maker and educator who co-founded Fab Lab CHCH which ran from 2014-18 in Christchurch, New Zealand. She has contributed to a number of STEM and digital fabrication events and projects, most notably the Quakecraft programme and kits for high school engineering education that won the 2018 Chevron STEM award at Fab 14 in Toulouse. Bridget is currently an academic staff member in the Fashion Technology department at Te Pukenga / Ara institute of Canterbury, teaching and researching 3D printing for apparel and textiles.

extendedProps.description: Quakecraft is an open source STEM education kit for teachers and students that was designed after our city suffered a major earthquake in 2011. In this session we will build our own programmable desktop earthquake simulation tables made with 3D printed and laser cut parts and our soldering kits. These can then be used to run our Quakecraft challenges for teams to compete to see who can build the most resilient structure to withstand a series of "quakes" !

title: [Trans]National & Continental FabLabs Networks: Lessons, Challenges, and Future Collaboration

speakers.title: Alexandre Rousselet
speakers.description: Makerspaces explorer since 2015. I co-created with some Erasmus student friends, VULCA Network and together we pushed the door of more than 1000 FabLabs, Makerspaces and Hackerspaces in Europe. Now I work full time for RFFLabs (The French FabLabs Network) and I'm a volunteer supporting the development of my local makerspace "Mountain Makers" located in my village in middle France.

extendedProps.description: Many continental +(trans)national networks have emerged to support their communities. But how do they operate? What do they offer to their members? What challenges do they face? In this moderated session, representatives from various FabLab networks—LATAM, FAN, Nordic, Benelux, VoW, RFFLabs, ... will share their experiences, common pitfalls, and success stories. How networks function, services they provide, what services they provide, and how they can engage in international collaboration

title: Round Table Lessons from FabLabs in Erasmus+: What We Learned & What’s Next

speakers.title: Alexandre Rousselet
speakers.description: Makerspaces explorer since 2015. I co-created with some Erasmus student friends, VULCA Network and together we pushed the door of more than 1000 FabLabs, Makerspaces and Hackerspaces in Europe. Now I work full time for RFFLabs (The French FabLabs Network) and I'm a volunteer supporting the development of my local makerspace "Mountain Makers" located in my village in middle France.

extendedProps.description: What is it like to run an Erasmus+ project as a FabLab? This round table brings together experienced FabLab leaders who have successfully participated in Erasmus+ to share their experiences—their challenges, successes, and lessons learned. This is an opportunity for newcomers to hear firsthand insights and get practical advice before applying.

title: Free FabLabs Charter: Promoting Openness, Collaboration, and Knowledge Sharing

speakers.title: Paddy Condevaux
speakers.description: Paddy Condevaux is a computer engineer from EPFL, Switzerland. Passionate about innovation, learning, teaching and sharing he joined the Fab Lab movement since its inception and shares its values and objectives. Founder of the Fablac in Anthy-sur-Léman (France), he is currently employed by fablab Onl'fait in Geneva where he is in charge of electronics and school programs. He was involved in the drafting of the Free Fab Labs Charter, initiated by Richard Stallman, and continues to actively promote its principles within the Fab Lab community.

extendedProps.description: We will exchange into the principles of a Free FabLabs Charter, a document initiated and supported by Richard Stallman. The Free FabLabs Charter aims promoting open-source software & hardware, open data, open design, and free access to knowledge. We will explore the importance of open design for creation within a FabLab, with a spirit of sharing and collective intelligence. Attendees will share insights into how they can apply these principles in their own Fab Labs and contribute to open design.

title: Workgroup: FabLab Residencies

speakers.title: Tauan Bernardo
speakers.description: Tauan Bernardo is the head of the FabLab at Center Rog, a designer, creator, and educator. He has led numerous FabLabs and is a passionate advocate for technology, innovation, digital manufacturing, and the maker movement.

extendedProps.description: Residency programs are an emerging trend in the FabLab community. Talent exchange between labs has become an important aspect of institutional integration and is essential to maintaining FabLabs as spaces of experimentation and innovation. We aim to create a structured conversation, applying service design methodologies to identify common challenges, propose solutions, and highlight good practices.

title: Designing and Transforming Sites for PK-12 School-Wide Integrating of Fab Labs and Maker Pedagogy

speakers.title: Adam devitt
speakers.description: Adam Devitt Ph.D. is an Associate Professor of Science Education at California State University, Stanislaus, and the Director of Maker Pedagogy and K-12 Outreach in the Warrior Fab Lab and Director of the Early Childhood Education Credential Program.

extendedProps.description: Explore strategies for educators working with children, youth, and adults, highlighting intersections and collaboration opportunities. Emphasizing creativity, innovation, inclusivity, and lifelong learning, the discussion will focus on problem-solving, critical thinking, and digital fabrication. Through brainstorming, storyboarding, and discussions, participants will develop ideas to build a regional pipeline of makers/fabricators, ensuring equitable access to STEM and activity-based learning.

title: Fab City Pledge

extendedProps.description: Fab City welcomes two new members to its network: Fab City Charlotte and Fab Region Bergisches. Join us in celebrating this milestone with the growing Fab City community.

title: Empowering Learning Spaces with Fab in a Box (FiaB)

speakers.title: Rodney Williams
speakers.description: As Program Manager for the Fab Foundation, Dr. Rodney Williams oversees educational outreach projects with public, corporate, and private partners across the U.S. and select international locations. He forges strategic partnerships that enhance the impact of these initiatives. Working closely with educators, lab managers, and administrators, he ensures successful project implementation. Driven by a passion for STEAM education, Dr. Williams is dedicated to expanding access to digital fabrication and hands-on learning worldwide.

extendedProps.description: Empower educators with hands-on digital fabrication tools! This interactive workshop introduces Fab in a Box (FiaB), a portable system designed for classrooms, libraries, and community spaces. Attendees will explore digital design, laser cutting, and 3D printing while engaging with curriculum-aligned lessons that enhance STEM learning, design thinking, and problem-solving. Whether you're new to digital fabrication or a FabLab veteran, discover practical ways to integrate FiaB into education!

title: Can FabLab help local manufacturing factories? Sharing of idea, FactoryScientist

speakers.title: MASATO TAKEMURA
speakers.description: I am managing FabLab Hamamatsu/TAKE-SPACE for 11years. Also I am running a IoT lecture cource for SME as a FabLab training.

extendedProps.description: I am running the online IoT cource for Manufacturing companies with some Japanese FabLabs. FabLab and manufacturing are very compatible and I want to share my experience.

title: The New Era of the Maker Movement:  the TimeBridge - A Collaborative STEAM Experience with xTool

speakers.title: Shaoli Ding
speakers.description:
We are a team of Europe-based Technical Support Engineers at xTool, passionate about empowering the maker and education communities. We provide hands-on guidance, troubleshoot challenges, and help users unlock the full potential of their xTool devices through local, expert support.
We are a team of Europe-based Technical Support Engineers at xTool, passionate about empowering the maker and education communities. We provide hands-on guidance, troubleshoot challenges, and help users unlock the full potential of their xTool devices through local, expert support.

extendedProps.description: Discover the power of making at xTool’s TimeBridge workshop @FAB25! Fab Lab coordinators, educators, young makers, and media/influencers will unite to reimagine Prague’s Orloj through storytelling, robotics, and digital fabrication. In just 3 hours, co-create a stunning clock sculpture that fuses heritage with innovation—bridging past, present, and future through hands-on, STEAM-driven creativity.

title: openUC2 - The Raspberry for Optics and Quantum Physics

speakers.title: Benedict Diederich
speakers.description: Benedict Diederich is the founder and CEO of openUC2 GmbH, a company dedicated to making modular, open-source microscopy accessible for education, research, and industry. With a background in optics and photonics, he leads the development of scalable hardware and software platforms - such as the UC2 cube system and FRAME microscope - that combine affordability with high performance. His work bridges disciplines through automation, smart imaging, and open innovation, enabling communities worldwide to build and customize their own scientific instruments.

extendedProps.description:
Participants will gain:

  • Understanding in optics and microscopy
  • learn how to build microscopes
    – Basic understanding of quantum principles
    – Inspiration to explore emerging tech
    – Hands-on skills using low-cost quantum experiments

title: Design Revolution Now!

speakers.title: Leopold Zyka
speakers.description:
DNA: X2b, R1a1a
Born as digital immigrant in Vienna, Austria, passionate Software Architect and Open Source Evangelist dedicated to fostering innovation through technology and sustainability.
🚀 Founder of OpenLandLAB: A makerspace and fab lab in rural Austria. As moderator of FAB Region Südburgenland, I champion the philosophy of "produce locally, network globally."
🌱 Visionary Projects: A multidisciplinary studio surrounded by TinyLabs for mushrooms, compost, algae. KInsecta, EdgeAI, Connecting The Things Network to the WOOD WIDE WEB

extendedProps.description:
The Working Group is committed to igniting a design revolution through collaboration and innovation focusing on the following key objectives:

  1. Setup: Utilize miro, a cutting-edge collaboration platform
  2. Establishing a Permanent Network: Build a sustainable network for continuous knowledge exchange and long-term collaborative projects.
  3. Exploring Alternatives to the Design for Distributed Innovation program.
  4. Investigate open-source alternatives for the RegGen Village Operating System

title: From Local Initiatives to Global Synergies: Scaling Sensing Data

speakers.title: Paola Zanchetta
speakers.description: Paola Zanchetta (She/Her) - Distributed Designer and Researcher - driven to create systemic change, through collaborative efforts and Open-Hardware outcomes. Her work promotes regenerative and planet-centric practices, transforming industrial waste into valuable materials and products while raising circular awareness and critical thinking.

extendedProps.description: Years of experience with low-cost IoT sensors and citizen data have brought us thus far. Open Data from innovative hardware such as the SCK has been primarily supply pushed, with clear challenges emerging at the time of sustainably using this source and upscaling its impact. This session brings a demand-pulled perspective from the 5 EU Missions. We will co-create with participants pathways for their ideas and projects to become interoperable with data spaces, funded, and upscaled.

title: PENCE Policy Lab: Unlocking Opportunities Through Creativity, Practice & Policy

speakers.title: Freda Yamorti Gbande
speakers.description: Freda Yamorti Gbande is an ecosystem builder passionate about global innovation. She has worked with initiatives such as the Africa Open Science Hardware Initiative, the AI for Agriculture and Food Systems Hub and the Africa-European Maker Innovation Ecosystem Project—championing innovation and development across Africa.

speakers.title: Milena Calvo Juarez
speakers.description: Milena Calvo Juarez is an Environmental Engineer and Project Coordinator at Fab Lab Barcelona, part of the Institute for Advanced Architecture of Catalonia (IAAC). She holds a master’s degree in Industrial Ecology from the Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona, and her work focuses on interdisciplinary fields such as circular economy, regenerative cities, innovative materials, local development, and social innovation. Milena is currently involved in several European projects, including CitiObs, more4nature, PENCE, and FOSTER, and leads the coordination of the ENSEMBLE Network of Productive Spaces in Barcelona.

extendedProps.description:
The Policy Lab is intended to support participants and the Fab City Global Initiative to explore the key challenges and opportunities to implementing policy to support local circular economy solutions.
It aims to:
-Showcase policy design experiences highlighting the design process, challenges and relevant impacts for the Fab City agenda
-Discuss, based on concrete experiences, strategic policy areas that could support the Fab City agenda and partners
-Share policy options and recommendations

title: Creating a Manifesto for Environmental Protection through Distributed Open Data and Citizen Science

speakers.title: Danisa Peric
speakers.description: We are a multidisciplinary group of makers from Ecuador, Spain, Chile, and Mexico, and we come together to promote projects, initiatives, and resources on technologies and citizen science at the ibero-american level. Our organizations are FabLab U. de Chile (Director), FabLab UDLA (Director), Fab City Leon (Director) and FabLab Barcelona (Sense Making Lead).

speakers.title: Óscar González
speakers.description: Óscar González is an Industrial Engineer with expertise in data analysis, testing, and calibration through his experience in automotive and sensor development. He is the Sense Making Lead at Fab Lab Barcelona doing research and development on the Smart Citizen project. Óscar has worked as a researcher on various projects, spanning the development and implementation of open source technologies and low-cost sensors, including their application in citizen science initiatives, as tools for environmental and health assessment, and the interoperability of data across research infrastructures.

speakers.title: Renato Avila Claro
speakers.description: Architect and digital fabrication specialist from Ecuador. He is the Director of the UDLA FabLab and Scientific Director at Master in Advanced Architectural Design at Universidad de Las Américas (UDLA) in Quito, where he integrates technology, education, and design to foster innovation. With a background in architecture and co-founder of PDG–Pata de Gallo and Automater 3DP, he works at the intersection of digital making, creative industries, and open-source tools. He is passionate about empowering students and local communities through accessible design and hands-on learning.

speakers.title: Paco Flores
speakers.description: Fab City León, Initiative Director.

extendedProps.description: We want to organize a 2-hour Working Group that defines the future of an international community of open and distributed environmental data. Together, we will write a collective manifesto that will define the group's purpose and objectives, focusing on environmental protection, socially focused technologies and the use of collaborative data. The goal is to strengthen collaboration between territories and bioregions to take action in environmental protection.

title: Mobile FabLabs World Record Meetup

extendedProps.summary: Join the official gathering of Mobile FabLabs from around the world – all in one place, for the first time ever!
extendedProps.description:
FAB25 is making history – and you can be part of it. For the first time ever, mobile FabLabs from across Europe are coming together in one spot. Vans, trucks, trailers – if it moves and makes, it’s invited.

– Meet the teams behind these rolling labs
– Tour their unique setups and share ideas
– Snap a photo with what might just be a world record

Whether you're already running a Mobile FabLab, dreaming of starting one, or just curious how makers stay mobile, this is the ultimate meetup. Let’s celebrate mobility, creativity, and the global power of making – together at FAB25.

title: Art & Design Expo

speakers.title: Robin Baumgarten
speakers.description:
Robin is an award-winning experimental hardware game developer and interactive installation artist based in Berlin. After researching Artificial Intelligence in Games and working on mobile games, he is now fully focused on creating playful interactive installations that straddle the divide of games and art, such as Line Wobbler and Wobble Garden. His most recent projects also involve a scientific aspect: Quantum Garden and its larger sequel, Quantum Jungle, both visualise quantum physics in a playful yet scientifically accurate manner.

Quantum Jungle

Type: Interactive installation
Photo Credits: Robin Baumgarten

An interactive installation that transforms the complexity of quantum physics into a sensory experience of sound and light. As visitors move through the space, their presence triggers shifts in rhythm, pattern, and color – reflecting the uncertainty and entanglement at the heart of quantum mechanics.

speakers.title: Kryštof Brůha
speakers.description:
Kryštof Brůha is an intermedia artist living and working in Prague, whose work explores specific environments – from the natural to the technological and virtual. He focuses on their influences and their overall transformation over time. He allows us to look at phenomena hidden from our perception, for example through macro and micro mapping of the environment, and through such scanning of space and time through artistic artefacts he offers the viewer a new view of reality. His media include installations, kinetic sculptures, generative videos and experimental films. In the development process, he uses scientific methods, specially designed algorithms, generative design and machine learning. In implementation, Brůha creates custom tools using Rapid Prototyping and CNC technology that are computer controlled. All these technological processes are then reflected in the aesthetics of his works.

Audire Fluctus

Type: Interactive light sculpture
Photo Credits: Tomáš Slavík & Dušan Vondra

An interactive sculpture that visualizes the invisible world of electromagnetic waves. Using a custom-developed medium called Spektraflux, the piece responds to ambient electromagnetic activity –⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ such as signals from electrical grids and communication devices –⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ by transforming them into dynamic light patterns.

speakers.title: Adam Cigler & Petr Vacek
speakers.description:
Petr Vacek and Adam Cigler are Czech architects who synergize a creative artistic approach with programming, robotics, digital fabrication and traditional craftsmanship. Their collaboration arose during their studies at the Faculty of Arts and Architecture in Liberec and, thanks to a common fondness for advanced technology, science, space or philosophy, crystallized into an aesthetically appealing freeform creative experiment.

Reflexion

Type: Kinetic sculpture
Photo Credits: Ray Baseley

A hypnotic kinetic sculpture made of dozens of independently moving mirrors. Each mirror is precisely controlled by servo motors and arranged in clusters, forming a responsive light object driven by a network of Raspberry Pi microcomputers. Made with 3D-printed components and CNC-cut panels, Reflexion blends digital control with analog beauty – reflecting not just light, but movement, complexity and the invisible choreography of data.

speakers.title: Fabricademy Collective
speakers.description:
Fabricademy is a global, distributed educational program that explores the intersection of textiles, digital fabrication, and biology. Over 6 months, participants dive into a hands-on journey that blends traditional crafts with cutting-edge technologies like 3D printing, electronics, wearable tech, and biofabrication. It's a space for experimentation, collaboration, and redefining the future of fashion and materials through open-source tools and sustainable innovation. Fabricademy is hosted by Fab Labs and innovation spaces worldwide, making it a truly international and community-driven experience.

Fabricademy Exhibition

Type: Exhibition of selected models
Photo Credits: Barbara Rakovska, Fabricademy

Discover the 11th annual showcase of innovation, sustainability, and digital craftsmanship by Fabricademy participants and local Czech designers. This exhibition highlights groundbreaking projects at the intersection of technology, textiles, and biology. It features a curated selection of forward-thinking designs that merge digital fabrication, electronics, and biofabrication with sustainable materials and techniques.

speakers.title:
Lukáš Dřevjaný,
Luboš Zbranek & others
speakers.description:
**Lukáš Dřevjaný** is a visual artist, lighting designer, and mentor of creative technologies. He combines light, space, and interactivity in live performances, light installations, large-scale projections, and theatre performances. He primarily works under his brand oxoo. Among other things, he mentors technology in the Art&Digital Lab in Hall 40 at the Holešovice Market and organizes technology workshops. **Luboš Zbranek** is a 3D graphic, VJ, video, and light designer. During his studies of Interactive Media Theory at Masaryk University, he has focused on creating interactive environments and deeper exploration of the human-machine, human-code relationship. By studying light design at JAMU, he expanded his scope to include light design, light art, and performance genres. He has collaborated visually on many techno and electronic music events, interactive installations, and theatre performances at home and abroad. **Other collaborators: David Minařík, Michal Mitro, Maurício Lobo**

Nexus

Type: Interactive light sculpture
Photo Credits: Viktor Schwing

The Nexus grows algorithmically in time and space. Like the rhizome, the mycelium, or human and non-human neural networks, the individual cells of this digital moss are constantly exchanging information. The body of the work is made up of 300 eight-legged acrylic cells, straddling all axes of space. The individual building elements are interconnected, functioning as a volumetric display.

extendedProps.summary: Explore how Czech and European artists are pushing the boundaries of art through digital making.
extendedProps.description:
Step into the Art & Design Expo – a curated collection of interactive installations, experimental objects, and inspiring works by Czech and European artists who blend creativity with cutting-edge fabrication tools.

– Meet the makers behind pieces like Quantum Jungle, Audire Fluctus, Reflexion, Nexus and the Fabricademy annual exhibition
– Discover how digital tools like CNCs, 3D printers, and laser cutters are used to create awe-inspiring works
– Take a walk through art that moves, reacts, glows – and definitely deserves a spot on your feed

Come take a look, snap some cool photos, and get inspired by what happens when art and digital fabrication collide.

title: Maker Circle with Dale Dougherty

speakers.title: Dale Dougherty
speakers.description: Dale Dougherty believes that all of us are makers, and he is a champion of the people and projects that form the Maker Movement. He founded Make: magazine in 2005 and first used the term "makers" to describe people who enjoyed hands-on work and play. Often called the Father of the Maker Movement, he launched Maker Faire in the San Francisco Bay Area in 2006, an event that has since spread worldwide, regularly showcasing and celebrating maker projects. He is the President of Make Community, LLC, which publishes Make: magazine and licenses Maker Faires globally.

extendedProps.summary: An informal, open conversation with Dale Dougherty – the Father of the Maker Movement – where your stories take the spotlight.
extendedProps.description: Join the Maker Circle with Dale Dougherty – a relaxed, no-agenda gathering where you can meet and chat with one of the most influential voices in the maker world. Whether you're running a FabLab, building projects in your garage, or sparking change in your local community, this is your space to share stories, ideas, and questions. No slides, no pressure – just people, projects, and spontaneous inspiration. Let’s see what the energy brings!

title: PENCE Policy Lab: Symposium

speakers.title: Guillem Camprodon
speakers.bio: Executive Director at Fab Lab Barcelona (IAAC) and Co-Director of the Master in Design for Emergent Futures
speakers.description: Guillem Camprodon (he/him) is a designer and technologist exploring how emerging technologies can empower communities and shape more equitable futures. He leads Fab Lab Barcelona at IAAC, a global reference in digital fabrication and civic innovation, and co-directs the Master in Design for Emergent Futures with IAAC and ELISAVA. Guillem also co-leads the Smart Citizen project and has contributed to numerous European research initiatives focused on data commons, regenerative design, and bottom-up innovation helping to scale Fab Lab Barcelona into a reference for applied action research.

speakers.title: Andrew Lamb
speakers.description: Andrew Lamb has been participating in or leading the Humanitarian FabLabs working group since the first sessions at Fab11 in Boston. He is the former Innovation Lead of the humanitarian relief organisation Field Ready and now chairs the Internet of Production Alliance which supports a number of makerspace initiatives around the world. Andrew is also the chair of FabLab Winam in Kenya.

speakers.title: Kate Armstrong
speakers.description: Kate Armstrong is a strategist, educator and community builder working across design, technology and ecology. As CEO of Fab City Foundation, she leads a global network of 50+ cities shifting toward regenerative, locally productive systems. She also directs Interspecies Internet, advancing interspecies communication through research and public programs, including work at the Santa Fe Institute. Kate has led EU research projects on design, shaped cultural programs across Europe and Australia, and taught at IAAC and contributed to numerous publications. She’s known for making complex ideas accessible, fostering dialogue on community resilience, sustainability and more-than-human futures.

speakers.title: Lucy von Sturmer
speakers.bio: Founder & CEO of global non-profit Creatives for Climate
speakers.description:
Lucy von Sturmer is Founder & CEO of global non-profit Creatives for Climate, the largest and most diverse network of creative professionals dedicated to climate action, with 6000+ members across 90 countries. A climate communications expert with over 15 years of experience, she has been recognized by AdWeek, Ad Age, and Forbes as one of the leading global voices driving sustainability in advertising. Her mission is to turn creative influence into climate action leading to a just and regenerative future — through bold leadership, better practices, and briefs that put the planet first. Creatives for Climate is an officially recognised Accelerator to the Race to Zero, the world’s largest
coalition of non-state actors working towards net zero under the UN Climate Change High Level Champions.

extendedProps.description: The PENCE Policy Lab highlights how projects by makers, researchers and built environment professionals engage with policy to enact change. The symposium brings together a panel of experts to discuss the possibilities, difficulties and realities of working with policy.

title: Flexible electronics in the fablab: using a vinyl cutter to make flexible circuit boards

speakers.title: Jessica Stanley
speakers.description: Jessica Stanley is a researcher working on e-textiles, flexible electronics, and engineering education. She is interested in making as a way to make technology accessible and inclusive, and has worked with, and in, several fablabs across Europe. She is a Fabricademy graduate, and participated in the EU project Shemakes, a collaboration between fablabs to support women in the textile industry. Jessica has also worked as maker education coordinator, running electronics and digital fabrication workshops in Ireland, the Netherlands, and the UK. She has recently completed a PhD in e-textiles.

extendedProps.description: Learn how to make a flexible circuit board using copper tape, some flexible plastic film, and a vinyl cutter. Flexible circuit boards (flexible PCBs) are made from thin conductive tracks on flexible plastic films, and are used in the electronics industry when a circuit needs to fold or roll up in a small space (like inside a camera), or bend and flex (in an e-textile, for example). This hands-on workshop will explain how to make flexible PCBs, and you will make your own to take home.

title: CAD Gpt. Generative AI for Everyone

speakers.title: Jorge Montalvo
speakers.description: Mechatronic Designer, and Digital Fabrication Specialist based in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates. Holds a Master's degree in Engineering and an a wide array of certifications including Project Management Professional PMP, Solidworks Expert CSWE, and Autodesk Certified Professional. With vast experience in Digital Manufacturing, 3d printing, CAD/CAM/CAE, and product design

extendedProps.description: Participants of this workshop will explore the application of generative AI language models for modeling in 3D. Multiple tools will be explored, from python code generation function to create scripts to turn prompts into 3d models, to different engines to turn images to ready to print STLs. Come learn the processes that we used to dive into generative design and discover the challenges and solutions that we learned from researching this process.

title: Communication and Concepts among Mobile FabLabs

speakers.title: Gerrit Rüdebusch
speakers.description: Gerrit Rüdebusch studied Empirical Social and Political Analysis at the University of Stuttgart. He joined the Professorship for Vocational and Occupational Education at the Helmut Schmidt University Hamburg in 2021 and is part of the Institute of Production Engineering in the Department of Mechanical Engineering since 2022. His research focuses on evaluating the impact of transformational education for sustainable development, currently by evaluating the OpenLab Mobile.

extendedProps.description: It is a great feeling of accomplishment once a Mobile FabLab is on the road, bring the idea of fabrication directly to the people. Thus being mobile as a teacher, community worker, promoter of ingenuity—or a bit of everything–does not mean to be stand-alone. We are part of an international group with similar goals and approaches, having the potential to learn from other team’s knowledge, concepts and experiences. This working group looks for the best way to do so and establish a foundation.

title: The future of circular library makerspaces

speakers.title: Jessica Guy
speakers.description: Jessica Guy is a designer, maker, and strategic research lead of Distributed Design at Fab Lab Barcelona. Jessica's work centers around researching ontologies and narratives of design from plural perspectives within design and maker education. At Fab Lab Barcelona, Jessica is the project manager of the EU-funded project Distributed Design Platform and Make Works global. They design and curate annual programs for learning experiences, knowledge exchanges, publications, hybrid events, awards, and other opportunities to support emerging creative talents.

extendedProps.description: The objective of this workshop is to bring together creative minds, makers, hackers, and designers to collaboratively shape the future of modular makerspaces in libraries, with a strong emphasis on circular design. This workshop seeks to reimagine and co-create the next generation of makerspaces within libraries, responding to pressing social, economic, and ecological challenges of our time.

title: How to Code Almost Anything for Hardware with LLM?

speakers.title: Leon Feng
speakers.description: Lei Feng is a student of Fab Academy 2025. He has worked at Seeed Studio in Shenzhen for six years, leading the user experience team. His career includes roles as a website editor, interaction designer, product manager, and game planner before joining Seeed Studio in 2018 to design courses for Chaihuo Maker Education. Since 2021, he has published several technical books. Lei Feng's Fab Academy 2025 homepage: https://fabacademy.org/2025/labs/chaihuo/students/lei-feng/

extendedProps.description: Presented by Seeed Studio, this workshop aims to revolutionize embedded hardware programming by teaching participants how to use Large Language Models (LLMs) as programming assistants to quickly transform ideas into functional code. Using the Wio Terminal as a practical platform, through systematic learning and progressive challenges, participants will master how to write effective prompts, obtain and optimize code, and design interactive UI interfaces.

title: Hands on quantum experiments

speakers.title: Michael Bennemann
speakers.description:
Michael Bennemann
- Enthusiastic FabLab user since the first Fablab in Germany
- PhD and research assistant in the field of bionics
- Lecturer for graphical freeware, statistics and computer science
- Employee of the company Haake for safety technology
- currently project manager of the Quantum FabLab project at the HRW-Fablab and the Saarland University

extendedProps.description: Learn the basics of quantum mechanics by setting up your own quantum experiments with our newly created low-cost quantum cube construction kit.

title: RepRapMicron - Micrometre scale 3D Printing from scratch

speakers.title: Vik Olliver
speakers.description: Vik is best known as the person who built the first replicating 3D printer, the RepRap, the ancestor of most desktop 3D printers. He also invented PLA filament. These days he works at the local Fab Lab in New Zealand which has a high proportion of special needs and elderly members of the community, as well as significant involvement with local cultural arts. He is equally at home behind a keyboard, soldering iron, screwdriver, or something entirely more energetic and dangerous.

extendedProps.description:
RepRapMicron is part of the Open Source RepRap Project that spawned 3D printers like the Prusa. It is designed to be mostly 3D printed, and uses only easily obtained parts.

It already produces 0.5mm objects with 10μm resolution from UV resin, using a head that can be made from household materials in ten minutes.

Problem: It has to be able to manipulate and operate them using a toolchain that it creates for itself. So if it made a really, really, small 3D printer ...

title: It's just beginning: EU policy and the rise of open products

speakers.title: Maximilian Voigt
speakers.description: Maximilian Voigt manages the Prototype Fund Hardware for OKF DE. He works on improving the transparency of technical products for civil society in order to create more opportunities for participation. Specific projects include the right to repair and the digital product passport. Maximilian was on the board of the Association of Open Workshops for six years and studied journalism, engineering and philosophy of technology.

extendedProps.description: Open source hardware is not dead! The EU is driving a fundamental shift in the product system through greater transparency and open design. By advancing a circular economy, it creates an ideal environment for open products reimagined from the ground up. In this talk, I will provide insight into EU policy decisions and present three promising open source hardware projects from Germany that are setting new standards.

title: Smart Citizen Meets Meshtastic - Results

speakers.title: Óscar González
speakers.description: Óscar González is an Industrial Engineer with expertise in data analysis, testing, and calibration through his experience in automotive and sensor development. He is the Sense Making Lead at Fab Lab Barcelona doing research and development on the Smart Citizen project. Óscar has worked as a researcher on various projects, spanning the development and implementation of open source technologies and low-cost sensors, including their application in citizen science initiatives, as tools for environmental and health assessment, and the interoperability of data across research infrastructures.

extendedProps.description: In this talk we would like to share the results of our "Smart Citizen Meets Meshtastic" Hackathon, both after the hacking phase and the deployment of Air Quality monitors in the cities.

title: Introducing the mAkE MOOC!

speakers.title: Andrew Lamb
speakers.description: Andrew Lamb has been participating in or leading the Humanitarian FabLabs working group since the first sessions at Fab11 in Boston. He is the former Innovation Lead of the humanitarian relief organisation Field Ready and now chairs the Internet of Production Alliance which supports a number of makerspace initiatives around the world. Andrew is also the chair of FabLab Winam in Kenya.

extendedProps.description: Presenting the free, open online course from the mAkE Project! The mAkE project was a three-year initiative to bring together makerspaces in Europe and in Africa to help build co-operation in an eco-system. One of its activities is a collection of online courses aimed at makerspace managers... we're here to tell you about it and do a quick demo!

title: The SET Service Model: Quantum Literacy as a Tool for Community-Led STEM Education

speakers.title: Heaven Whitby
speakers.description: Heaven Whitby is the Director of the FVSU Fab Lab, FVSU Mobile STEM Lab, and FVSU Precision Agriculture Labs. She focuses on quantum education in minority communities, particularly small and rural areas, ensuring equitable access to emerging technologies. Through community-driven initiatives, she integrates quantum literacy with real-world applications, collaborating with institutions like HBCUs. Supported by HRW FabLab Bottrop, she enhances global understanding of quantum concepts, advancing the international dialogue on quantum education.

extendedProps.description: The SET Service Model integrates quantum literacy into community-based projects, allowing students to apply quantum principles to real-world problems. In collaboration with the Girl Scouts of Historic Georgia, students engage in STEM projects, bridging science and everyday life. This approach aligns with the Engaged Humanities and Co-Production Models, emphasizing public scholarship and student-driven learning to bridge the digital divide and prepare students for the quantum economy.

title: Unlocking the past: how technology is transforming heritage preservation

speakers.title: Aristarco Cortés
speakers.description: Aristarco is an industrial engineer with MBA studies and aspiring polymath. He has over 30 years of working experience in different sectors such as industry, government and universities. He is an entrepreneur, in love with fabrication processes, Fab Academy alumni and hardcore Maker. After being director of the biggest makerspace in the world, the Innovation, Design and Technology Institute, IDIT, at the Jesuit Iberoamericana University of Puebla, Mexico, today he develops entrepreneurial tech, social and innovation projects at IDIT.

extendedProps.description: This talk will showcase how 3D scanning and digital fabrication are revolutionizing heritage preservation. We will discuss collaborative projects with museums and Mexico’s National Institute of Anthropology and History (INAH), highlighting how technology is preserving cultural heritage, creating digital replicas, and enhancing public and academic engagement.

title: FabLabs in European Programmes: 10 Years of Impact and Opportunities

speakers.title: Alexandre Rousselet
speakers.description: Makerspaces explorer since 2015. I co-created with some Erasmus student friends, VULCA Network and together we pushed the door of more than 1000 FabLabs, Makerspaces and Hackerspaces in Europe. Now I work full time for RFFLabs (The French FabLabs Network) and I'm a volunteer supporting the development of my local makerspace "Mountain Makers" located in my village in middle France.

extendedProps.description: Over the past decade, FabLabs have actively contributed to various European funding programmes, yet many still struggle to navigate these opportunities. This talk provides an insightful overview of FabLabs’ participation in Erasmus+, Interreg, Horizon, and other EU initiatives. Attendees will leave with a better understanding of funding trends, the role of FabLabs in European policies, and practical insights into how (over the following working group) they can take part in future opportunities.

title: Technological and digital culture in European Arts & Crafts. FabLabs as a transformation lever.

speakers.title: Pauline Kuntz
speakers.description: I'm involved in european projects as MOSAIC and Transform4European about Arts & Crafts, design, participatory research, interdisciplinarity and innovation. Member of the RFFLabs collegial council and founder of Imprae, I also practice as a glass creator, a designer, a lecturer and teacher.

extendedProps.description: This talk explores the technological and digital transformation of professional practices in the Arts & Crafts sector. Drawing on data from the European project MOSAIC, it highlights key challenges arising from socio-material changes, based on country-specific case studies. Gaps between the use of technological/digital tools and their transformative dynamics are discussed in relation to FabLab experiences and how they can enhance Arts & Crafts practices.

title: Ruwaytech in TAMBOs: Empowering Rural Communities through MiniFabLabs

speakers.title: Juan Diego Cerron Salcedo
speakers.description:
Master of Science in Electronic Engineering (UNSA), with specialties in Instrumentation, Automation, and Process Control (TECSUP), Industrial Maintenance Management (TECSUP), and Digital Manufacturing (Fab Foundation - CBA MIT). I graduated in Electronic Engineering from the Catholic University of Santa María (UCSM).


I am currently pursuing a Doctorate in Business Administration (DBA) at the National University of San Agustín (UNSA), where I will consolidate my experience in Strategic Organizational Management.

extendedProps.description: In this session, we’ll explore how Ruwaytech leverages mini-fablabs within rural TAMBO facilities to empower communities through hands-on digital fabrication. Attendees will learn practical strategies for integrating technology and education in resource-limited settings, discover tools and methods for developing local solutions, and understand the social impact of equipping rural populations with modern fabrication skills. Through real-world examples and interactive insights, participants will l

title: Precision Farming : Introduction to Open-Source GPS-RTK precision positioning solutions

speakers.title: Nicolas Kaufmann
speakers.description: Nicolas Kaufmann is Fab Manager at AgriLab, an open Innovation center in Agriculture and a FabLab in a Farm related to the polytechnical campus with large agricultural facilities & agricultural experimental fields in Beauvais, France since 2022. He start his journey in the FabLab ecosystem during his indstrial design studies at ENSAAMA in Paris in 2016 and worked in several workshop before joining AgriLab. Prototyping teacher for a bachelor's degree in Agrotechnology and embedded systems, Nicolas is an IOT and robotics applied to agriculture enthousiast.

extendedProps.description: Dive into GPS-RTK for centimetre-level accuracy and the global Centipede network. Configure and test your own GPS-RTK rover and explore self-driving agricultural robots. Perfect for tech enthusiasts and farmers seeking to innovate. Gain practical skills and insights into the future of precision agriculture. Join us to lead the way in sustainable farming !

title: Foodshift Pathways and Remix the School: Make, Learn, and Rethink Sustainability with Biomaterials i

speakers.title: Julia Leirado
speakers.description: Julia Leirado is an architect based in Barcelona interested in educational innovation and how new technologies and digital fabrication tools can intersect with traditional education curriculum and their impact on improving the learning experience. She holds a Graduate Diploma in Architecture from the Polytechnic University of Catalonia (ETSAB), a master’s in Project Management (EAE), and a Fab Academy diploma in digital fabrication from the Fab Lab Network.

extendedProps.description: This hands-on workshop is for K-12 educators and fab lab practitioners to integrate sustainability and circularity into teaching. Using international case studies from Foodshift Pathways and Remix the School, participants will explore food waste-based biomaterials to make environmental concepts tangible. Attendees will create biomaterials, engage in reflection, and leave with a recipe book and a three-session model for introducing biomaterials in the classroom.

title: From Prishtina to Prague – A Robotic Journey

speakers.title: Dea Basholli
speakers.description: Dea is a mentor at BONEVET Prishtina, a leading makerspace and educational organization in Kosovo, where she has been empowering young minds for four years. With a background in Psychology, she is passionate about fostering hands-on learning experiences that allow children and youth to engage in practical, creative problem-solving. As a supporter of STEAM education, Dea is dedicated to shaping the next generation of innovators by providing them with the tools and opportunities to explore, create, and learn through technology and collaboration.

extendedProps.description:
Building and Programming the mBot Robot
Participants will begin by assembling the mBot robot, an educational tool developed by Makeblock that introduces children to electronics, mechanics, and basic programming. After building the robot, participants will use mBlock software to program it for basic movement and light/sound responses.

Programming the Maqueen Robot – A Journey from Prishtina to Czechia
In the second part of the workshop, participants will use the Maqueen robot to simulate a visual journey from Prishtina to Brno, Prague, or the MakerTrain. They will design a custom map, representing this symbolic trip, and program the robot to follow a black line path across the map using sensors and condition-based logic.

This workshop is suitable for children aged 8–12, and we welcome up to 10 participants per session. It is ideal for young learners eager to explore robotics and express their creativity.

title: Mobile FabLabs showcase

speakers.title: Berlin University Alliance (BUA) Mobile Lab
speakers.description: The BUA Mobile Lab is a trailer equipped with open-source digital tools like 3D printers, laser cutters, and CNC machines. It promotes open-source hardware and community building at Berlin universities by enabling rapid prototyping and collaborative creation. Serving as a hub for innovation, it supports sharing reproducible designs via platforms like GitHub, bridging digital fabrication with open-source development practices.

speakers.title: FabLab University Truck
speakers.description: The Mobile FabLab is a traveling workshop bringing cutting-edge technology to students aged 13-19. Operating mainly in the South Moravian Region, it has reached as far as Sarajevo. Through hands-on technology workshops, students explore 3D printing, laser cutting, milling, robotics, and plotting. Equipped with advanced digital tools, the FabLab fosters creativity, problem-solving, and innovation, making STEM education more accessible and engaging.

speakers.title: EDU.Lab
speakers.description: The EDU .lab is Škoda Auto’s cutting‑edge mobile laboratory — truck and trailer combo that brings hands‑on STEM and digital fabrication directly to schools and the public across Czechia and Slovakia. Targeting pupils in upper primary and secondary school, this mobile lab aims to spark curiosity in robotics, AI, VR/AR, 3D‑printing and more — with afternoon slots open to families and general visitors. By the end of 2025, EDU .lab will have visited all Czech regions and major Slovak cities.

speakers.title: Fab Mobile by Fab Lab Iasi
speakers.description: Fab Mobile aims to give access to digital production education for vulnerable communities of youngsters located in the rural areas of Iași region.

speakers.title: Clay Jet
speakers.description: Piaggio APE that is transformed into a mobile clay 3D print lab in cooperation with JetClay.

speakers.title: AWO Makerkutsche
speakers.description: In the structurally weak area around Lübbenau in the Spreewald, the team introduces young people to digital and manual technologies – from model making and screen printing to Arduino programming and 3D printing. The organization is firmly rooted in the local community and also maintains cross-border collaborations with Polish institutions. The "Makerkutsche" itself is a trailer that the team, together with students from the local technical school, has converted into a mobile lab.

extendedProps.summary: A one-of-a-kind showcase of mobile FabLabs from across Europe – ready to explore, inside and out.
extendedProps.description: Step inside the world of wheels and makers! Explore a unique lineup of mobile FabLabs from across Europe – from compact vans to full-size trailers, each one packed with digital fabrication tools. Meet the teams behind them, hear their stories, and get hands-on with the machines they carry. It’s a moving lab experience you don’t want to miss!

title: Reshaping Fashion with Digital Fabrication & Biomaterials

speakers.title: Batoul al-Rashdan
speakers.description: I am an award-winning FashionTech specialist in sustainable fashion and digital fabrication, merging technology, biomaterials, and computational design to reshape fashion’s future. As the founder of Studio B.O.R., I pioneer 3D printing, parametric design, and biofabrication, transforming waste into avant-garde wearables. My work has been exhibited in 17+ countries, including Paris and NYC Fashion Weeks, and recognized by FTA, Taipei Design, and WDO Sustainability Awards. I also teach globally, including at MIT, driving innovation in sustainability and digital craftsmanship.

extendedProps.description: This talk explores the intersection of digital fabrication, sustainable biomaterials, and computational design in fashion. Attendees will gain insights into how 3D printing, parametric modeling, and biofabrication transform waste into avant-garde wearables. The session will highlight real-world applications, challenges, and future opportunities in FashionTech, bridging technology with environmental responsibility.

title: FreeCAD at 1.0: What's new, and what's next?

speakers.title: Chris Hennes
speakers.description: Chris Hennes is a Science, Technology, Engineering, Art & Math (STEAM) Specialist for Pioneer Library System in Central Oklahoma, USA, where he trains customers and library staff on the use of maker equipment ranging from 3D printers to floor looms. He has a PhD in aerospace engineering, studying rotorcraft noise for several years before deciding that humans were more interesting than helicopters and taking a job that lets him work with people to turn their ideas into physical reality. He is one of the maintainers of FreeCAD, the open-source 3D parametric design software, and serves on the administrative board of the FreeCAD project association, a not-for-profit collecting and distributing donations from FreeCAD supporters.

title: Bio-based Electronics

speakers.title: David Cuartielles
speakers.description: Holds a PhD in Interaction Design and MSc in Telecommunications Engineering. He founded different laboratories at Malmo University as well as the open source platform Arduino. Teaches in the fields of interactive art, creative coding, design, and embedded technology. Has been awarded by / with the EU OS Academy, STINT and Ashoka Foundations, the Swedish SER, and the Sant Carles Medal of the Arts.

extendedProps.summary: Possible Path Towards Sustainable Electronics, Tackling PCB Manufacturing

title: Fast Talk

speakers.title: Francesco Siddi
speakers.bio: COO Blender & General Manager Blender Studio
speakers.description: Francesco Siddi discovered content creation as a teen in the early 2000s – mixing music, interactive visuals and community. Fell in love with open source, with Blender right at the heart of it all. He studied Industrial Design, launched a multimedia and 3D visualization studio, and in 2012 joined Blender’s Tears of Steel as a 3D artist. Today, Francesco leads as Blender Studio’s General Manager and Blender COO. Since 2024, he also serves on the Blender Foundation board as secretary. Passionate about creativity, collaboration and keeping Blender open for everyone.

title: From Fab Lab, to Fab City to Full Stack

speakers.title: Josefina Nano
speakers.description: Josefina Nano is a Design Researcher and Architect from Argentina, currently based in Menorca. With a Master's in Design for Emergent Futures from IAAC/ELISAVA, she specializes in systemic design, research design, material innovation, mobility, architecture, and urbanism. Passionate about distributed innovation and learning, Josefina explores new ways to foster knowledge-sharing and collaboration across networks. Her work spans multiple disciplines, including editorial projects, art direction, public tenders, academia, and sustainable housing. Committed to transformative change, she continuously seeks new experiences and strategies to challenge paradigms and drive meaningful impact through design.

speakers.title: Kate Armstrong
speakers.description: Kate Armstrong is a strategist, educator and community builder working across design, technology and ecology. As CEO of Fab City Foundation, she leads a global network of 50+ cities shifting toward regenerative, locally productive systems. She also directs Interspecies Internet, advancing interspecies communication through research and public programs, including work at the Santa Fe Institute. Kate has led EU research projects on design, shaped cultural programs across Europe and Australia, and taught at IAAC and contributed to numerous publications. She’s known for making complex ideas accessible, fostering dialogue on community resilience, sustainability and more-than-human futures.

speakers.title: Daniele Ingrassia
speakers.description: Daniele Ingrassia is the founder of InMachines, a company focused on open source digital fabrication machines based in Germany. With a background in computer science, he is developing open hardware and has accumulated a long list of Fab Lab projects. Daniele has developed several open source electronics and machinery such as the satshakit boards, a dual source laser cutter (Laserduo) and a large format 3D printer (BigFDM). Currently developing a full Fab Lab inventory of replicable machines called Open Lab Starter Kit, with his projects being replicated in many other countries, Daniele held several fabrication and machine building workshops around the world.

speakers.title: Steffen Pabst
speakers.description: Steffen Pabst is an educator and facilitator, with a background in sustainability economics and intercultural management. Steffen focusses on explorative learning experiences and is passionate about shaping our common future with innovative education methods, like Serious Gaming and Design Thinking. By empowering students and professionals alike to change their disciplinary perspectives, foster interdisciplinary thinking and develop transdisciplinary solutions to real-world problems, he thrives to make an impact on reaching our shared sustainability development goals. Steffen Pabst is a member of the FAB.Region Project in the Berg City Triangle in Germany, associated with the Startup Center of the University of Wuppertal, Germany. He is also a visiting lecturer at the Carl von Ossietzky University of Oldenburg and a referee at the Center for Radicalization Prevention in Nature Conservation (FARN) for Friends of Nature Germany.

extendedProps.summary: This panel discussion brings together innovators and changemakers in the Fab community who are utilising the Full Stack methodology to bring about change in their cities and regions.

title: Keynotes, panels, talks (Emerging Technology)

speakers.title: Neil Gershenfeld
speakers.bio: Director of MIT's Center for Bits and Atoms
speakers.description:
Prof. Neil Gershenfeld is the Director of MIT's Center for Bits and Atoms, where his unique laboratory is breaking down boundaries between the digital and physical worlds, from pioneering quantum computing to digital fabrication to the Internet of Things. Technology from his lab has been seen and used in settings including New York's Museum of Modern Art and rural Indian villages, the White House and the World Economic Forum, inner-city community centers and automobile safety systems, Las Vegas shows and Sami herds.

He is the author of numerous technical publications, patents, and books including Designing Reality, Fab, When Things Start To Think, The Nature of Mathematical Modeling, and The Physics of Information Technology, and has been featured in media such as The New York Times, The Economist, NPR, CNN, and PBS. He is a Fellow of the American Physical Society, has been named one of Scientific American's 50 leaders in science and technology, as one of 40 Modern-Day Leonardos by the Museum of Science and Industry, one of Popular Mechanic's 25 Makers, has been selected as a CNN/Time/Fortune Principal Voice, and by Prospect/Foreign Policy as one of the top 100 public intellectuals.

He's been called the intellectual father of the maker movement, founding a growing global network of over one thousand fab labs that provide widespread access to prototype tools for personal fabrication, directing the Fab Academy for distributed research and education in the principles and practices of digital fabrication, and chairing the Fab Foundation. Dr. Gershenfeld has a BA in Physics with High Honors from Swarthmore College, a Ph.D. in Applied Physics from Cornell University, honorary doctorates from Swarthmore College, Strathclyde University and the University of Antwerp, was a Junior Fellow of the Harvard University Society of Fellows, and a member of the research staff at Bell Labs.

speakers.title: Chris Hennes
speakers.description: Chris Hennes is a Science, Technology, Engineering, Art & Math (STEAM) Specialist for Pioneer Library System in Central Oklahoma, USA, where he trains customers and library staff on the use of maker equipment ranging from 3D printers to floor looms. He has a PhD in aerospace engineering, studying rotorcraft noise for several years before deciding that humans were more interesting than helicopters and taking a job that lets him work with people to turn their ideas into physical reality. He is one of the maintainers of FreeCAD, the open-source 3D parametric design software, and serves on the administrative board of the FreeCAD project association, a not-for-profit collecting and distributing donations from FreeCAD supporters.

speakers.title: Judith Kreukels
speakers.bio: Head of Programmes, Women in Quantum Development
speakers.description: Judith Kreukels is an Advisor on Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion at the Dutch Scientific Research Institutes (NWO-I) and Head of Programmes for We in Quantum Development in the Netherlands. She has extensive experience in advancing Equity Diversity and Inclusion, particularly in quantum, leading initiatives for systemic change in academia and the non-profit sector. Her 10+ years of experience includes developing international consortia and policies supporting underrepresented groups, including LGBTQI+ communities, migrants, and women. She has worked across Argentina, Australia, Eastern Europe, and Central Asia.

speakers.title: David Cuartielles
speakers.description: Holds a PhD in Interaction Design and MSc in Telecommunications Engineering. He founded different laboratories at Malmo University as well as the open source platform Arduino. Teaches in the fields of interactive art, creative coding, design, and embedded technology. Has been awarded by / with the EU OS Academy, STINT and Ashoka Foundations, the Swedish SER, and the Sant Carles Medal of the Arts.

speakers.title: Leo McElroy
speakers.description: Leo McElroy builds technology for making things, for learning, and for learning by making things. He has designed, developed, and deployed dozens of open-source tools for digital fabrication and creative coding environments. Currently, he is developing fundamental CAD infrastructure to power the next generation of design technology. He has taught digital fabrication to people of all ages, from children to senior citizens, and has investigated makerspaces across the globe during a year-long tour of maker communities.

speakers.title: Jan Kučera
speakers.bio: Metrologist and deputy director of CMI Inspectorate Prague
speakers.description: Jan Kučera is deeply merged in the world of precise electrical measurements, development of new instruments and measurement techniques. Jan's work covers area from quantum effects at deep space temperatures with billionth accuracies to tabletop multimeters for daily use in your fab labs. He works at Czech Metrology Institute at primary department for electrical quantities, takes a care about national standard of electrical resistance, develops measurement tools to bring electrical DC and AC metrology to the next level. Beside that, he is a part time researcher at CTU in Prague, FEE, Department of measurement. Jan enjoys cooperation between scientists around the world and work with students, leading to new ideas how to measure both simple and difficult tasks more accurate.

speakers.title: Pieter Hijma
speakers.description: Pieter Hijma is a self-employed software developer and researcher focusing on open source software.  His background is Computer Science and has a PhD on the topic of High Performance Computing.  He is an open source software and hardware enthusiast with an interest in how to document open source hardware.  After a research project on automatically generating assembly manuals for open source hardware, he deepened his knowledge of documenting open source hardware by completing the Fab Academy program at Waag Futurelab Amsterdam. This inspired him to dedicate his time to develop FreeCAD to 1) make designing physical products highly accessible to the public in general and 2) to ensure that open source hardware can be designed with open tools.

speakers.title: Gonzalo Guarner
speakers.bio: Digital Twin developer with physics background
speakers.description: Gonzalo is a software developer with experience in Digital Twin systems and a background in physics and complex systems, currently based between Berlin and Barcelona. He specializes in 3D software development using engines like Unity, Unreal, and MATLAB, where math and logic can come to life through visual simulation. Gonzalo is currently participating in Fab Academy, where he's exploring digital fabrication and thoroughly enjoying 3D modeling and electronic circuit production. Outside of work, he enjoys playing chess and attending live music events.

speakers.title: Francesco Siddi
speakers.bio: COO Blender & General Manager Blender Studio
speakers.description: Francesco Siddi discovered content creation as a teen in the early 2000s – mixing music, interactive visuals and community. Fell in love with open source, with Blender right at the heart of it all. He studied Industrial Design, launched a multimedia and 3D visualization studio, and in 2012 joined Blender’s Tears of Steel as a 3D artist. Today, Francesco leads as Blender Studio’s General Manager and Blender COO. Since 2024, he also serves on the Blender Foundation board as secretary. Passionate about creativity, collaboration and keeping Blender open for everyone.

extendedProps.summary: Explore how FabLabs are embracing open engineering and making quantum technologies more accessible.
extendedProps.description:
The Emerging Technology track at FAB25 brings two powerful themes into focus: Open Engineering and Quantum in FabLabs. Both represent bold frontiers in how we build, share, and shape the future of technology – and both are closer than you think.

Open Engineering is redefining the way we design and build. Rooted in transparency, collaboration, and open-source practices, it empowers makers, technologists and communities to openly share tools, designs and knowledge. The result? Faster experimentation, shorter development cycles, and more inclusive and sustainable innovation. In this track, we’ll explore how open-source hardware and platforms are enabling anyone, anywhere, to create impact – together.

We’ll also take a bold step into the world of quantum technologies – often seen as distant or theoretical – and ask: how can we bring quantum closer to people, especially in FabLabs? You’ll get an introduction to this exciting field, along with inspiring examples of projects already bridging the gap between quantum theory and real-world maker applications.
