speakers.title: Fadia Elgharib
speakers.description: Fadia is a Berlin-based digital media activist and corporate lawyer originally from Cairo, Egypt. She works with the re:publica team to create and curate the Makerspace. Fadia has been part of the Global Innovation Gathering (GIG) since 2018 and supports managing a community of over 250 members worldwide.

speakers.title: Nantjen Kuesel
speakers.description: From 2019 to 2024, Nantjen was responsible for curating and designing program sessions on sustainability—on and around the stages—while also overseeing the "Economy & Work" track. Since 2022, she has been in charge of sustainability management at re:publica Berlin and re:publica GmbH, and currently serves as program lead for re:publica x Reeperbahn Festival in Hamburg.

extendedProps.description:
re:publica, Europe’s largest festival on digital societies, has been a story of success for over 20 years. At the heart of this 18,000-person event is the Makerspace—a hands-on, international community hub. Curated and built by the Global Innovation Gathering (GIG), the space brings together makers, tinkerers, and change-makers from across the globe.
In this session, we’ll explore why this format of exchange is so impactful, how it fosters international collaboration, and how the Makerspace cont

title: Open Source Circular Fashion

speakers.title: Fabricademy Textile and Technology Academy
speakers.description: Our mission is to (re)shape and (re)define the implications and applications of technology in the textile and clothing industry, from the fashion sector to the upcoming wearable market. The program offers a broad overview of the state of the art of the current industry and builds on “learning by doing” methodologies, tackling themes like personal fabrication, distributed manufacturing, industry 4.0, wearable technology, biofabrication, assistive technologies and sustainability.

extendedProps.description: Learn how to create laser cut modular garments and accessories for zero waste fashion design! Check our platform > oscircularfashion.com and join the workshop to practice from 2D vector design to laser cutting textiles

title: Who owns your CAD tools and why does it matter?

speakers.title: Leo McElroy
speakers.description: Leo McElroy builds technology for making things, for learning, and for learning by making things. He has designed, developed, and deployed dozens of open-source tools for digital fabrication and creative coding environments. Currently, he is developing fundamental CAD infrastructure to power the next generation of design technology. He has taught digital fabrication to people of all ages, from children to senior citizens, and has investigated makerspaces across the globe during a year-long tour of maker communities.

extendedProps.summary: What I learned building dozens of open-source tools

title: How to launch your space startup? When rockets meet 3D printers

speakers.title: Adéla Boráňová
speakers.description: déla is an Incubation Manager at the Prague branch of ESA BIC Czech Republic, part of the ESA Commercialisation Gateway – the world’s largest network of space-focused incubators. She specializes in supporting startups developing breakthrough space technologies with the potential to disrupt markets. Adéla has a background in project and business management. Her experience includes fostering partnerships, overseeing business operations, and implementing and optimizing internal processes.

extendedProps.description: Ever wondered how space technologies land in your everyday maker projects? Join us for an exclusive behind-the-scenes journey into Czech Republic's thriving space ecosystem, where rockets meet 3D printers and satellites inspire your next fabrication adventure.

title: FAB25 Maker Coin: Solder It. Blink It. Wear It.

extendedProps.summary: Come and finish your FAB25 Maker Coin by soldering everything together.
extendedProps.description:
Grab a soldering iron, the PCB from your welcome pack, and components from the table – and let's bring your FAB25 Maker Coin to life in just about 20 minutes!

  • By choosing the resistor values, you get to control how fast your badge blinks. It’s not just about soldering – it’s about making it your own.
  • Never soldered before? No worries. Our friendly instructors are here to guide you step by step. Whether you're a first-timer or a seasoned maker, this is a fun and hands-on way to kick off your FAB25 experience.
  • Wear your coin proudly all week – especially during networking sessions. Attach it to your plastic pouch or use the included magnet for a clean, stylish look.

title: Day Start
Bridging Creativity and Capability – One Spark at a Time

speakers.title: Salomé Bibilashvili
speakers.bio: XTOOL Brand Manager for EU and UK
speakers.description: Salomé Bibilashvili brings over 15 years of experience in leading marketing and PR roles within international companies, where she has overseen brand development and global expansion strategies. Her career reflects a strong track record in driving international growth, building strategic partnerships, and managing high-impact campaigns across diverse markets.

title: Keynote

speakers.title: Neil Gershenfeld
speakers.bio: Director of MIT's Center for Bits and Atoms
speakers.description:
Prof. Neil Gershenfeld is the Director of MIT's Center for Bits and Atoms, where his unique laboratory is breaking down boundaries between the digital and physical worlds, from pioneering quantum computing to digital fabrication to the Internet of Things. Technology from his lab has been seen and used in settings including New York's Museum of Modern Art and rural Indian villages, the White House and the World Economic Forum, inner-city community centers and automobile safety systems, Las Vegas shows and Sami herds.

He is the author of numerous technical publications, patents, and books including Designing Reality, Fab, When Things Start To Think, The Nature of Mathematical Modeling, and The Physics of Information Technology, and has been featured in media such as The New York Times, The Economist, NPR, CNN, and PBS. He is a Fellow of the American Physical Society, has been named one of Scientific American's 50 leaders in science and technology, as one of 40 Modern-Day Leonardos by the Museum of Science and Industry, one of Popular Mechanic's 25 Makers, has been selected as a CNN/Time/Fortune Principal Voice, and by Prospect/Foreign Policy as one of the top 100 public intellectuals.

He's been called the intellectual father of the maker movement, founding a growing global network of over one thousand fab labs that provide widespread access to prototype tools for personal fabrication, directing the Fab Academy for distributed research and education in the principles and practices of digital fabrication, and chairing the Fab Foundation. Dr. Gershenfeld has a BA in Physics with High Honors from Swarthmore College, a Ph.D. in Applied Physics from Cornell University, honorary doctorates from Swarthmore College, Strathclyde University and the University of Antwerp, was a Junior Fellow of the Harvard University Society of Fellows, and a member of the research staff at Bell Labs.

speakers.description: Judith Kreukels is an Advisor on Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion at the Dutch Scientific Research Institutes (NWO-I) and Head of Programmes for We in Quantum Development in the Netherlands. She has extensive experience in advancing Equity Diversity and Inclusion, particularly in quantum, leading initiatives for systemic change in academia and the non-profit sector. Her 10+ years of experience includes developing international consortia and policies supporting underrepresented groups, including LGBTQI+ communities, migrants, and women. She has worked across Argentina, Australia, Eastern Europe, and Central Asia.

speakers.title: Chris Hennes
speakers.description: Chris Hennes is a Science, Technology, Engineering, Art & Math (STEAM) Specialist for Pioneer Library System in Central Oklahoma, USA, where he trains customers and library staff on the use of maker equipment ranging from 3D printers to floor looms. He has a PhD in aerospace engineering, studying rotorcraft noise for several years before deciding that humans were more interesting than helicopters and taking a job that lets him work with people to turn their ideas into physical reality. He is one of the maintainers of FreeCAD, the open-source 3D parametric design software, and serves on the administrative board of the FreeCAD project association, a not-for-profit collecting and distributing donations from FreeCAD supporters.

title: 3D Printing on Fabrics: Merging Digital Fabrication & Fashion

speakers.title: Batoul al-Rashdan
speakers.description: I am an award-winning FashionTech specialist in sustainable fashion and digital fabrication, merging technology, biomaterials, and computational design to reshape fashion’s future. As the founder of Studio B.O.R., I pioneer 3D printing, parametric design, and biofabrication, transforming waste into avant-garde wearables. My work has been exhibited in 17+ countries, including Paris and NYC Fashion Weeks, and recognized by FTA, Taipei Design, and WDO Sustainability Awards. I also teach globally, including at MIT, driving innovation in sustainability and digital craftsmanship.

extendedProps.description: This hands-on workshop introduces the innovative technique of 3D printing directly onto fabrics, bridging digital fabrication with fashion. Participants will explore material compatibility, parametric design, and adhesion techniques, gaining practical skills to integrate customized textures, flexible structures, and smart textiles into fashion applications. No prior experience is required—just curiosity and creativity!

title: Learn how to fire breath (Just kidding: Make LED poi)

speakers.title: Tex Kang
speakers.description:
Current manager of University of Pennsylvania's library and makerspace 
Former manager of Fab Lab Seoul and Bio Lab Seoul 
Organizer of the Philadelphia Maker Faire 
Burning Man designer and fire performer 
Has two dogs named 'light' and 'speed' in Tolkien elvish

extendedProps.description:
Learn how to make LED poi and learn flow arts.
Using ESP32S3 (or whatever Fab Foundation prefers) learn to make LED poi that you can program. Then learn how to use the poi to have a fun spin session when it gets darker. If the organization allows it, I'll also bring my fire props for a small demonstration.

title: Making your own Prusa-farm software on octoprint

speakers.title: Henry Clasen
speakers.description: Student in computer science, been working in the FabLab as a student for 2 years, participating in STEM-camps and keeping the lab open.

extendedProps.description: In FabLab-Leuven there are 30+ Prusa's that used to run on a Rais3d farm. To optimize printing capacity and efficiency, we wrote our own software based on PrusaSlicer. This software is running in the lab from january and now it's time to evaluate and get input from the community.

title: 3d printed products: from Ideation to Commercialization

speakers.title: Alexander Iulius Vasilescu
speakers.description:
25 year old nerd, with 10 years of printing, 4 of which professionally. Proud babysitter of 3 FDM and 2 resin printers. =)
- Robotics BEng from Plymouth UK
- nearly 2 years of e-Nable volunteering as designer for hand prosthetics. (enablingthefuture.org, hub.e-nable.com)
- founder of 3Dapi, a 3d printing startup that connects designers with print farms

extendedProps.description: Go from idea to functional business in one session! Learn how to split creative and manufacturing roles, optimise for production costs, and validate market demand. Through hands-on exercises, you’ll leave with a manufacturable product prototype and a clear path to sales - whether you’re a designer or an engineer.

title: Fab City Awards: Winners Roundtable

speakers.title: Josephina Nano
speakers.description: Josefina Nano is a Design Researcher and Architect from Argentina, currently based in Barcelona. With a Master's in Design for Emergent Futures from IAAC/ELISAVA, she specializes in systemic design, research design, material innovation, mobility, architecture, and urbanism. Passionate about distributed innovation and learning, Josefina explores new ways to foster knowledge-sharing and collaboration across networks. Her work spans multiple disciplines, including editorial projects, art direction, public tenders, academia, and sustainable housing.

extendedProps.description: The Fab City Awards: Winners Roundtable offers a glimpse of the wide variety of impactful projects implemented by the Fab City Global Network. Attendees will hear from successful projects from different geographical localities working at the intersection of distributed innovation, climate justice and community engagement.

title: Measuring What Matters: Rethinking Impact and Sustainability in Fab Labs and Fab Cities

speakers.title: Victoria Wenzelmann
speakers.description: Victoria Wenzelmann is a research associate at the New Production Institute in Hamburg, focusing on distributed manufacturing and the role of makerspaces in resilient, localized innovation. She worked in 14 countries, e.g. initiating the Tolocar mobile makerspace project in Ukraine, or consulting on making within women-centered innovation ecosystems in Cameroon and Tanzania. Victoria has been connecting ecosystems to promote inclusive, sustainable, and community-driven innovation for over a decade, including as a co-founder of GIG, a network of grassroots innovators and hubs in 30+ countries.

extendedProps.description:
This workshop explores how Fab Labs and Fab Cities can self-assess
impact and sustainability in ways that empower rather than control.
Moving beyond external benchmarks, we use mapping tools, games, and case
studies to reflect on localization, sustainable transformation, and what
"impact" really means in our own contexts. Participants will leave with
practical tools, shared insights, and ideas for how to make their
contributions visible to themselves, their communities, and potential
allies.

title: Working Group: Fab Regions & cities in Dialogue for Sustainable Transitions

speakers.title: Adriana Cabrera
speakers.description: Adriana Cabrera is a designer and media artist working at the intersection of innovation, digital fabrication, and sustainable material ecologies. She co-leads Fabcare.network and lectures at Fabricademy in the field of soft robotics, promoting inclusive health and care through open innovation. At CSCP, she supports Fab City strategies for circular and co-creative regional development in Germany’s Fab Region Bergische Triangle.

extendedProps.description: This working group connects Fab Cities and Fab Regions to share experiences, identify challenges, and explore ways to build sustainable, resilient, and circular ecosystems. Through open dialogue, we will reflect on collaboration, local production, and how these initiatives can thrive beyond funding programs. Join us to co-create future pathways at city and regional levels.

title: Livecoding Machines for Creativity and Realtime Expression

speakers.title: Char Stiles
speakers.description: Char Stiles is a computational artist, educator, and programmer. She works creatively in the lower levels of graphical computational systems & makes jokes about how computers work. She is currently at the MIT Media Lab's Future Sketches group researching the future of creative coding in performance. She is a part of the Livecode.nyc collective & she co-founded Hex House, a maker space, artist studio and event space in East Williamsburg, New York.

extendedProps.description: Livecoding is the practice of writing and compiling code on-the-fly in front of an audience to make expressive realtime music and visuals, but can a machine be incorporated in this loop? In this workshop you will get a historical overview of how artists have incorporated realtime robotic companions in artistic performances, a demonstration of livecoding music and visuals, and a simulation of sending G-code commands realtime, and a kit of resources to try to livecode your own machines at home!

title: Fiber laser engraving of circuit boards for tiny components

speakers.title: Quentin Bolsee
speakers.description: Quentin Bolsée is a postdoctoral researcher at the MIT Center for Bits and Atoms. His research focuses on rapid prototyping of electronics, modular machine building and computer vision applications.

extendedProps.description: In this workshop, we will use a low-cost fiber laser system (xTool F1) to engrave circuit boards with extremely small features, enabling the use of tiny electronics components (e.g. QFN, BGA). We will also look at soldering strategies for those components, through a mixed use of soldering irons and hot air stations.

title: FAB JUNK - Speculation on the Fab Network 300 years in the future.

speakers.title: Ricardo Marques
speakers.description:
A Designer from Portugal, he founded a design consultancy company called Eltryus in 2020. More recently he's looking to support new deep-tech projects/research.
His connection to the Fab Network has been growing since enrolling in Fab Academy in 2022. In 2025 he became an instructor at Fab Lab Benfica.
He is passionate about understanding how things work and focuses on facilitating the development of new ideas anyway they need.

speakers.title: Rico Kanthatham
speakers.description: Rico is the Founder of Skylabworkshop in Kamakura, Japan, an independently run community fablab that provides STEAM learning workshops for children and young adults. Rico is also a Fabacademy and Fabricademy instructor, a co-creator of the Fab All-in program, and a coordinating member of the Global Kids team.

speakers.title: Enrico Bassi
speakers.description: Enrico Bassi is a designer, maker, professor, and innovation consultant. He managed Italy's first Fab Lab, Fablab Italia, FabLab Torino, supported by Officine Arduino, and then Opendot. He has been instructor and global evaluator in fab academy. He co-founded Fabcare.network and careables.org, two international projects on co-design open solutions with people with disabilities. He has taught digital fabrication and design at universities in Italy, Germany, Poland, and Switzerland and has participated in projects and events in over 35 countries, from the USA to Bhutan, from Djibouti to Kenya.

speakers.title: Renato Avila Claro
speakers.description: Architect and digital fabrication specialist from Ecuador. He is the Director of the UDLA FabLab and Scientific Director at Master in Advanced Architectural Design at Universidad de Las Américas (UDLA) in Quito, where he integrates technology, education, and design to foster innovation. With a background in architecture and co-founder of PDG–Pata de Gallo and Automater 3DP, he works at the intersection of digital making, creative industries, and open-source tools. He is passionate about empowering students and local communities through accessible design and hands-on learning.

extendedProps.description:
Over the last year a group of volunteers, under the tutelage of Alex McDowell, ran a JUNK exercise within the Fab Network about the Fab Network.
The JUNK World Building exercise is about looking at what happens to civilization 300 years after a crisis event destroys our way of life.

If Civilization goes how do Fab Labs survive? Are they still Fab Labs?

We'll try to answer these and more questions about what the Fab Network may look like in the far future!

title: Art & Design Expo

speakers.title: Robin Baumgarten
speakers.description:
Robin is an award-winning experimental hardware game developer and interactive installation artist based in Berlin. After researching Artificial Intelligence in Games and working on mobile games, he is now fully focused on creating playful interactive installations that straddle the divide of games and art, such as Line Wobbler and Wobble Garden. His most recent projects also involve a scientific aspect: Quantum Garden and its larger sequel, Quantum Jungle, both visualise quantum physics in a playful yet scientifically accurate manner.

Quantum Jungle

Type: Interactive installation
Photo Credits: Robin Baumgarten

An interactive installation that transforms the complexity of quantum physics into a sensory experience of sound and light. As visitors move through the space, their presence triggers shifts in rhythm, pattern, and color – reflecting the uncertainty and entanglement at the heart of quantum mechanics.

speakers.title: Kryštof Brůha
speakers.description:
Kryštof Brůha is an intermedia artist living and working in Prague, whose work explores specific environments – from the natural to the technological and virtual. He focuses on their influences and their overall transformation over time. He allows us to look at phenomena hidden from our perception, for example through macro and micro mapping of the environment, and through such scanning of space and time through artistic artefacts he offers the viewer a new view of reality. His media include installations, kinetic sculptures, generative videos and experimental films. In the development process, he uses scientific methods, specially designed algorithms, generative design and machine learning. In implementation, Brůha creates custom tools using Rapid Prototyping and CNC technology that are computer controlled. All these technological processes are then reflected in the aesthetics of his works.

Audire Fluctus

Type: Interactive light sculpture
Photo Credits: Tomáš Slavík & Dušan Vondra

An interactive sculpture that visualizes the invisible world of electromagnetic waves. Using a custom-developed medium called Spektraflux, the piece responds to ambient electromagnetic activity –⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ such as signals from electrical grids and communication devices –⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ by transforming them into dynamic light patterns.

speakers.title: Adam Cigler & Petr Vacek
speakers.description:
Petr Vacek and Adam Cigler are Czech architects who synergize a creative artistic approach with programming, robotics, digital fabrication and traditional craftsmanship. Their collaboration arose during their studies at the Faculty of Arts and Architecture in Liberec and, thanks to a common fondness for advanced technology, science, space or philosophy, crystallized into an aesthetically appealing freeform creative experiment.

Reflexion

Type: Kinetic sculpture
Photo Credits: Ray Baseley

A hypnotic kinetic sculpture made of dozens of independently moving mirrors. Each mirror is precisely controlled by servo motors and arranged in clusters, forming a responsive light object driven by a network of Raspberry Pi microcomputers. Made with 3D-printed components and CNC-cut panels, Reflexion blends digital control with analog beauty – reflecting not just light, but movement, complexity and the invisible choreography of data.

speakers.title: Fabricademy Collective
speakers.description:
Fabricademy is a global, distributed educational program that explores the intersection of textiles, digital fabrication, and biology. Over 6 months, participants dive into a hands-on journey that blends traditional crafts with cutting-edge technologies like 3D printing, electronics, wearable tech, and biofabrication. It's a space for experimentation, collaboration, and redefining the future of fashion and materials through open-source tools and sustainable innovation. Fabricademy is hosted by Fab Labs and innovation spaces worldwide, making it a truly international and community-driven experience.

Fabricademy Exhibition

Type: Exhibition of selected models
Photo Credits: Barbara Rakovska, Fabricademy

Discover the 11th annual showcase of innovation, sustainability, and digital craftsmanship by Fabricademy participants and local Czech designers. This exhibition highlights groundbreaking projects at the intersection of technology, textiles, and biology. It features a curated selection of forward-thinking designs that merge digital fabrication, electronics, and biofabrication with sustainable materials and techniques.

speakers.title:
Lukáš Dřevjaný,
Luboš Zbranek & others
speakers.description:
**Lukáš Dřevjaný** is a visual artist, lighting designer, and mentor of creative technologies. He combines light, space, and interactivity in live performances, light installations, large-scale projections, and theatre performances. He primarily works under his brand oxoo. Among other things, he mentors technology in the Art&Digital Lab in Hall 40 at the Holešovice Market and organizes technology workshops. **Luboš Zbranek** is a 3D graphic, VJ, video, and light designer. During his studies of Interactive Media Theory at Masaryk University, he has focused on creating interactive environments and deeper exploration of the human-machine, human-code relationship. By studying light design at JAMU, he expanded his scope to include light design, light art, and performance genres. He has collaborated visually on many techno and electronic music events, interactive installations, and theatre performances at home and abroad. **Other collaborators: David Minařík, Michal Mitro, Maurício Lobo**

Nexus

Type: Interactive light sculpture
Photo Credits: Viktor Schwing

The Nexus grows algorithmically in time and space. Like the rhizome, the mycelium, or human and non-human neural networks, the individual cells of this digital moss are constantly exchanging information. The body of the work is made up of 300 eight-legged acrylic cells, straddling all axes of space. The individual building elements are interconnected, functioning as a volumetric display.

extendedProps.summary: Explore how Czech and European artists are pushing the boundaries of art through digital making.
extendedProps.description:
Step into the Art & Design Expo – a curated collection of interactive installations, experimental objects, and inspiring works by Czech and European artists who blend creativity with cutting-edge fabrication tools.

– Meet the makers behind pieces like Quantum Jungle, Audire Fluctus, Reflexion, Nexus and the Fabricademy annual exhibition
– Discover how digital tools like CNCs, 3D printers, and laser cutters are used to create awe-inspiring works
– Take a walk through art that moves, reacts, glows – and definitely deserves a spot on your feed

Come take a look, snap some cool photos, and get inspired by what happens when art and digital fabrication collide.

title: GreenInCities Marketplace

speakers.title: Lars Petrova Taylor
speakers.description:
I’m Lars, Project Manager representing Fab City in the EU-funded GreenInCities project, part of the European Cities Mission. Our goal is to transform urban spaces using nature-based solutions and innovative technologies. By centring both human and non-human needs, we combine data-driven strategies with insights from neuroscience to co-create sustainable futures for climate adaptation and mitigation.

Beyond my role, I’m a maker at heart and currently pursuing a Master’s in Design for Distributed Innovation. I see myself as a local change-maker, dedicated to embedding impact in everything I do.

speakers.title: Guillem Camprodon
speakers.bio: Executive Director at Fab Lab Barcelona (IAAC) and Co-Director of the Master in Design for Emergent Futures
speakers.description: Guillem Camprodon (he/him) is a designer and technologist exploring how emerging technologies can empower communities and shape more equitable futures. He leads Fab Lab Barcelona at IAAC, a global reference in digital fabrication and civic innovation, and co-directs the Master in Design for Emergent Futures with IAAC and ELISAVA. Guillem also co-leads the Smart Citizen project and has contributed to numerous European research initiatives focused on data commons, regenerative design, and bottom-up innovation helping to scale Fab Lab Barcelona into a reference for applied action research.

extendedProps.description: This working group challenges FAB25 attendees to co-create impactful, inclusive urban partnerships through an interactive marketplace and rapid prototyping format. Participants will trade skills, resources, and ideas. They will then form synergistic teams, and prototype urban interventions, partnerships or events. Through dynamic matchmaking, real-time collaboration, and investment-style pitching, we’ll generate concrete, scalable solutions that extend beyond FAB25.

title: FABx Reimagined: Designing the Future of Our Global Gatherings

speakers.title: Norella Coronell
speakers.description: With expertise spanning the tech and nonprofit sectors. Norella is a seasoned event producer, as well as marketing, networking and currently the Communications Director of the Fab Foundation. Holding a Fab Academy Diploma in Digital Fabrication, a master’s degree in Project Management, and a Bachelor’s degree in Product Design. Norella brings a multidisciplinary perspective to every endeavor, combining creativity with strategic execution.

extendedProps.description: Join us for an open and dynamic session where we explore what’s next for FABx events—the heart of our global movement. From FABx Conferences to regional gatherings, this conversation will dive into how we can evolve our events to be more inclusive, sustainable, and impactful. Whether you're a past organizer, a future host, or a passionate participant, come shape the roadmap for the next decade of FABx.

title: ReeFab : towards meaningful and guided learning experiences on climate change for young adults.

speakers.title: Alexandre LICATA
speakers.description:
I've been a designer for 10 years and in charge of the FabLab at Cap Sciences in Bordeaux (France) for 3 years, a Science Culture Center, where I'm responsible for offering learning workshops for all publics, through making and design.
I discovered FabLabs in 2014 during my final-year design internship at Timelab in Ghent, Belgium. There I discovered that designers alone no longer had the skills and means to design or prototype new objects. So I learned the role of mediator designer, while exploring the design possibilities offered by rapid prototyping machines.

extendedProps.description: ReeFab is an Erasmus+ project developing climate learning experiences for young adults in FabLabs. At FAB25, we seek feedback, refine outcomes, and expand our network to sustain the project. This interactive session introduces our "atlas" -a collection of real-life insights- and a Guide to help FabLabs adapt our workshops and methods. Through guided brainstorming, participants will shape these resources and explore how climate education in makerspaces can have a lasting impact.

title: 3D printer Firmware Hacking

speakers.title: Michal Vaner
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: In this talk, we'll discuss how to build your own firmware for a Prusa 3D printer. Then, we'll show you how to load it onto the printer and explore some ways to fix things if they don't go as planned and how to debug them. All with tools readily available; no special hardware (except for the printer itself) is needed.

title: Multi-Material 3D Printing

speakers.title: Simon Doubek
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: Did you know there is a difference between multi-color and multi-material 3D printing? With machines like the Original Prusa XL, you can discover a whole new world of multi-material prints. Add flexible, rubber-like grips to objects printed with durable PETG and make your life easier with soluble supports. This and much more will be the focus of this segment.

title: Getting More out of Prusa Printers with GPIO Hackerboard

speakers.title: Michal Zoubek
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: This workshop serves as an introduction to the Prusa GPIO Hackerboard. You will learn how to use this clever little board to enhance your MK3.5, MK3.9, MK4, or Core One printer with new, handy functions. We will cover a few practical examples.

title: Prusa Connect and the Future of Remote Print Management

speakers.title: Vladimir Kaspar
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: Get ready for a journey into the world of advanced 3D printer control. We'll show you how we've evolved from archaic USB file transfers to a fully connected and remotely controllable ecosystem. Discover how Prusa Research continuously innovates to make 3D printing even more accessible and efficient.

title: PrusaSlicer Masterclass

speakers.title: Krystof Kohout
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: Go beyond the default slicing profiles! PrusaSlicer allows you to customize and optimize your prints in many ways. In this workshop, we will demonstrate various techniques for reducing filament use, customizing models with text and logos, and generating interesting surface textures.

title: 3D printing in Education

speakers.title: Stepan Feik
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: 3D printers are fantastic educational tools! Kids love bringing their ideas to life, gaining valuable hands-on experience. This not only sparks creativity and problem-solving skills, but also prepares them for a future where technology and innovation are key.

title: 3D Modelling for 3D printing with Solidworks

speakers.title: Carlos 3D
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description:
CAD and 3D printing = superpowers !
Come and discover how I use Solidworks and PRUSA printers to bring to life amazing objects inspired by pop culture and science fiction.
Using engineering for fun projects!
I will show you how mechanics and electronics allow me to bring these projects to life.

title: Polymer Development for 3D Printing

speakers.title: Adam Pinkner
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: Understanding the development of polymers for 3D printing helps us appreciate the technology's versatility and potential. From PLA and PETG to carbon-fiber-filled polymers and high-end engineering plastics, the range of materials continues to expand. This presentation will guide you through these material innovations and their real-world applications.

title: From Fablab to Hardware Startup Accelerator: The PrusaLab Evolution

speakers.title: Ondřej Kašpárek
speakers.description:
Ondřej Kašpárek is the Head of PrusaLab, one of the most advanced makerspaces in Europe, where creativity, technology, and community-driven innovation come together. In his role, he oversees the development and operations of a workshop that serves as a hub for experimentation and digital fabrication for makers, students, startups, and established companies alike.

Ondřej's work focuses on fostering innovation through shared production spaces where technologies like 3D printing, CNC milling, and laser cutting empower groundbreaking projects. His expertise spans design, prototyping, and implementing cutting-edge solutions that connect digital fabrication with practical applications.

His mission is to build bridges between technology, education, and industry while strengthening a maker community passionate about sustainable and open innovation. Under his leadership, PrusaLab has inspired countless projects, embodying the spirit of creativity and collaboration.

extendedProps.description:
Behind-the-scenes look at how PrusaLab, originally launched as a corporate fablab, evolved into a full-fledged hardware startup accelerator. Through pitches and a panel discussion with three founders, we will explore how the fablab infrastructure can be leveraged to support early-stage hardware innovation. Attendees will gain insights into the unique value of this approach, especially when compared to traditional startup programs, and learn how such a model can be replicated in other labs.

This one-hour session, moderated by Ondřej Kašpárek (Director of PrusaLab), presents a case study of how a company-based fablab can transform into a thriving hub for hardware startups. The program begins with short, 7-minute presentations from three selected startup founders who were part of the PrusaLab acceleration journey. Following the presentations, a panel discussion will explore their experiences in depth.

The discussion will focus on key topics:

  • What made the acceleration process within PrusaLab unique?
  • What specific support did the fablab environment enable?
  • How did the experience differ from other, software-oriented or generalist accelerators?

The panel will feature founders from three hardware startups that went through the PrusaLab acceleration program:

  • Posedla – Custom 3D-printed bike saddles combining performance and ergonomics. posedla.com
  • IRMA – Smart purse designed to support everyday safety routines. irmagard.com
  • Mirnix Hoceky – A Czech brand reinventing hockey accessories. mirnixhockey.com
    Each startup brings a different angle on product development, user testing, and scaling with hardware in mind.

title: Setting up a 3D Printing Business in Fab Labs

speakers.title: Rudolf Krcmar
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: Fab Labs offer a powerful launchpad for exciting new projects and 3D printers are one of the key elements of the entire operation. In this talk, you will learn about the best practices for setting up a 3D printing business: picking the right machines, scaling up, and managing your business model.

title: The Importance and Power of Parametric Design in 3D Printing

speakers.title: Carlos 3D
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: While making visually appealing, AI-driven models is in vogue, parametric design is far from outdated; in fact, it remains the standard in the manufacturing industry. In this talk, I will show you how to use parametric design to create an appealing "everyday product" and how to scale up 3D print production of products easily customizable with a single click!

title: Industrial 3D Printing & Advanced Materials (Prusa Pro HT90)

speakers.title: Vojtech Tambor
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: This talk is about how companies are using 3D printing in real industrial settings and where it truly makes a difference. It’s not just about the toughest materials or the flashiest machines, but often about the smart use of what’s already available. This is the kind of shift that quietly changes how things get done and where some of the most useful and surprising results emerge.

title: Injection Molding vs 3D Printing

speakers.title: Stepan Feik
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: We'll uncover how 3D printing excels in rapid prototyping, design freedom, and low-volume production, while injection molding dominates high-volume manufacturing with its cost-efficiency and material versatility. Discover how these powerful technologies work together to shape product development.

title: Photogrammetry: How to Scan Real-World Objects with Your Camera

speakers.title: Mikolas Zuza
speakers.description: Soon

speakers.title: David Schwarz
speakers.description: David Schwarz is known in the 3D printing world as "The Prusa Guy". A true master of his craft, David is more than just a highly skilled 3D designer—he’s an artist, an innovator, and a magician when it comes to taking raw prints and transforming them into stunning, lifelike creations. With an eye for detail and a passion for perfection, he’s earned a reputation for his exceptional work in post-processing, from spray painting to meticulous finishing touches that elevate any project.

extendedProps.description: 3D printer users often ask about 3D scanners - particularly which cheap 3D scanner is the best. The answer is simple: none of them. If you're looking for a budget solution for great results (and you don't mind putting a bit of work into it), photogrammetry is your best option.

title: How to Use CAD to Simplify 3D Printing and Avoid Using Supports

speakers.title: Joseph Casha
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: This workshop will explore CAD techniques and design hacks to enhance the strength and printability of 3D prints. Participants will learn how to incorporate support-free features and apply structural reinforcements directly in their models—minimizing post-processing and material waste.

title: Adopting 3D Printing in a Game Dev Studio

speakers.title: Luna Wullaert
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: Larian Studios became a worldwide phenomenon after releasing the critically acclaimed RPG Baldur's Gate 3, and fans of the Divinity series fell even more in love with the Belgian team. But at Larian, it's not just about handsome bears and armor-clad CEOs. They love 3D printing, too, and they are here to talk about how 3D printers found their way into the studio.

title: G-code Hacking

speakers.title: Jon Shone
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: Everybody who has a 3D printer knows what a G-Code is - a set of instructions which the machine's electronics translate into controlled movements. But what if we told you that you can actually dig into the code, and modify it manually - with some interesting results?

title: How to Print Massive Objects on a Regular 3D Printer

speakers.title: David Schwarz
speakers.description: David Schwarz is known in the 3D printing world as "The Prusa Guy". A true master of his craft, David is more than just a highly skilled 3D designer—he’s an artist, an innovator, and a magician when it comes to taking raw prints and transforming them into stunning, lifelike creations. With an eye for detail and a passion for perfection, he’s earned a reputation for his exceptional work in post-processing, from spray painting to meticulous finishing touches that elevate any project.

extendedProps.description: You don't need a huge 3D printer to create huge objects. There are ways to use your standard desktop machine to produce larger-than-life statues and more. We'll dive deep into scaling and cutting models for printing, and we'll show you how to cut a 3D object so it can be glued together later.

title: The Prusacaster Experience

speakers.title: Mikolas Zuza
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: You wouldn't download a guitar! Well - turns out you would. And you actually can! Mikolas will talk about his journey towards the creation of his 3D-printed electric guitar. Does PLA sound better than PETG? Is wood obsolete now? Can we print an entire band? So many questions. Mikolas played thousands of songs on his "Prusacaster" guitar and got dozens of ovations. And now he's ready to give you a deep dive into the world of 3D-printed instruments.

title: Post-Processing of 3D Prints

speakers.title: David Schwarz
speakers.description: David Schwarz is known in the 3D printing world as "The Prusa Guy". A true master of his craft, David is more than just a highly skilled 3D designer—he’s an artist, an innovator, and a magician when it comes to taking raw prints and transforming them into stunning, lifelike creations. With an eye for detail and a passion for perfection, he’s earned a reputation for his exceptional work in post-processing, from spray painting to meticulous finishing touches that elevate any project.

extendedProps.description: There is so much more you can do with a 3D print - it doesn't have to be a single-colored object forever! In this hands-on workshop, we'll focus on post-processing. That means sanding, painting, and detailing, three steps that will take your 3D prints to the next level and make them look as good as store-bought merchandise. You'll learn a ton of new things (mostly patience) and take your first steps toward your new obsession.

title: Using 3D Printing to Solve Local Problems in Africa

speakers.title: Jiri Prusa
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: Across Africa, locally run 3D-printing initiatives are proving that complex problems can be solved on-site, in hours rather than weeks, and supporting local innovation ecosystems. Field hospitals now fabricate critical spare parts next to the operating table, community workshops scan and print low-cost prosthetic limbs, and social enterprises turn e-waste into housings for solar lamps that light off-grid homes. These case studies illustrate how distributed additive manufacturing bypasses fragile

title: Fab City Network Gathering

extendedProps.description: Come together with likeminded makers, urbanists, designers and community champions to share ideas, research and projects with the network and foundation.

title: Mobile FabLabs showcase

speakers.title: Berlin University Alliance (BUA) Mobile Lab
speakers.description: The BUA Mobile Lab is a trailer equipped with open-source digital tools like 3D printers, laser cutters, and CNC machines. It promotes open-source hardware and community building at Berlin universities by enabling rapid prototyping and collaborative creation. Serving as a hub for innovation, it supports sharing reproducible designs via platforms like GitHub, bridging digital fabrication with open-source development practices.

speakers.title: FabLab University Truck
speakers.description: The Mobile FabLab is a traveling workshop bringing cutting-edge technology to students aged 13-19. Operating mainly in the South Moravian Region, it has reached as far as Sarajevo. Through hands-on technology workshops, students explore 3D printing, laser cutting, milling, robotics, and plotting. Equipped with advanced digital tools, the FabLab fosters creativity, problem-solving, and innovation, making STEM education more accessible and engaging.

speakers.title: EDU.Lab
speakers.description: The EDU .lab is Škoda Auto’s cutting‑edge mobile laboratory — truck and trailer combo that brings hands‑on STEM and digital fabrication directly to schools and the public across Czechia and Slovakia. Targeting pupils in upper primary and secondary school, this mobile lab aims to spark curiosity in robotics, AI, VR/AR, 3D‑printing and more — with afternoon slots open to families and general visitors. By the end of 2025, EDU .lab will have visited all Czech regions and major Slovak cities.

speakers.title: Fab Mobile by Fab Lab Iasi
speakers.description: Fab Mobile aims to give access to digital production education for vulnerable communities of youngsters located in the rural areas of Iași region.

speakers.title: Clay Jet
speakers.description: Piaggio APE that is transformed into a mobile clay 3D print lab in cooperation with JetClay.

speakers.title: AWO Makerkutsche
speakers.description: In the structurally weak area around Lübbenau in the Spreewald, the team introduces young people to digital and manual technologies – from model making and screen printing to Arduino programming and 3D printing. The organization is firmly rooted in the local community and also maintains cross-border collaborations with Polish institutions. The "Makerkutsche" itself is a trailer that the team, together with students from the local technical school, has converted into a mobile lab.

extendedProps.summary: A one-of-a-kind showcase of mobile FabLabs from across Europe – ready to explore, inside and out.
extendedProps.description: Step inside the world of wheels and makers! Explore a unique lineup of mobile FabLabs from across Europe – from compact vans to full-size trailers, each one packed with digital fabrication tools. Meet the teams behind them, hear their stories, and get hands-on with the machines they carry. It’s a moving lab experience you don’t want to miss!

title: 3D-Printed Soft Robots

speakers.title: Falk Tauber
speakers.description: Falk leads an independent group for bioinspired soft robotics at the University of Freiburg. With over a decade of experience in biomimetic research he is an expert in the transfer of biological principle into technical applications.  He and his team created a variety of bioinspired and biomimetic devices using additive manufacturing. Including a fully 3D printed electronic free soft robotic walker inspired by a turtle. His goal is to make soft robotic more sustainable utilizing additive manufacturing and biological principles.

extendedProps.description: Dr. Falk Tauber, a leading innovator in soft robotics from the University of Freiburg, will explore the future of autonomous, bio-inspired machines. The presentation will focus on his pioneering work in developing entirely 3D-printed soft robots that operate without any traditional electronics.

title: Open Footwear - digital shoe making for FAB LABs

speakers.title: Juraj Suska
speakers.description: I’ve been designing footwear for over 25 years. Open Footwear combines my love for running, digital development, and sustainable fashion.

extendedProps.description: In this presentation we will discuss the background of the Open Footwear project, its connection to the Fab Lab community and our plans for the future.

title: 3D Printing for Business

speakers.title: Jiri Lorencik
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: A showcase of real-world 3D printing applications presented by companies that have benefited from using 3D printing in their business.

title: Advanced SLA printing workshop: lessons from PrusaLab Prague

speakers.title: Jiří Novák
speakers.description:
PrusaLab is a prototype workshop of Josef Prusa and Prusa Research.
We offer development and prototyping support for individuals, project teams and companies.
With the backing of a large company and a team of experts, we provide consulting, acceleration packages and access to a wide ecosystem of technologies.
We are a place where almost anything can be made, and our goal is to turn ideas into reality.

Jiří is the Product Manager for SLA products, focusing on the development and optimization of high-quality solutions for resin-based 3D printing.

extendedProps.description:
Workshop Highlights:

  • Directly experience the difference between SLA and FDM 3D printing by creating translucent, textured prints.
  • Understand the core mechanisms, capabilities, and distinct advantages of SLA and FDM technologies.
  • Leave with printed samples showcasing the unique surface finishes and details achievable with SLA method.

title: How to F*ck Around with Prusa XL

speakers.title: Jon Shone
speakers.description: Soon

extendedProps.description: Jón Schone properprinting.pro is an accomplished designer and engineer. Which sounds nice and proper. And perhaps a little boring. So let's try again: Jón Schone is the creative mind behind some seriously crazy ideas - from unique printer modifications to 3D-printed kayaks. And now, Jón has his sights set on the XL. And he plans to do... stuff with it.

title: Managing a Prusa Farm

speakers.title: Joel Telling
speakers.description: Joel Telling is a passionate 3D printing enthusiast and content creator, widely known as "The 3D Printing Nerd." With a love for technology and hands-on innovation, Joel shares his knowledge through engaging YouTube videos, tutorials, and reviews. He explores the world of 3D printing, offering insights on machines, materials, and projects for both beginners and experts. Through his approachable style and extensive expertise, Joel has built a community of fellow makers, inspiring countless individuals to dive into the world of 3D printing.

extendedProps.description: Joel recently set up a massive 3D printing farm using Prusa 3D printers. And he's ready to tell you everything about it - from picking the right printers for the job to remote print management.

title: The story of Slic3r (and why I love open source communities)

speakers.title: Alessandro Ranellucci
speakers.bio: Technology Executive, Founder Slic3r, Curator Maker Faire Rome
speakers.description: Alessandro Ranellucci is a technology and open source expert. He is currently the curator of Maker Faire Rome, founder of his (third) startup Bobuild, and host of the podcast Sparks, which shares stories of makers and innovation. Previously, he served as VP of Products at Arduino, where he coordinated product strategy for the maker community. Before that, he led the team of software policy experts within the Italian government introducing open source as a mandatory requirement for the public sector and was previously director of the Make In Italy Foundation, which coordinated Italian FabLabs. He also founded and led the open-source project Slic3r for ten years.

extendedProps.summary: Modern 3D printing originates from a community-driven distributed R&D effort, made possible by open source.

speakers.title: Josef Průša
speakers.bio: Founder and CEO of Prusa Research
speakers.description:
Josef Průša is the Founder and CEO of Prusa Research, a company that has become synonymous with cutting-edge 3D printing technology. His company produces the iconic Prusa i3 printers, celebrated for their quality, reliability, and accessibility. Průša is a key figure in the world of 3D printing and a pivotal advocate for bringing digital manufacturing tools to individuals and communities globally.

Technology from Prusa Research is found everywhere—from home workshops to educational institutions, research laboratories, and commercial manufacturing facilities. Josef Průša champions the power of open-source technologies and knowledge sharing, highlighting his commitment to innovation and his dedication to the global maker community.

His work has earned numerous prestigious accolades, including being featured in Forbes 30 Under 30. His printers have received countless awards in the tech industry, and 3D Printing Industry magazine has recognized him as one of the most influential figures in 3D printing.

In addition to leading Prusa Research, Josef actively contributes to the development of open-source software and hardware, organizes workshops, and promotes education in digital manufacturing. His vision bridges technology with sustainability, and his innovations continue to shape and inspire the global maker movement.

extendedProps.summary: Start of the day focused on 3D printing innovation and community – co-designed with our general partner.
extendedProps.description:
Welcome to Prusa Day – a specially co-designed segment of the FAB25 program by our general partner Prusa Research, celebrating Prague as the global capital of 3D printing.

Over the years, 3D printing has become incredibly accessible and easy to use, thanks to open-source movements like RepRap and also the massive contributions of Prusa Research, who helped bring high-quality, user-friendly 3D printers to makers and professionals around the world.

This dedicated block dives into the world of 3D printing and additive manufacturing, with a rich mix of inspiration, community stories, and forward-looking conversations. It includes:

  • A keynote by Josef Prusa himself
  • A showcase of cutting-edge 3D printing projects from across the FabLab and maker communities
  • Talks featuring Prusa team members and leading voices in 3D printing

Let’s print the future – together.

title: Bio Break
Workshops (Prusa Day)

extendedProps.summary: Hands-on sessions, talks, and masterclasses led by experts in 3D printing and everything around it.
extendedProps.description:
As part of the Prusa Day program, this workshop block offers a mix of hands-on sessions, theoretical deep dives, talks, and masterclasses – all centered around 3D printing.

Some sessions will be led by Prusa Research employees, sharing their deep expertise in additive manufacturing, prototyping, materials, post-processing, and more. Whether you're a beginner or an advanced maker, there’s something here to level up your skills and knowledge.

The workshops will take place across the FAB25 campus, with plenty of room to experiment, ask questions, and connect with fellow makers. Get ready to learn, make, and explore the possibilities of 3D printing with the people who helped shape it.
