title: FAB25 Maker Coin: Solder It. Blink It. Wear It.

extendedProps.summary: Come and finish your FAB25 Maker Coin by soldering everything together.
extendedProps.description:
Grab a soldering iron, the PCB from your welcome pack, and components from the table – and let's bring your FAB25 Badge to life in just about 20 minutes!

  • By choosing the resistor values, you get to control how fast your badge blinks. It’s not just about soldering – it’s about making it your own.
  • Never soldered before? No worries. Our friendly instructors are here to guide you step by step. Whether you're a first-timer or a seasoned maker, this is a fun and hands-on way to kick off your FAB25 experience.
  • Wear your badge proudly all week – especially during networking sessions. Attach it to your lanyard or use the included magnet for a clean, stylish look.

title: Prusa Party!

extendedProps.summary: Last official big night to celebrate, connect, and soak in the FAB25 energy – Prusa style!
extendedProps.description:
What would a FAB25 day be without a proper networking party? The Prusa Party is here to bring together the community one more time for an evening full of music, fun, and connection.

By now, you already know the FAB25 energy drill – good vibes, open conversations and unforgettable moments. This is the last official party on the schedule, so it’s also your chance to start saying goodbye in style.

Come raise a glass with the makers, teams, and friends who made this journey unforgettable – and let’s close it out the Prusa way!

title: Day Start + Prusa Announcements
Prusa Factory Tours

extendedProps.summary: Take a behind-the-scenes look at the Prusa factory. Must be booked via the Eventee app!
extendedProps.description:
Get an exclusive peek into the world of Prusa Research with a short but exciting tour of their Prague-based factory. You'll see where the iconic orange 3D printers come to life – from parts to packaging – and experience the energy of one of the most impactful 3D printing companies in the world.

You must book your spot in advance via the Eventee app. Due to safety reasons only a limited number of people are allowed to enter at one time. This is the only FAB2 session that must be booked in advance.

title: OPPx2: Bigger and Stronger through Iterative Improvements!

speakers.title: Morgan Chivers
speakers.description: Morgan Chivers is a lifelong learner who graduated San Jose State University with four simultaneously-conferred degrees: BA History, BA Global Studies, BFA Photography, & BFA Spatial Arts. Morgan then earned an MFA in Glass/Intermedia before joining the UTA FabLab to lead the evolution from small engineering makerspace to a huge space with thriving culture representative of UTA’s extraordinarily diverse campus! Morgan is now the Maker Literacies Librarian, focusing on embedded librarianship and research around experiential learning and the transferrable skills gained through iterative design.

extendedProps.description:
I led a group of FabLab Student Staff through an iterative design project to build on the phenomenal Open Press Project; our goals were to build the largest press we could using the 3D printers in our lab, and the most robust press possible that maintains the mass-accessibility vision of the Open Press Project with common makerspace tools & off-the-shelf components.
Now you can use the OPPx2 yourself to make a limited-edition Fab25 postcard!

title: Lunch

extendedProps.summary: Pick-up, refuel, refresh – it’s lunch time at FAB25!
extendedProps.description: Time to recharge! Head to the lunch pick-up points and grab your meal box based on your selected preference. Whether you’re in the mood for something fresh or hearty, we’ve got you covered. Don’t forget to top it off with a coffee, a free water refill, or a chilled drink to beat the summer heat. Grab some energy and get ready for the rest of your FAB25 day!

title: Ingenious Minds: Empowering young engineers on a shoestring budget

speakers.title: Deborah Ivie
speakers.description: Deb is the Utah STEM Lead for 4-H, the largest youth development organisation in the USA. She has a strong passion for making STEM interesting and accessible for all youth. Her approach involves meeting youth & adults wherever they are on their STEM journey. Often this involves helping people find the STEM connection in everyday activities. She has created innovative curriculum such as the Physics of Music, Kitchen Science, & Quilting Geometry.  Her programmes have been used & adapted across the US. She is excited to collaborate with makers around the world to empower young people.

extendedProps.description: Make way for a new generation of innovators! Often the best ideas start with simplicity. We will look at ways to adapt making for young hands and minds by building on their interests and natural curiosity. Join frontline STEM educators as we explore different maker challenges that teach kid-friendly engineering skills through creativity and imagination.

title: Caribbean Code: 3D-Printed Lingerie Weaving Jamaican Heritage into Innovative Fashion

speakers.title: Dr Kadian Gosler
speakers.description:
Dr. Kadian A. Gosler is redefining lingerie at the crossroads of design, technology, and
wearer experience. A pioneering design researcher, earning her PhD from University
of the Arts London, where she developed an experience-centred design process for Bra
Wearables, a subset of smart bras. She founded KDN Research & Design Atelier,
a future-focused lab advancing bra wearables, 3D-printed lingerie, and smart design.
Passionate about inclusivity and innovation, Dr. Gosler challenges traditional lingerie
discourse, ensuring intimate apparel is shaped by wearer needs and cutting-edge technology.

extendedProps.description: This exhibition showcases the first 3D-printed lingerie collection, crafted without traditional fabrics, blending island motifs like hibiscus and masquerade aesthetics with radical material innovation. Discover how supple TPU/TPE filaments mimic lacebark’s delicacy while textured prints recreate fabric drapes. Learn how cultural intuition—like the island rhythm—shaped designs for wearable comfort. Attendees will be inspired by culturally-rooted fashion, proving technology can honour tradition.

title: Quakecraft challenge - build a model house and see if it can withstand an earthquake!

speakers.title: Bridget McKendry
speakers.description: Bridget McKendry is a maker and educator who co-founded Fab Lab CHCH which ran from 2014-18 in Christchurch, New Zealand. She has contributed to a number of STEM and digital fabrication events and projects, most notably the Quakecraft programme and kits for high school engineering education that won the 2018 Chevron STEM award at Fab 14 in Toulouse. Bridget is currently an academic staff member in the Fashion Technology department at Te Pukenga / Ara institute of Canterbury, teaching and researching 3D printing for apparel and textiles.

extendedProps.description: In this workshop we will work in teams to build resilient house models with our 3D printed kits, and then test them on the Quakecraft open source Fab lab-designed earthquake simulation table against different programmed "earthquakes" to see which ones stay standing!

title: Gaussian Spatting - scan your project to get a 3D model from any camera.

speakers.title: Axel Just
speakers.description:
Inspired by well-documented projects that I wanted to replicate, I fell in love with FabLabs, which gave me the opportunity to do so.
Since the birth of our makerspace "ZAM" in Erlangen, I have visited over 60 FabLabs in Europe to listen to their stories and learn from their experiences. During my travels I discovered projects that were worth documenting, sharing and replicating.

extendedProps.description:
You learn the Work-flow proses to create the 3D Model out of pictures and how to publish them on-line.
Make project your pop out with its Documentation

title: Fablab Meets Art: PrusaLab & SIGNAL Festival Collaborations

speakers.title: Ondřej Kašpárek
speakers.description:
Ondřej Kašpárek is the Head of PrusaLab, one of the most advanced makerspaces in Europe, where creativity, technology, and community-driven innovation come together. In his role, he oversees the development and operations of a workshop that serves as a hub for experimentation and digital fabrication for makers, students, startups, and established companies alike.

Ondřej's work focuses on fostering innovation through shared production spaces where technologies like 3D printing, CNC milling, and laser cutting empower groundbreaking projects. His expertise spans design, prototyping, and implementing cutting-edge solutions that connect digital fabrication with practical applications.

His mission is to build bridges between technology, education, and industry while strengthening a maker community passionate about sustainable and open innovation. Under his leadership, PrusaLab has inspired countless projects, embodying the spirit of creativity and collaboration.

extendedProps.description: This session presents three unique art-tech collaborations between PrusaLab and the SIGNAL Festival Prague. Through artist presentations and a panel discussion, we’ll explore how a fablab can act as a technological partner in the creation of large-scale, concept-driven light installations.

title: Art & Design Expo

speakers.title: Robin Baumgarten
speakers.description:
Robin is an award-winning experimental hardware game developer and interactive installation artist based in Berlin. After researching Artificial Intelligence in Games and working on mobile games, he is now fully focused on creating playful interactive installations that straddle the divide of games and art, such as Line Wobbler and Wobble Garden. His most recent projects also involve a scientific aspect: Quantum Garden and its larger sequel, Quantum Jungle, both visualise quantum physics in a playful yet scientifically accurate manner.

Quantum Jungle

Type: Interactive installation
Photo Credits: Robin Baumgarten

An interactive installation that transforms the complexity of quantum physics into a sensory experience of sound and light. As visitors move through the space, their presence triggers shifts in rhythm, pattern, and color – reflecting the uncertainty and entanglement at the heart of quantum mechanics.

speakers.title: Kryštof Brůha
speakers.description:
Kryštof Brůha is an intermedia artist living and working in Prague, whose work explores specific environments – from the natural to the technological and virtual. He focuses on their influences and their overall transformation over time. He allows us to look at phenomena hidden from our perception, for example through macro and micro mapping of the environment, and through such scanning of space and time through artistic artefacts he offers the viewer a new view of reality. His media include installations, kinetic sculptures, generative videos and experimental films. In the development process, he uses scientific methods, specially designed algorithms, generative design and machine learning. In implementation, Brůha creates custom tools using Rapid Prototyping and CNC technology that are computer controlled. All these technological processes are then reflected in the aesthetics of his works.

Audire Fluctus

Type: Interactive light sculpture
Photo Credits: Tomáš Slavík & Dušan Vondra

An interactive sculpture that visualizes the invisible world of electromagnetic waves. Using a custom-developed medium called Spektraflux, the piece responds to ambient electromagnetic activity –⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ such as signals from electrical grids and communication devices –⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ by transforming them into dynamic light patterns.

speakers.title: Adam Cigler & Petr Vacek
speakers.description:
Petr Vacek and Adam Cigler are Czech architects who synergize a creative artistic approach with programming, robotics, digital fabrication and traditional craftsmanship. Their collaboration arose during their studies at the Faculty of Arts and Architecture in Liberec and, thanks to a common fondness for advanced technology, science, space or philosophy, crystallized into an aesthetically appealing freeform creative experiment.

Reflexion

Type: Kinetic sculpture
Photo Credits: Ray Baseley

A hypnotic kinetic sculpture made of dozens of independently moving mirrors. Each mirror is precisely controlled by servo motors and arranged in clusters, forming a responsive light object driven by a network of Raspberry Pi microcomputers. Made with 3D-printed components and CNC-cut panels, Reflexion blends digital control with analog beauty – reflecting not just light, but movement, complexity and the invisible choreography of data.

speakers.title: Fabricademy Collective
speakers.description:
Fabricademy is a global, distributed educational program that explores the intersection of textiles, digital fabrication, and biology. Over 6 months, participants dive into a hands-on journey that blends traditional crafts with cutting-edge technologies like 3D printing, electronics, wearable tech, and biofabrication. It's a space for experimentation, collaboration, and redefining the future of fashion and materials through open-source tools and sustainable innovation. Fabricademy is hosted by Fab Labs and innovation spaces worldwide, making it a truly international and community-driven experience.

Fabricademy Exhibition

Type: Exhibition of selected models
Photo Credits: Barbara Rakovska, Fabricademy

Discover the 11th annual showcase of innovation, sustainability, and digital craftsmanship by Fabricademy participants and local Czech designers. This exhibition highlights groundbreaking projects at the intersection of technology, textiles, and biology. It features a curated selection of forward-thinking designs that merge digital fabrication, electronics, and biofabrication with sustainable materials and techniques.

speakers.title:
Lukáš Dřevjaný,
Luboš Zbranek & others
speakers.description:
**Lukáš Dřevjaný** is a visual artist, lighting designer, and mentor of creative technologies. He combines light, space, and interactivity in live performances, light installations, large-scale projections, and theatre performances. He primarily works under his brand oxoo. Among other things, he mentors technology in the Art&Digital Lab in Hall 40 at the Holešovice Market and organizes technology workshops. **Luboš Zbranek** is a 3D graphic, VJ, video, and light designer. During his studies of Interactive Media Theory at Masaryk University, he has focused on creating interactive environments and deeper exploration of the human-machine, human-code relationship. By studying light design at JAMU, he expanded his scope to include light design, light art, and performance genres. He has collaborated visually on many techno and electronic music events, interactive installations, and theatre performances at home and abroad. **Other collaborators: David Minařík, Michal Mitro, Maurício Lobo**

Nexus

Type: Interactive light sculpture
Photo Credits: Viktor Schwing

The Nexus grows algorithmically in time and space. Like the rhizome, the mycelium, or human and non-human neural networks, the individual cells of this digital moss are constantly exchanging information. The body of the work is made up of 300 eight-legged acrylic cells, straddling all axes of space. The individual building elements are interconnected, functioning as a volumetric display.

extendedProps.summary: Explore how Czech and European artists are pushing the boundaries of art through digital making.
extendedProps.description:
Step into the Art & Design Expo – a curated collection of interactive installations, experimental objects, and inspiring works by Czech and European artists who blend creativity with cutting-edge fabrication tools.

– Meet the makers behind pieces like Quantum Jungle, Audire Fluctus, Reflexion, Nexus and the Fabricademy annual exhibition
– Discover how digital tools like CNCs, 3D printers, and laser cutters are used to create awe-inspiring works
– Take a walk through art that moves, reacts, glows – and definitely deserves a spot on your feed

Come take a look, snap some cool photos, and get inspired by what happens when art and digital fabrication collide.

title: Hackster IoT Workshop: A Hands-On Journey with Nordic Thingy91X DAY 2

speakers.title: Jinger Zeng
speakers.bio: Hackster.io, Head of Applied Innovation Lab
speakers.description: Jinger is a tech entrepreneur, currently leading Applied Innovation Lab initiatives at Hackster.io, an international online community for engineers and innovators. She is a global evangelist for open-source technology and open science, and an alumni of IaaC's Master in Distributed Design program.

speakers.title: Sachin Soni
speakers.description: I'm a passionate Maker and full-time content creator, exploring electronics and IoT to build innovative and practical projects. With over 147K+ subscribers and 550+ videos on my YouTube channel, I've dedicated myself to simplifying technology through engaging tutorials. Our channel proudly won the Best YouTube Channel in Technology & Automation (2023) award. My mission is simple: inspire creativity, build new things, and empower others to learn and innovate in the world of electronics and IoT.

speakers.title: Salman Faris
speakers.bio: Co-founder MakerGram and Customer Success at Screenly
speakers.description:
Salman Faris is a passionate maker, Fab Academy graduate, and dedicated community builder based in Kerala. He leads MakerGram, a vibrant maker community that fosters collaboration, learning, and innovation. Salman has previously served as a Fab Academy instructor at FabLab Kerala and ChaiHio Makerspace. 

He currently works full-time at Screenly as a Customer Success professional and part of the global maker ecosystem as a member of the Hackster Pro, Seeed Studio Ranger team and the Edge Impulse Experts team, with a strong focus on TinyML & IoT.

extendedProps.description: Join Hackster’s IoT workshop to get a hands-on IoT prototyping experience with the Nordic Thingy:91 X. This device supports LTE-M, NB-IoT, GNSS and NR+, and certified for global operation. It is an ideal device for rapidly developing any cellular IoT concept, and is especially suited for asset tracking applications. This board is packed with real-world capabilities like sensors, GNSS, BLE, and ultra-low power performance, making it a perfect playground for both beginners and advanced makers. The workshop is a 2 part series, where we will introduce the hardware in Brno workshop on July 7, then participants will have the full week to play and experiment with it especially during travel, then have a final workshop in Prague on July 11.

title: Mobile FabLabs showcase

speakers.title: Berlin University Alliance (BUA) Mobile Lab
speakers.description: The BUA Mobile Lab is a trailer equipped with open-source digital tools like 3D printers, laser cutters, and CNC machines. It promotes open-source hardware and community building at Berlin universities by enabling rapid prototyping and collaborative creation. Serving as a hub for innovation, it supports sharing reproducible designs via platforms like GitHub, bridging digital fabrication with open-source development practices.

speakers.title: FabLab University Truck
speakers.description: The Mobile FabLab is a traveling workshop bringing cutting-edge technology to students aged 13-19. Operating mainly in the South Moravian Region, it has reached as far as Sarajevo. Through hands-on technology workshops, students explore 3D printing, laser cutting, milling, robotics, and plotting. Equipped with advanced digital tools, the FabLab fosters creativity, problem-solving, and innovation, making STEM education more accessible and engaging.

speakers.title: EDU.Lab
speakers.description: The EDU .lab is Škoda Auto’s cutting‑edge mobile laboratory — truck and trailer combo that brings hands‑on STEM and digital fabrication directly to schools and the public across Czechia and Slovakia. Targeting pupils in upper primary and secondary school, this mobile lab aims to spark curiosity in robotics, AI, VR/AR, 3D‑printing and more — with afternoon slots open to families and general visitors. By the end of 2025, EDU .lab will have visited all Czech regions and major Slovak cities.

speakers.title: Fab Mobile by Fab Lab Iasi
speakers.description: Fab Mobile aims to give access to digital production education for vulnerable communities of youngsters located in the rural areas of Iași region.

speakers.title: Clay Jet
speakers.description: Piaggio APE that is transformed into a mobile clay 3D print lab in cooperation with JetClay.

speakers.title: AWO Makerkutsche
speakers.description: In the structurally weak area around Lübbenau in the Spreewald, the team introduces young people to digital and manual technologies – from model making and screen printing to Arduino programming and 3D printing. The organization is firmly rooted in the local community and also maintains cross-border collaborations with Polish institutions. The "Makerkutsche" itself is a trailer that the team, together with students from the local technical school, has converted into a mobile lab.

extendedProps.summary: A one-of-a-kind showcase of mobile FabLabs from across Europe – ready to explore, inside and out.
extendedProps.description: Step inside the world of wheels and makers! Explore a unique lineup of mobile FabLabs from across Europe – from compact vans to full-size trailers, each one packed with digital fabrication tools. Meet the teams behind them, hear their stories, and get hands-on with the machines they carry. It’s a moving lab experience you don’t want to miss!

title: Rooted Projects: Designing Culturally Relevant & Meaningful Fabrication Projects for Youth

speakers.title: Luella Provenza
speakers.description: Luella Provenza brings 17 years of experience in the nonprofit sector, specializing in organizational strategy, program design and implementation, and fund development. Her diverse background includes impactful work in STEM education, sports-based youth development, educator development, and food access. Luella launched ProFab Education to help bridge the gap between education and careers. Co-founders Sam & Luella are dedicated to integrating advanced manufacturing into K-12 learning experience through collaborative partnerships with formal and informal educators.

speakers.title: Liz Whitewolf
speakers.description: Liz Whitewolf is the founder of eduFAB, a global organization advancing equity in STEM through digital fabrication. She leads the annual International Fab Educators Summit and supports global STEM educators. A former classroom teacher, she co-founded the Fab Learning Academy and serves as a Global Instructor and executive board member. Liz earned her Ed.D. in STEM Education from the University of Pittsburgh, focusing on K–12 makerspace integration, and is a 2016 FAB Academy graduate.

extendedProps.description: Join the “Rooted Projects” workshop to generate your next culturally relevant hands-on project for youth in your community. Attendees will spur their creativity through a Mardi Gras mask-making activity and exploring other Southeast Louisiana-inspired projects. By the end of the session, attendees will complete a draft project design through a guided design thinking activity. The session will conclude with a facilitated peer review to further refine project designs.

title: Fusion of Art and Tech: Clay Sculptural Table with Water Fountain & LED Illusion

speakers.title: Matthew Koomson
speakers.description: Matthew Koomson is a Workshop Coordinator and Project Manager at Ghana FabLab, specializing in digital fabrication, STEM education, and sustainable innovation. He has expertise in electronics, mechatronics, robotics, IoT, CNC, and 3D printing. Passionate about hands-on learning, he empowers youth and makers through technology, creativity, and problem-solving, driving innovation and community development.

extendedProps.description: Discover the fusion of traditional clay sculpting and modern technology in this innovative center table design. Featuring a built-in water fountain and LED illusions, this project showcases craftsmanship, digital fabrication, and interactive design. Attendees will learn about material selection, fabrication techniques, and integrating lighting and water flow to create functional art. Join us to explore the future of artistic, tech-enhanced furniture.

title: Turning Filament Waste into Arabic Art

speakers.title: Noha Hani
speakers.description: Nerdy maker, craft lover & crazy cat lady! Playing around in Fab Labs for 10 years—combining my love for DIY with digital fabrication and making sure to spread it to everyone through managing Fab Lab Egypt’s makerspace.

extendedProps.description: Have you ever wanted to create cross-stitch-inspired art but found it too complex or slow? This 20-minute workshop offers a fun solution while repurposing 3D printing filament offcuts into Arabic-inspired keychains and earrings. Experiment with vibrant colors, patterns, and shapes while learning simple crafting and waste-reuse techniques. Open to all skill levels, this session blends cultural creativity with sustainability for a unique and impactful experience.

title: Vacuum Cleaner Simulator

speakers.title: Jeffrey Roe
speakers.description: Jeffrey Roe is a software and hardware engineer from Ireland, designing public transport systems at Telclic. He is an active member of Tog Hackerspace, co-runs Dublin Maker, and lectures in design innovation at Maynooth University. With expertise in IoT, electronics, and sustainable transport, Jeffrey enjoys sharing hands-on skills through workshops on Arduino, repair, and citizen science. He is also an amateur radio enthusiast and part of the global maker movement.

extendedProps.description: Experience hands-on control of a robot vacuum cleaner in this interactive exhibit. Learn how IoT, electronics, and coding come together to create smart systems. This showcase will demonstrate real-time control, automation, and sensor integration in a fun and engaging way.

title: Fab Festival (More info here: https://fabfestival.cz/)

extendedProps.summary: The big public celebration of making – hands-on, family-friendly, and open to everyone!
extendedProps.description:
Join us for the Fab Festival – a full-day, free and open-to-public event that brings the magic of digital fabrication to the Czech general public.

Main website available here: https://fabfestival.cz/

This is your chance to meet local makers, introduce newcomers to the FabLab network, and help demystify digital fabrication through simple hands-on workshops, machine demos, tours, talks, project showcases, and even live music!

FAB25 may be nearing its end, but the energy is still going strong. The Fab Festival is the perfect opportunity to share what we do, what we love, and why making matters – with curious minds of all ages. Let’s open the doors wide, celebrate creativity, and inspire the next generation of makers right here in Prague.

title: FAB25 Maker Coin: Solder It. Blink It. Wear It.

extendedProps.summary: Come and finish your FAB25 Maker Coin by soldering everything together.
extendedProps.description:
Grab a soldering iron, the PCB from your welcome pack, and components from the table – and let's bring your FAB25 Maker Coin to life in just about 20 minutes!

  • By choosing the resistor values, you get to control how fast your badge blinks. It’s not just about soldering – it’s about making it your own.
  • Never soldered before? No worries. Our friendly instructors are here to guide you step by step. Whether you're a first-timer or a seasoned maker, this is a fun and hands-on way to kick off your FAB25 experience.
  • Wear your coin proudly all week – especially during networking sessions. Attach it to your plastic pouch or use the included magnet for a clean, stylish look.

title: FabLab University Truck

speakers.title: Martina Michlovska
speakers.description: I'm Martina, a passionate designer and maker at FabLab Brno, where I lead student education and help young innovators bring their ideas to life. With my team, I run the FabLab University truck, bringing hands-on tech experiences on the road. I also support high school students through competitions that spark creativity and skill-building. As a former design student and maker at heart, I love experimenting—though I do have a knack for breaking milling cutters along the way! 😄🚀

extendedProps.description: The Mobile FabLab is a traveling workshop bringing cutting-edge technology to students aged 13-19. Operating mainly in the South Moravian Region, it has reached as far as Sarajevo. Through hands-on technology workshops, students explore 3D printing, laser cutting, milling, robotics, and plotting. Equipped with advanced digital tools, the FabLab fosters creativity, problem-solving, and innovation, making STEM education more accessible and engaging.

title: Clay Jet

speakers.title: David Pap
speakers.description: I am a digital fabrication expert and the chief executive officer of FabLab Budapest. Previously, I described myself as someone who believes in bottom-up innovation and helps others (mostly my university students and young entrepreneurs) to shape their ideas into prototypes. I took my time to learn what a FabLab can offer: the chance to learn to create "almost" anything.  I would introduce myself as a craftsman still exploring materials and their manufacturing technologies. I also speak about traditional crafts and their heritage. I am also interested in the philosophical side of design.

extendedProps.description: This is a Piaggio APE. Yes, I know—cute and cool! In cooperation with JetClay, we are transforming it into a mobile clay 3D print lab.

title: Fab Lab Mobile

speakers.title: Dan Zaharia
speakers.description: Dan Zaharia is the co-founder of Fab Lab Iași, the largest network of coworking spaces in Western Moldova (Romania), fostering a dynamic tech and entrepreneurial community. With over 20 years of experience in real estate development, leasing, and business consulting, he actively supports the growth of regional innovation hubs. Through his company, Extind, he has played a key role in positioning Iași as a competitive destination for IT & Outsourcing. He is also the publisher of PINmagazine, a regional tech industry publication, and a frequent speaker at business and academic events.

extendedProps.description: Fab Mobile aims to give access to digital production education for vulnerable communities of youngsters located in the rural areas near the city Iași in Western Moldova region of Romania.

title: Berlin University Alliance (BUA) Mobile Lab

speakers.title: Sebastian Krüger
speakers.description: I studied automotive engineering at HAW Hamburg until September 2021 focusing on calculation & simulation and final thesis work at Jungheinrich and Daimler on multi-body simulations and finite element methods. My career start at the Department of Automotive Engineering at TU Berlin in March 2022 included teaching and research on crash simulations. Since April 2025 I have been a research fellow at the Department of Quality Science at the Institute for Machine Tools and Factory Management at TU Berlin and research quality aspects of decentralized open-source hardware in the Fab City project.

It promotes open-source hardware and community building at Berlin universities by enabling rapid prototyping and collaborative creation. Serving as a hub for innovation, it supports sharing reproducible designs via platforms like GitHub, bridging digital fabrication with open-source development practices.

Makerspaces: Makerkutsche, Makerspace Senftenberg, fablabCB, Technikschule cleverinside, Colab, Machbar Potsdam
